Name: Copeinca
Description:
Somos la empresa pesquera líder en el Perú, dedicados a la extracción, procesamiento y producción de harina y aceite de pescado.

Estamos comprometidos con el desarrollo sostenible asegurando la pesca responsable del recurso y realizando proyectos de responsabilidad social y ambiental, contribuyendo con el crecimiento del país y generando valor a todos nuestros grupos de interés. 

Nuestros productos gozan de la mejor calidad utilizando como materia prima a la anchoveta (Engraulis ringens), fuente rica en proteína y ácidos grasos poliinsaturados omega 3 como EPA y DHA.

Name: Industrial Pesquera Santa Priscila
Description: Desde 1976 Industrial Pesquera Santa Priscila S.A. produce y exporta camaron ecuatoriano (Litopenaeus Vannamei)  Hemos sido pioneros y formamos parte del continuo desarrollo de la acuaultura ecuatoriana. Somos mas de 6000 personas que formamos parte de la familia IPSP y tenemos como objetivo lograr las mejores condiciones sociales, ambientales y economicas dentro de la industria. Durante 38 años, nuestro trabajo nos ha permitio una integracion total, desde programas geneticos, laboratorios, piscinas, alimento balanceado, plantas empacadoras y exportacion.

Name: GRUPO CORPORATIVO FAJARDO
Description: En Grupo Corporativo Fajardo transformamos el cultivo de camarón mediante procesos innovadores y sostenibles con excelencia operacional, desarrollando talento y marcando la diferencia en cada etapa de nuestra industria.

Name: Cofimar S.A.
Description: Our group owns more than 2,500 hectares of shrimp farms that have been certified by the National Fisheries Institute (Instituto Nacional de Pesca). We also have laboratories from which we source larvae for planting, which are managed under organic standards. This guarantees the quality and reliability of our shrimp from the very beginning, right up until it reaches the final consumer. At COFIMAR, we are committed to an international expansion program for our production.

Name: Camanchaca SA
Description:
Camanchaca inició operaciones en Chile el año 1965, con una actividad centrada en la pesca y procesamiento de camarones y langostinos. En 1980, un cambio de dirección en la empresa trajo como resultado una estrategia de diversificación hacia otros productos del mar, incluyendo la acuicultura, lo que impulsó un crecimiento uniforme y constante. En la actualidad, Camanchaca participa en los negocios de pesca extractiva en el norte y sur de Chile, en la acuicultura del Salmón Atlántico y en el cultivo de Mejillones y Abalones. Sus operaciones se desarrollan desde el norte de Chile, en Iquique hasta el sur del país, a lo largo de 6,640 kilómetros de costa. Este crecimiento la ha llevado a un lugar de privilegio en los mercados internacionales, exportando a más de 50 países.

En el área de pesca, Camanchaca ha ido orientando su producción cada vez más hacia el consumo humano, incluyendo el jurel congelado y en conservas, langostinos y aceites ricos en Omega 3, además de la producción de harina de pescado.

En el área de salmones, la Compañía se convirtió en la primera empresa productora de salmón en todo el mundo en obtener cuatro estrellas para la certificación Best Aquaculture Practices (BAP), provisto por la Global Aquaculture Alliance (GAA). Esta norma es muy valorada por consumidores, retailers y operadores de Food Service en todo el mundo, y garantiza que la producción de salmón se realiza de forma ambiental y socialmente responsable, y que es un producto seguro para el consumo humano.

Además, destaca que Camanchaca es miembro fundador del Global Salmon Initiative (GSI), alianza que representa aproximadamente el 50% de la producción de salmón de cultivo en el mundo y que reconoce el rol de la salmonicultura para satisfacer en forma sustentable la creciente demanda por proteínas sanas del mundo en las siguientes décadas, haciendo progresos significativos a nivel de la industria.

Con el fin de establecer una presencia cercana en los mercados estratégicos en los que se comercializan sus productos, la Compañía ha establecido oficinas o representaciones comerciales en Estados Unidos, Japón, Europa y más recientemente en Centroamérica/Caribe y en China/Sudeste Asiático; en este último mercado desarrollando la marca New World Currents junto a otros productores chilenos; además de sus oficinas corporativas en Santiago de Chile.
Todos en Camanchaca están comprometidos con su misión de “alimentar el mundo desde el mar”, entregando pescados y mariscos de primera calidad.

Name: Grupo Diosmar
Description:
Grupo Diosmar is an Ecuadorian company with more than 20 years of experience in the aquaculture industry, dedicated to the production and export of premium shrimp.

With over 3,300 hectares of sustainable farming, we ensure full traceability, quality, and excellence across every stage of our process.

Exporting from Ecuador to the world, we proudly deliver shrimp recognized for its superior flavor, freshness, and responsibility toward the environment.

Quality, trust, and sustainability — that’s the Diosmar seal.

Name: Pesquera Exalmar SAA
Description: Pesquera Exalmar S.A.A. es una empresa líder en el sector pesquero. Con 33 años de trayectoria, participa en la captura, procesamiento y comercialización de productos marinos de calidad y alto valor proteico. Actualmente, cuenta con un 18% de participación en la industria, lo que la posiciona como la tercera empresa en procesamiento de harina y aceite de pescado, en un país que se ha consolidado como el primer productor y exportador de dichos productos a nivel mundial. Su estrategia de sostenibilidad se basa en una pesca y procesamiento responsable y sostenible, para la creación de valor económico, social y ambiental a lo largo de la cadena de valor y comunidades de su entorno.

Name: PT. Central Proteina Prima, Tbk
Description:
We are the dominant integrated aquaculture industry and foods producer company and we continuously set the global standards for superior market-driven products with our innovation and management.

PT Central Proteina Prima Tbk (CP Prima) is a leading aquaculture company in Indonesia which was incorporated in April 1980. CP Prima produces and sells feed, fry, pet food, probiotics, and shrimp products  and processed food products for domestic and export markets. Not only in Indonesia, we have also expanded our wings in supplying innovative feeds to India market, and shrimp products to international markets like Vietnam, China, Japan, USA, Canada, United Kingdom, Belgium, France, Netherland, Germany and New Zealand, tuning each needs based on each country’s regulations and standards respectively. Our shrimps feed has recently landed in India in 2014, gaining pleasant responses from the market within a short period of time.

Become a fan on Facebook.com/CPPrima 
Follow us on Twitter.com/CPPrima

Name: Fisheries Development Oman
Description: الحساب الرسمي للشركة تنمية أسماك عمان   The Official Account of Fisheries Development Oman (FDO)

Name: AquaBioTech Group
Description:
AquaBioTech Group is a global consulting and engineering company with over 25 years of experience in aquaculture, fisheries and other aquatic sciences. 

Detailed information about each entity within the group can be obtained by clicking directly on the name of the entity concerned. The main contact points are also listed below.

AquaBioTech Group is an independent aquaculture, fisheries and marine environmental consulting, testing, research and development company operating on a global scale with clients and projects in over fifty-five countries.  Each division of the company is focused on a specialised field of work related to the aquatic environment.  
 

Primarily involved in Consulting, Advisory and Training (CAT) for aquaculture, fisheries and other marine / oceanographic projects; Testing, Research, Inspection and Certification (TRIC) work in our aquatic research facilities; and the development of land-based fish and shrimp farms and aquatic research facilities using our proprietary RAS technology in our Design Engineering Technology Services (DETS) Division, we seek to offer a complete and holistic service to our clients.  

Staff working at AquaBioTech Group are all considered specialists in their relevant area of work and the company has sought to create a multi-national team of more than 115 in-house experts from more than thirty-five countries that have a variety of backgrounds and experiences bringing a wealth of knowledge and experience to any assignment undertaken.  

AquaBioTech Group is an export focused business that has consistently derived more than 98% of its business from outside of Malta and continues to seek to internationalise its business in all three core sectors of the business.

Name: Sustainable Fisheries Partnership
Description:
Sustainable Fisheries Partnership's (SFP) mission is to maintain healthy ocean and aquatic ecosystems, enhance fishing and fish-farming livelihoods and secure food supplies.

SFP is a young and dynamic, business-focused NGO that is reshaping the world of corporate responsibility through the creation of powerful information tools and a methodology that allows companies to directly engage with suppliers of natural resources. It does not campaign or provide eco-labels but works to reduce the barriers to action by industry in creating a more sustainable world. SFP operates through two main principles: information and improvement.

www.bycatchsolutions.org - an industry-led hub to reduce bycatch in fisheries
www.seafoodmetrics.com - sustainability intelligence for the seafood industry

Name: DELOS
Description:
We are a science-driven aqua-tech company seeking to disrupt the traditional and fragmented aquaculture industry in Indonesia, with eyes on South Asia as a whole.

Our vision is the disruption and metamorphosis of the aquaculture industry through the Blue Revolution. We believe Indonesian aquaculture has the potential to second to none. 

Our mission is to equip aquaculturists with the technology, and management capabilities in order to sustainably and profitably harvest, pushing them to drive the Blue Revolution.

Name: Trident Seafoods
Description: At Trident Seafoods, we've pioneered responsible fishing in the pristine waters of Alaska for more than 51 years. As a proud champion of bringing the freshest, healthiest, wild-caught fish from Alaska to families worldwide,  we have a history of innovation and a commitment to doing business in ways that are good for the planet and good for people.

Name: Mowi Chile
Description: Nuestra visión es liderar la revolución azul: Más del 70% del planeta está cubierto por agua, y aun así solo un 2% del suministro mundial de alimento viene del mar. Al mismo tiempo, la población mundial está aumentando, y proveer a todos de alimentos saludables y sostenibles se ha convertido en un gran reto. El potencial más grande para la producción sostenible de alimentos proviene de los océanos del mundo. Mowi es la Empresa más grande y lider a nivel mundial en Salmón Atlántico. En Chile estamos ubicados en la X y XI región.

Name: Aquaculture Focus
Description: Aquaculture Focus is a new concept in communications, marketing & information services for the fish farming industry.

Name: American Fisheries Society
Description: The American Fisheries Society (AFS), founded in 1870, is the oldest and largest professional society representing fisheries scientists. The AFS promotes scientific research and enlightened management of  aquatic resources for optimum use and enjoyment by the public. It also encourages comprehensive education of fisheries scientists and continuing on-the-job training.

Name: Prilabsa
Description:
Prilabsa es una empresa multinacional fundada en el año 1992, dedicándose a la comercialización de alimentos, probióticos, aditivos, equipos y químicos con altos estándares de calidad.

Prilabsa ha podido cubrir todas las necesidades de los laboratorios de camarón, peces y  camaroneras,  gracias al pleno conocimiento del medio ambiente y la sólida experiencia con nuestro personal capacitado en varios mercados  de la industria acuícola.

Prilabsa ha expandido sus actividades en países establecidos como puntos estratégicos del continente americano, contando con oficinas comerciales  y bodegas climatizadas en USA (Miami), Brasil (Natal y Aracati), Ecuador (Guayaquil, Manta, Pedernales, San Vicente, Ponce Enríquez, Hualtaco, Machala, Esmeraldas, Libertad), Honduras, Nicaragua, Perú y Panamá.

Contamos con mas de 31 años de experiencia y servicio dentro del sector, lo que evidencia que la excelencia no se improvisa, se consolida a través de la  eficiencia de cada uno de nuestro equipo de trabajo. Llegando así a convertirnos en la solución integral del sector acuícola en las Américas.

Name: Aquaconnect
Description:
Aquaconnect is an integrated seafood company that delivers tech-driven solutions across global production and consumption value chains, ensuring transparency, efficiency, and predictability. The company provides stakeholders in the production value chain with access to innovative farm care solutions and post-harvest market linkages and enables consistent, high-quality seafood sourcing assistance to buyers worldwide. Aquaconnect is backed by marquee investors such as S2G, LDC Ventures, Lok Capital, AgFunder, Hatch, Omnivore, Flourish Ventures, and Rebright Partners.

Our efforts have been appreciated and recognised by several esteemed organisations including the Paris Peace Forum, Google, GIIF- World Bank, TGS 100, NASSCOM, FICCI, Forbes, and Fast Company etc.,

Name: Oceana Group Limited
Description: The Oceana Group is Africa’s largest fishing company and one of the most empowered food companies on the JSE. We operate in South Africa, Namibia and the United States of America and market fish-based products in 46 countries, while managing a fleet of 54 vessels and eight cold storage facilities.  We are the home of Lucky Star, Oceana’s iconic canned fish product. Our group is a purpose-led business contributing positively to addressing societies imbalances while continuing to harvest responsibly and delivering sustained economic value to all our stakeholders.

Name: Innovasea
Description:
From land to open ocean, Innovasea provides aquatic solutions that hold up in the most challenging conditions. And stand up for life. 

This requires more than just delivering the world’s most advanced aquatic technologies. It means continuously applying knowledge in science and engineering, fish tracking and farm operations to develop the ideal systems for each site. It means working shoulder-to-shoulder with customers to cultivate and protect fish populations. And it means consciously designing products and services to give back more to nature than we take. Day in and day out, we are driven by a commitment to make our ocean and freshwater ecosystems sustainable for future generations.

A pioneer in developing end-to-end solutions for aquatic ecosystems, at Innovasea we’re intent on leading and feeding innovation. Today. Tomorrow. For life.

Name: Pacific Islands Forum Fisheries Agency (FFA)
Description:
The Pacific Islands Forum Fisheries Agency (FFA) strengthens national capacity and regional solidarity so its 17 members can manage, control and develop their tuna fisheries now and in the future.

Based in Honiara, Solomon Islands, FFA's 17 Pacific Island members are Australia, Cook Islands, Federated States of Micronesia, Fiji, Kiribati, Marshall Islands, Nauru, New Zealand, Niue, Palau, Papua New Guinea, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tokelau, Tonga, Tuvalu and Vanuatu.

FFA was established to help countries sustainably manage their fishery resources that fall within their 200 mile Exclusive Economic Zones (EEZs). FFA is an advisory body providing expertise, technical assistance and other support to its members who make sovereign decisions about their tuna resources and participate in regional decision making on tuna management through agencies such as the Western and Central Pacific Fisheries Commission (WCPFC).

Since 1979, FFA has facilitated regional cooperation so that all Pacific countries benefit from the sustainable use of tuna – worth over $3 billion a year and important for many people’s livelihoods in the Pacific.

Vision Statement

The joint aim of members of the Forum Fisheries Agency is captured in its Vision Statement, which states:

“Our people will enjoy the highest levels of social and economic benefits through the sustainable use of our offshore fisheries resources.”



FFA’s Corporate Mission

For staff and management at FFA’s regional headquarters, their work is guided by the Corporate Mission Statement, which states the mission of the organization is:

“To drive regional cooperation to create and enable the maximum long term social and economic benefit from the sustainable use of our shared offshore fishery resources.”

Name: Scottish Sea Farms
Description: Rearing superior quality salmon in the most responsible, sustainable way for supply to major retailers, exclusive restaurateurs and kitchen tables alike.

Name: Growel Feeds Private Limited
Description:
Growel Feeds Private Limited (An ISO 9001:2015 certified company) is the one of the leading Aqua Feed manufacturers in India. They specialize in Shrimp feed and Fish feed.

The name 'Growel' was coined to help the Aqua farming community to grow well. We do not compromise on quality and we ensure that products are delivered on time.

Growel Feeds is promoted by a field of experienced and leading entrepreneurs and also by the promoters of M/S Growel Formulations (P) Ltd, Hyderabad which has been an exceptional aquaculture healthcare products manufacturer since 1994.

Growel Formulations (P) Ltd was the first Indian company set-up to provide Aquaculture Healthcare formulations and has been proving its merit for more than 2 decades in India and overseas. Being promoted by such a pioneering company as Growel Formulations is the pillar of strength for Growel Feeds.

The first unit of Growel Feeds includes latest technology in the manufacturing process and has an installation capacity of 1, 20,000 tons per annum. This unit began its commercial production in April 2010 with automation of the entire processes from batching to bagging.

Growel Feeds opened its second unit recently with a capacity of 1, 80,000 tons of feed every year. This unit is well equipped to produce shrimp feeds and other aqua feeds of high value.

 'Growel Feeds Pvt Ltd' is committed to sound business practices to ensure world-class product quality. We have been consistent in fetching top-notch results, for the aqua-farmers.

 'Growel Feeds Pvt Ltd' has witnessed that the continuous research and development has resulted in high quality products ever since it was started.

Name: Seafood Industry Australia
Description:
Seafood Industry Australia (SIA) is the national peak body which represents the Australian seafood industry as a whole, including members from wild catch, aquaculture and post-harvest sectors.

As the voice of Australian seafood, SIA provides consumers, Government and other stakeholders with confident and united representation. Our unity indicates that we all love what we do, we stand by our products and that those products are the best in the world.

SIA provides services identified through a process involving member input to fill a critical gap that currently exists, to have more influence on Government decisions, to act as a national industry voice, to be a marketing and communications hub, and to remove obstacles to growth standing in the way of the Australian seafood industry.

Our vision is for the Australian seafood industry to be United, Effective and Respected. Our mission is to Promote, Protect and Develop the Australian seafood industry on the national and international level.

Name: Seafish
Description:
Seafish is the public body supporting the £10bn UK seafood industry. Our vision is for an industry that is truly thriving. 

We sit at the heart of the seafood industry, bringing all sectors together to support them with the issues that matter most. We offer a central pool of expertise that can support our stakeholders and drive improvement across all areas of the industry.

Our area of work spans everything from catch to plate and our 80-plus professional staff work alongside their industry colleagues on big challenges and opportunities such as increasing consumption of seafood to improving responsible sourcing.

We are funded through a statutory levy made on the first sale of sea fish, shellfish, sea fish products and from landings destined for subsequent sale outside the UK.

Name: Tropical Packing Ecuador S. A. -TROPACK-
Description: Cuenta oficial de Tropical Packing Ecuador S. A. TROPACK

Name: Sea Harvest Group Ltd
Description: Sea Harvest is a South African deep sea trawling company established in 1964 on the Atlantic West Coast of South Africa. As a vertically integrated company, Sea Harvest owns all its fishing vessels and processing facilities. From its head office in Cape Town, the company markets and distributes its wide range of products to diverse markets around the world.

Name: Caleta Bay
Description: Trabajamos día a día para ganar el reconocimiento de nuestros clientes, el cariño de nuestros trabajadores y el respeto de nuestras comunidades. Produciendo un alimento exquisito, que te entrega todo lo que necesitas para estar bien.

Name: Salmon Evolution
Description:
Salmon Evolution is a Norwegian land-based salmon farming company focused on extending the ocean's potential by transferring the best preconditions offered by the sea to farm fish on land through its chosen technology, hybrid flow-through system technology (HFS). This secures a truly sustainable production process with controlled and optimal growth conditions and limiting operational and biological risk.

The Company's first production facility is under construction at Indre Harøy, strategically located on the Norwegian west coast with unlimited access to fresh seawater, renewable energy, established infrastructure for salmon farming, and an educated and experienced work force. The entire project is designed for an annual capacity of approx. 31,500 tonnes HOG.

The Company has also entered into a joint venture with Dongwon Industries where the parties will develop, construct and operate a land-based salmon farming facility in South Korea with an annual production capacity of 16,800 tonnes HOG, using Salmon Evolution's chosen HFS technology.

Salmon Evolution ASA is listed on Oslo Børs under the ticker SALME.

To learn more, please visit www.salmonevolution.no

Name: Oman Fisheries CO. SAOG
Description: Oman's largest fishing Company and total workforce of 350 employees.

Name: Kılıç Holding
Description:
Kılıç olarak , sağlıklı beslenmenin dünyada herkesin hakkı olduğuna inanıyor ve sağlıklı bir hayat sürmek için gerekli olan proteine ulaşmanın da ne kadar önemli olduğunu biliyoruz. Kurulduğumuz 1993 yılından beri de bunu sağlamak için var gücümüzle çalışıyoruz.

Son derece özen gerektiren bu işte, balıklarımızla sağlıklı nesillerin yetişmesine katkıda bulunmak, en önemli misyonumuz. Bu amaçla sürekli yeni yatırımlar yapıyor, ülkemizde ve dünyada yaşayan her kesime hitap eden sağlıklı ve lezzetli balıklarımızı dünya sofralarına ulaştırıyoruz.

Çevreye verdiğimiz önemle son teknolojileri kullandığımız tam entegre tesislerimizle faaliyet gösteriyoruz. Güzel bir dünya için sürdürülebilir bir üretimin şart olduğuna inanıyoruz. Bunun bilinci ve sorumluluğuyla da üretimimizde karbon salımı azaltımına büyük önem veriyoruz. “Karbon Ayak İzi”nin minimize edilmesinde, yenilenebilir enerji kaynaklarına yönelimin maksimum seviyede olması gerektiğine inanıyoruz. Ülkemizdeki ve yurt dışındaki mevcut yatırımlarımızı  buna göre planlıyoruz.

Bodrum’dan başlayan ve çeyrek asıra yaklaşan Su Ürünleri Sektörü’ndeki yolculuğumuzda, aradan geçen zaman içerisinde dünyanın 5 kıtasındaki 50 ülkeye yaptığımız ihracat yaklaşık 300 milyon Dolar’a ulaştı. Bu performansımızla 12 yıldır üst üste Su Ürünleri ve Hayvansal Mamuller Sektörü’nün ihracat şampiyonu olduk.

Türkiye’ye kazandırdığımız döviz ve ödediğimiz vergilerle ekonomimize önemli katkılarda bulunmaktan gurur duyuyoruz. İhracat yaptığımız pazarlara yakın olarak lojistik avantajlarından yararlanmak adına yurt dışındaki yatırım fırsatlarını da değerlendiriyoruz.  Avrupa’nın öncü kuruluşları arasında sektörümüzde ülkemizin lideriyiz...

Name: PT Perikanan Indonesia
Description:
PT Perikanan Indonesia merupakan perseroan terbatas yang didirikan berdasarkan hukum Negara Republik Indonesia dan berkedudukan di Jakarta sebagaimana tercantum dalam Akta Pendirian Perusahaan Perseroan (Persero) PT Perikanan Indonesia atau disingkat PT Perikanan Indonesia No. 6, tanggal 5 Agustus 2021, dibuat di hadapan Dr. Isyana Wisnuwardhani Sadjarwo, SH, Notaris di Jakarta dan telah memperoleh pengesahan Menteri Kehakiman Republik Indonesia sesuai dengan Surat Keputusan Menkumham No. AHU-0048836.AH.01.01.TAHUN 2021, tanggal 5 Agustus 2021, sebagaimana telah didaftarkan dalam Daftar Perseroan No. AHU-0134186.AH.01.11.TAHUN 2021, tanggal 5 Agustus 2021. PT Perikanan Indonesia merupakan penggabungan dua Perusahaan BUMN yaitu PT Perikanan Nusantara dan PT Perikanan Indonesia melalui PP no. 99 th 2021 yang bergerak dalam bidang Perikanan. Setelah penggabungan tersebut di tahun 2021 PT Perikanan Indonesia bergabung kedalam Holding BUMN Pangan (ID FOOD) melalui PP No. 118 Tahun 2021.

PT Perikanan Indonesia memiliki 3 lini bisnis utama yakni Pengolahan&Perdagangan ikan, Bisnis Kepelabuhanan dan Pabrik Pakan Ikan. PT Perikanan Indonesia, member of ID FOOD, saat ini memiliki 12 kantor cabang dan 21 kantor unit di seluruh Indonesia.

Name: Aquasur
Description:
Aquasur es la exhibición y Congreso Internacional líder en acuicultura del hemisferio sur. Es el punto de encuentro por excelencia para empresas, instituciones públicas, centros de investigación, proveedores, expertos y profesionales del sector, quienes se reúnen para conocer las últimas innovaciones tecnológicas, soluciones sostenibles y tendencias que impulsan el desarrollo de la industria acuícola.

Organizada por FISA del grupo GL events Chile, Aquasur se ha consolidado como una plataforma estratégica para el diálogo, la transferencia de conocimiento y la creación de redes de colaboración que fortalecen el crecimiento de la acuicultura a nivel nacional e internacional.
Location: Región de Los Lagos, Región de Los Lagos

Name: Avanti Group
Description:
Established in 1993, Avanti Feeds has grown into one of India's largest integrated fisheries companies, encompassing interests in Hatcheries, Farms, Aqua feed, and Processing. Committed to promoting sustainability and reliability within the aquaculture industry, Avanti Feeds strives to enhance the sector's long-term viability.

Listed on the Indian Stock Exchanges, Avanti Feeds has forged a joint venture with the esteemed Thai Union Group Public Company Limited.

With a workforce exceeding 5000 employees, the company has achieved annual revenues surpassing $500 million. Avanti Feeds has garnered numerous accolades, including recognition as one of the Fortune Next 500 companies, Fortune 500 India, and Forbes Asia's Best under a Billion.

"Avanti Tickr: Our Distinguished Stock Market, Setting the Standard in Fisheries Excellence "

Name: misPeces.com
Description:
mispeces.com is a quality aquaculture breaking news, in Spain, Portugal, Greece, Italy, France, and rest of Mediterranean , reports, multimedia.
mispeces.com is increasingly a multiplatform in delivery and global in reach.
The Company’s core purpose is to enhance aquaculture by creating, collecting and distributing high-quality news, information and entertainment.
mispeces.com incudes blogacuicola.com, youtube channel http://www.youtube.com/mispeces and facebook.
Location: El Puerto de Santa María (Cádiz), Andalucía

Name: CRIMASA CRIADEROS DE MARISCOS S.A
Description: PRODUCTORES DE CAMARÓN DESDE 1972

Name: Sealord Group Ltd
Description:
Sealord is a leading global seafood company based in Aotearoa New Zealand, that is dedicated to sustainable fishing practices and providing high-quality seafood to consumers across the globe. Sealord focuses on delivering excellence in every aspect of its operations. 

At Sealord, we believe in doing right by the environment and our community. We understand the importance of preserving our oceans and ensuring the longevity of marine resources. 

We are a diverse and dedicated group of people who are passionate about the seafood industry and always welcome more people that share our values. Whether you are interested in exploring career opportunities at Sealord or learning more about our delicious products, we invite you to join us on this exciting journey. Find out more at http://www.sealord.com

Name: FRDC - Fisheries Research and Development Corporation
Description:
The FRDC is widely recognised as the leading Australian agency concerned with planning, funding and managing fisheries research and development.

The FRDC does not undertake research itself; rather it identifies research and development needs - and the means of addressing them - through a planning process and by contracting with research providers.

Stakeholders in the FRDC are the fishing industry; the governments of the Commonwealth, the states and the territories; and the people of Australia.

The FRDC acknowledge Australia’s Indigenous People who are the Traditional Owners of country throughout Australia and recognises their continuing connection to land, waters and culture. We pay our respect to their Elders both past and present and extend that respect to all Indigenous People.

Name: Fishery.News: Aquaculture Media & Marketing Hub
Description: A Digital Platform covering Insights & Opportunities for India’s Fisheries and Aquaculture Industry. Fishery.News aims to bridge the information gap in India’s fisheries and aquaculture industry.

Name: Global Shrimp Forum
Description:
Welcome to the Global Shrimp Forum!

Join us in September for the next annual Global Shrimp Forum—a fully dedicated, high-level executive meeting place for the warm-water shrimp industry. We will gather over 500 delegates representing farmers, feed companies, technology suppliers, service providers, processors, exporters, importers, wholesalers, retailers, civil society and other industry stakeholders.

Established by the Aquaculture Stewardship Council, Shrimp Insights and Contango, the Global Shrimp Forum Foundation is an independent non-profit and mission-oriented organisation based in Utrecht, the Netherlands.

The Global Shrimp Forum enables industry leaders to come together and discuss the most pressing issues that businesses are confronted with and to develop a strategy towards a resilient and sustainable industry.

Name: NCE Seafood Innovation
Description:
NCE Seafood Innovation is a leading business cluster within the seafood industry, with more than 150 partners and members representing the whole seafood value chain. The cluster plays a central role in contributing to the sustainable growth and development of the seafood industry. 


We believe that mobilizing joint forces is the way forward, and our cluster contributes through sustainable innovation projects and by facilitating interaction across the industry. Promoting team efforts is a focal point.

In the cluster, we push projects, provide services, and host meeting arenas to mobilize forces for collective efforts toward a sustainable seafood industry.

We work within five industrial areas of priority, which are defined by the industry and reflect the challenges we need to solve together with partners and members to realize opportunities for sustainable growth and development in the seafood industry. 

The five areas: 

Climate, Environment and Circular Economy
Digital Transformation and Digitalization
Fish health and welfare 
Future feed ingredients
Future competence and talent attraction

Name: Seafood Scotland
Description: Set up by the industry, Seafood Scotland are a small and busy team working to increase the value of return to the Scottish seafood sector through trade marketing promotion and business development support.

Name: Albacora
Description:
Albacora is a family-owned company with over 50 years of experience in responsible tropical tuna fishing. We oversee every stage of the process—from global fishing operations to the final product—ensuring sustainability, traceability, and quality.

Since our founding in 1974, we have operated with a strong commitment to the marine environment and to the communities where we work. We actively collaborate with scientific institutions and international organizations to promote sustainable fishing practices.

As we look to the future, our growth is guided by our innovation, technical expertise, and the values that define us as a family: responsibility, transparency, and respect for the sea.

Name: SAPMER
Description:
Savoir-faire et tradition au service d’une pêche d’exception depuis 1947.

Acteur majeur de la pêche en France, SAPMER est l’armement réunionnais pionnier de la grande pêche dans les eaux des Terres Australes et Antarctiques Françaises (TAAF) au départ de l’Ile de la Réunion. 

Créée en 1947, SAPMER opère une flottille de 8 navires de pêche sous pavillon français : quatre palangriers congélateurs pour la pêche à la légine, un caseyeur congélateur pour la pêche à la langouste et 3 thoniers senneurs surgélateurs (surgélation à bord à -40°C) pour la pêche au thon tropical (Albacore et Listao) dans l’océan Indien.

SAPMER est engagé dans une démarche de pêche responsable et durable avec une certification MSC au titre de sa pêcherie légine à Kerguelen et à Crozet et au titre de sa pêcherie Langouste à Saint-Paul et Amsterdam dans les Terres Australes et Antarctiques Françaises.

Elle adhère pour ses activités thonières aux programmes Dolphin Safe, Sustainable Indian Ocean Tuna Initiative (FIP SIOTI), ISSF Proactive Vessel Register (PVR) et la certification Friends of the Sea FAD Free. 

Positionnée sur des activités de niches à fortes valeurs, SAPMER segmente ses activités en deux catégories :
- Une activité de pêcherie qui comprend les ventes de légines et de langoustes australes pêchées dans les mers Australes (Zones économiques des Terres Australes et Antarctiques Françaises) et les ventes de thon brut (Albacore et Listao) pêché en océan Indien.
- Une activité de valorisation de ses pêches en produits Premium : longes sashimi, steaks, cubes, produits appertisés

APROMAR tiene como objeto la representación del sector empresarial de la acuicultura marina española, la prestación de servicios que contribuyan a mejorar la competitividad de sus asociados e impulsar comportamientos socialmente responsables para una acuicultura sostenible.

Name: National Fisheries Institute
Description:
NFI is the leading trade association for the seafood community. From vessels at sea to your favorite seafood restaurant, our diverse member companies bring delicious fish and shellfish to American families. NFI promotes the US Dietary Guidelines that recommends Americans include fish and shellfish in their diets twice per week for longer, healthier lives.
 
NFI and its members are committed to sustainable management of our oceans and being stewards of our environment by endorsing the United Nations’ Principles for Responsible Fisheries. Our investment in our oceans today will provide our children and future generations the health benefits of a plentiful supply of fish and seafood tomorrow.
 
From responsible aquaculture, to a marketplace supporting free trade, to ensuring the media and consumers have the facts about the health benefits of fish and shellfish, NFI and its members support and promote sound public policy based on ground truth science.

Name: HBZ-GROUP
Description:
HBZ GROUP

Founded in mid 80s by EL HABZA brothers, HBZ GROUP has become one of the largest and leading entities in the fishing industry in Morocco . The group is a consortium of 25 affiliated companies specializing in different, yet interlinked sectors. Focusing on the fisheries sector, but extending its activities to transport, real estate, education, shipbuilding and services has been our way of working.


HBZ GROUP has been and still investing considerably at a yearly basis in order to upgrade our equipment, expand our activities and strengthen our position as a leader of this sector. As a result, we are in full control of the supply chain, the value chain and the needed logistics. Such position enabled us to satisfy our clients both on quality and quantities.


Internationally, HBZ GROUP has invested in the building of a freezing plant in Senegal considered to be the first in the country to use automatized systems during production. The sky’s the limit this is the moto of the CEO of HBZ GROUP, Mr. Abderrahim El Habza , who’s in continuous efforts to bring the consortium to a level of excel to not compete at the national level but also to establish a strong foot print at the international level.

Name: Three Streams Smokehouse
Description:
Three Streams is owned by the Stubbs Family with a long history in the Fishing Industry, dating back to the early 1900’s. Gregory Stubbs,recognised the need to remove pressure from the ocean’s dwindling resources and believed that Aquaculture would be the only solution to meet the future Seafood demand. He began Trout farming in 1986 on the family farm as an undergraduate student, then went on to complete an MSc in Aquaculture at Sterling University, Scotland in 1989. Gregory then took the company on a vertical integration strategy, with brothers David and Andrew part of the company management team. Today the Three Streams Group is involved in the entire value chain, including Research & Development.

Our aquaculture farming practices are based on our deep caring for the planet and the absolute need for sustainable fish farming. Our founder was trained in the Scottish Highlands last century and these skills and techniques are used today to produce fish of both uncompromising quality and sustainability.

Modern facilities are used to produce over 1 million juveniles which are then placed on selected on-growing sites throughout South Africa. These fish are raised on a unique feed recipe which ensures the fish are lean and of the highest quality. One year later they return to our famous Three Streams Smokehouse for value-adding and distribution to the finest dining tables world wide.

When the Salmon and Trout arrive at the Smokehouse in Franschhoek we hand select the best fish for curing using traditional methods and our secret recipe of 25 years. We then rest the cured sides to allow the secret blend to gently mature.

We use time honored techniques with modern standards to produce a truly luxury product of exceptional taste. During the smoking process the fillets develop a deep, rich colour, the texture changes and the full smokey flavours ensue. Traditionally cured, naturally smoked with modern standards.

Name: Del Mar Seafoods
Description:
A vertically integrated seafood producer, processor, and exporter specializing in high-quality California market squid with industry-leading processing facilities and premiere fishing partners.  

With three generations experience in the seafood and fishing industry, Del Mar Seafoods is a family owned and operated business based in the Monterey Bay Peninsula. What started as a local operation selling calamari to restaurants, has grown into one of the largest seafood providers on the California coast. 
 
Today we catch, freeze, and export over 80 million pounds each year. We have the expertise, dedication, and passion to provide our customers with the highest quality seafood both domestically and globally.

Name: Centre for Sustainable Aquatic Resources (CSAR)
Description:
The Centre for Sustainable Aquatic Resources (CSAR) is an applied research unit within the School of Fisheries at the Fisheries and Marine Institute, Memorial University.

Recognized as Canada’s experts in fish capture, our team has core strengths in fishing gear design and testing, conservation engineering, fisheries biology, fish capture behaviour, and mechanical engineering.

The team uses a combination of numerical simulation, physical modelling in the flume tank, and at-sea comparative fishing experiments to improve sustainable fishing practices and profitability of commercial fishing enterprises.

This involves maximizing the value of the catch while at the same time reducing ecological impacts of fishing, such as bycatch of non-targeted animals, seabed impacts, fuel consumption, discarding, and ghost fishing of lost gears.

The team is also recognized for its expertise in fisheries development, including resource assessments of underutilized species. Documenting the abundance, distribution, and life history characteristics of new species of interest ensures that Canada’s emerging fisheries are established with excellent science advice.

Name: Centre for Aquaculture and Seafood Development
Description:
The Centre for Aquaculture and Seafood Development (CASD) is a comprehensive industrial response unit within the School of Fisheries at the Fisheries and Marine Institute of Memorial University. The Centre is internationally recognized for its applied scientific and technical expertise, comprehensive research facilities and commitment to clients in the seafood processing and aquaculture industries.

CASD employs scientists and technicians and undertakes contractual technical support services to the seafood and aquaculture sectors.

The Centre focuses on three main theme areas:

Seafood Processing
Aquaculture
Marine Bioprocessing
CASD possesses the largest pilot plant facilities of its kind in Atlantic Canada, with a combined area of nearly 1,400 square metres. Facilities include a 1,100 square metre state-of-the-art food processing pilot plant, an aquaculture facility and a marine bioprocessing facility.

Name: AquaX
Description:
AquaX is full-stack tech enabled aquaculture platform where-in we enable marginal farmers to do precision fish culture, handholding them using tech during whole life cycle , integrating them with our own retail centers/partners/market and add value to their produce ,  doubling the profit margins for farmers
Our solution is driven by IoT based remote technology to monitor partner farmer culture unit and using bioscience to lessen carbon footprint.On the other hand providing fully controlled retail centers/market for produce sale eliminating middlemen and increasing profit margins for our partner farmers.

Name: Caribbean Regional Fisheries Mechanism
Description: The Caribbean Regional Fisheries Mechanism (CRFM) was officially inaugurated on 27 March 2003, in Belize City, Belize, where it is headquartered, following the signing of the “Agreement Establishing the CRFM” on February 4, 2002. It is an inter-governmental organization with its mission being to “To promote and facilitate the responsible utilization of the region's fisheries and other aquatic resources for the economic and social benefits of the current and future population of the region”. The CRFM consist of three bodies – the Ministerial Council; the Caribbean Fisheries Forum; and the CRFM Secretariat. Its members are Anguilla, Antigua and Barbuda, The Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Dominica, Grenada, Guyana, Haiti, Jamaica, Montserrat, St. Kitts and Nevis, St. Lucia, St. Vincent and the Grenadines, Suriname, Trinidad and Tobago and the Turks and Caicos Islands.

We operate our own fleet of fishing vessels as well as shore and sea processing facilities, all working in harmony with our environment to bring the finest quality fish to our markets. Description:
Osprey Group is a fishing company where family values come first. Founded in 1941 by Louwe de Boer as de Boer Rederij, Osprey has grown into the largest Dutch shipping company, operating internationally with 85 employees. With 11 fishing ships, including 8 flyshoot/twinriggers and 3 beam trawlers, Osprey catches fish from the North Sea in a sustainable, responsible way. Since 2011, Osprey has been selling their fresh products to various countries in Europe. Honesty and quality are essential. The product is caught and sold directly from Osprey to the customer, so there are no additional middlemen involved. Simple and without any fuzz.  With fourteen different fish species from Plaice, sole to squid and turbot, we can meet our customer's needs. 24/7, 6 days a week we are ready to provide you with the best quality and service. 

The high family involvement and our sustainable ships, with high quality standards, make the fish caught by Osprey a unique product. The customer is our focus and the products we deliver are first class. Fresh or frozen, we deliver the fish the way you want it. 

Curious about what Osprey can do for you? Take a look at our website for our contact information. We would like to invite you to see how we can supply you with the best fish in Europe.

Foreningen for Skånsomt Kystfiskeri Producentorganisation (FSK-PO) er en fiskeriforening for fiskere, der bruger med skånsomme redskaber. 

Det skånsomme kystfiskeri høster af havet uden at ødelægge muligheden for fremtidens kystfiskere for at gøre det samme. Vi skal passe på havets natur og fiskene i det. Uden et sundt hav – intet kystfiskeri.  

FSK-PO arbejder for, at kystfiskeriet atter skal udgøre en vigtig og betydelig del af dansk erhvervsfiskeri. Afviklingen af havnene skal vendes til fremgang. Så danskerne igen opdager glæden ved den friskfangede fisk af høj kvalitet, og  får mulighed for at købe friske, gode fisk fanget med respekt for naturen, lokalmiljøet og den danske fiskertradition – uanset hvor i Danmark man måtte bo.

Spanning across Asia to the Americas and Europe to Australia, SFC has a vivid clientele list who have served as the backbone to the company's growth and success from the day of it's inception.
Over the years, SFC has relentlessly proved to be the market leader in seafood export, import and local supply with over 25 percent market share in the vibrant Omani seafood market, providing customers with the best quality seafood at prices that other competitors aren't able to match.

Name: Europêche
Description:
Europêche is the representative body for fishermen in the European Union representing around 45,000 vessels, both artisanal and large scale, 80,000 fishermen and counting 16 member organisations from 10 European countries.

“Our mission is to promote responsible and sustainable fishing practices that not only ensures a healthy and diverse marine environment but preserves an economically and socially sustainable fishing sector able to contribute to the growing world demand for healthy seafood.” Javier Garat, President of Europêche.

Europêche is the European platform enabling fluent communication between the European institutions and the fishing sector by keeping all relevant stakeholders informed of the concerns and objectives of EU fishermen in political decision making.

The value of the output of the industry represented by AIPCE-CEP amounts to around EUR 30 billion, about three and a half times the turnover of the EU catch sector.

Ci occupiamo dell’approvvigionamento, sia diretto sia da fornitori internazionali certificati, garantiamo abbattimento a bordo e rinvenimento presso i nostri stabilimenti, fino alla lavorazione con vari metodi e soluzioni, preservando le caratteristiche organolettiche del prodotto e assicurando un consumo sicuro, come attestano le nostre certificazioni;
Infine controlliamo lo stoccaggio, il trasporto e la consegna diretta al cliente nel rispetto della catena del freddo.

Name: Mississippi Department of Marine Resources
Description:
The Mississippi Department of Marine Resources (MDMR) is dedicated to enhancing, protecting and conserving the State's marine interests for present and future generations. Created by the legislature as a new state agency in 1994, MDMR manages Mississippi coastal resources through the authority of the Commission on Marine Resources. It manages all marine life, public trust wetlands, adjacent uplands and waterfront areas for the long-term recreational, educational, commercial and economic benefit of these resources consistent with environmental concerns and social changes.

The MDMR and the Commission on Marine Resources play an important role in administering Mississippi Seafood Laws, the Mississippi Coastal Wetlands Protection Act, the Public Trust Tidelands Act, the Boat and Water Safety Act, the Derelict Vessel Act, the Non-Point Source Pollution Act, the Magnuson Act, the Wallop-Breaux Sportfish Restoration Act, Marine Litter Act and other state and federal mandates.

In effect Sea Source catches, sells and processes its own fish and shellfish products, with the aim of reducing costs and maximising the financial return to local fishermen. Name: SOPASEN SA
Description:
SOPASEN | Pionnier de la pêche industrielle et de la transformation de produits halieutiques
Depuis 1985, SOPASEN (Société de Pêche et d'Armement Sénégalais) s’impose comme un acteur majeur de la pêche industrielle au Sénégal. Située au cœur du Port de Dakar, la SOPASEN associe expertise, innovation et engagement pour répondre aux plus hauts standards internationaux en matière de pêche et de transformation de produits halieutiques.
Avec un capital de 500 millions de FCFA et une flotte moderne de 19 chalutiers dont 15 crevettiers et 04 poissonniers, nous maîtrisons l’ensemble de la chaîne de valeur, de la capture à l’exportation, garantissant ainsi fraîcheur et traçabilité à nos partenaires.
Nos Activités
 Pêche industrielle: Avec une flotte de 19 navires, nous assurons une collecte optimale des ressources maritimes tout en respectant les normes environnementales.
 Transformation et stockage: Nos infrastructures de pointe, comprenant trois chambres froides d’une capacité totale de 1 300 tonnes et une usine de transformation pouvant traiter jusqu’à 60 tonnes par jour, nous permettent d’assurer une qualité optimale et une fraîcheur irréprochable de nos produits.
 Exportation: Forte de 40 ans d’expérience, SOPASEN est aujourd’hui l’une des entreprises de pêche industrielle les plus performantes du Sénégal. Nous employons plus de 1 000 collaborateurs, véritables artisans de notre succès, et exportons chaque année près de 15 000 tonnes de produits halieutiques vers l’Afrique, l’Union Européenne et l’Asie.
Nos Produits:
 Crustacés: Crevettes, crabes
 Poissons: Pélagique, Sompatte, Daurade, Sole langue
 Mollusques: Poulpes, Seiches
 Produits élaborés: filet de sole, couronnes de sole, chair de crabe, pince de crabe, crevette décortiquée.
 Un engagement pour une pêche durable et responsable:
Conscients des défis environnementaux, nous adoptons des pratiques de pêche durable visant à préserver les écosystèmes marins et limiter notre impact sur la biodiversité.

Name: PanaSea Aquaculture
Description:
In a time of increasing world hunger and endangered sea life, PanaSea Aquaculture provides a sustainable solution. Sea Cucumbers are a hatchery-based form of ocean farming and clean the ocean floor as they grow. Based on the Caribbean coast of Panama, PanaSea is devoted to protecting and preserving the marine ecosystem as well as bringing employment to a challenged labor market.
Our enterprise is committed to replenishing the depleted worldwide stores of Sea Cucumbers which have been overfished for their health benefits. Sea Cucumbers have been a staple in Eastern medicine for centuries and are known to have advantageous nutritional properties. High in protein and low in fat, they are especially prized in Chinese cuisine and culture.

It has always been able to maintain its reputations, experiences in trading seafood products. Correspondingly, accomplishing local and international market demands with consistent, competitive and superior quality seafood products. In conclusion, PT. SPA aims to be a strong establishment and recognizable as one of the fisheries company worldwide.

Name: Gendev Fishing Group Pty Ltd
Description:
Gendev was founded in the fifties by Mr. A.P. Du Preez Snr and Mr. P.S.G Neethling Snr. The company was 100% owned by Suiderland Corporation, a South African Company and specialised in catching, canning, fishmeal processing and the marketing of pelagic products – namely tinned pilchards and fishmeal.

From the fifties to the eighties, the pilchard biomass was healthy and Gendev then employed over 1500 people. Shortly after Independence, Gendev commenced with a Namibianisation process, and Guinas Investment and Dun-Al (another Namibian shareholder) came onboard as shareholders of Gendev.

In 1992, Gendev invested in a tuna canning plant and by 1993 the canning of pilchard and tuna, combined, employed 1465 people – making Gendev one of the major employers in the country. In 1993/1994, Gendev pioneered the canning of Horse Mackerel but the canning processing could not last, because of the tins manufacturing technology at the time.

In 1998, Gendev Fishing Resources (Pty) Ltd was granted mid water Trawl Horse Mackerel rights and in 2000, the pilchard canning plant was replaced with a state of the art freezing plant, to process the pilchard quota of Genmir Marine. 

The company underwent a few transformations over the years but today Gendev is a proudly 72% Namibian owned company. Shareholders include Gendev Fishing Resources (Pty) Ltd; Guinas Investment (Pty) Ltd; Dun-Al Fishing (Pty) Ltd; Compoposato Investments (Pty) Ltd; Vernier Investments (Pty) Ltd and Eco Fish Processors (Pty) Ltd.

Gendev is the first land-based Horse Mackerel Factory in Namibia and the Inauguration took place in 2014. Gendev welcomed the Minister of Fisheries and Marine Resources – Honorable Bernhard Esau along.

Name: Shellfish Association of Great Britain
Description:
We are the industry's trade body based at Fishmongers' Hall, by kind permission of the Fishmongers' Company, one of London's oldest livery companies, which is also home to the Worshipful Company of Fishmongers, in the City of London.

Our membership is composed of shellfish farmers, fishermen, fishermen's Associations, processors, commercial traders and retail companies including restaurants, many of the Inshore Fisheries Conservation Authorities, organisations such as Seafish, academics, scientists, consultants and anyone with a passion for shellfish.

First founded as the Oyster Merchants' and Planters' Association in 1903, and renamed the Shellfish Association of Great Britain in 1969, the association has steadily extended its range of activities from "harvest to sale" in over a century of support to the industry.

HOW CAN WE ASSIST YOU?

Name: Catch Welfare Platform
Description:
Public interest in catch welfare is growing. Four out of five European consumers now think that fish deserve the same protection as other food-production animals. 

Tesco and other leading supermarkets are setting welfare standards for seafood products. Government agendas also increasingly consider fish welfare. 

The debate on animal welfare is currently dominated by NGOs, policy officers and researchers. This may lead to unrealistic expectations or suggestions that do not match the daily reality onboard. To create workable solutions for fisheries, it is key that industry is involved.

This is why we created the Catch Welfare Platform. By bringing scientists, fisheries and their suppliers together, we aim to create pragmatic solutions to improve catch welfare.

--------------------------

The Catch Welfare concept promotes profitability and sustainability in wild capture fisheries by reducing stress in the catch. Reduced stress improves meat quality and ethical reputation while also reducing collateral mortality in unwanted catches.

--------------------------

Our goals
Make fisheries future-proof by…
•	Giving industry a voice in the catch welfare conversation
•	Steering away from controversies and focusing on facts
•	Sharing knowledge, creating synergy and realistic expectations
•	Creating practical and profitable solutions for catch welfare

----------------------------------

This is an initiative by:

Wageningen Livestock Research 
Institute of Marine Research Norway
NOFIMA
Michelle Boonstra Consultancy

Partners:
the Arctic University of Norway
Carefish/Catch
The Fishing Daily

Name: Central American Fisheries
Description:
Central American Fisheries (CAF) is a Nicaraguan Corporation owned by Nicaraguan and Foreign investors. The Company has operations all along the Nicaraguan Caribbean and in the northern Pacific Coast also the main seafood processing plant located in Corn Island in the Caribbean Sea of Nicaragua. 

The plant is a self-sustained unit, with its own power, water, and security certified terminal, it has a capacity to process and freeze 30,000 pounds of seafood per day, specializing in lobster, shrimp and fish.

Main sources of supply for the plant are independent day fishermen. These spread around the Corn Islands as well as the Caribbean Cays. CAF also exploits a number of permits for industrial fishing vessels. The plant has implemented its HACCP program and has been certified by the Nicaraguan Authorities for export to the European Union and other markets. External, independent audits are performed yearly.
Corn Island is located 48 miles to the East of the coastal city of Bluefields. The island is surrounded by Caribbean sea of great beauty; it has 4 square miles of surface and a small population . An asphalt airstrip provides the main way of communication for tourist and businessmen. The CAF plant is one of the main economic activities of the island and is located to the Southwest.
Product is available almost all year round, and is sold in North American, European and Japanese markets under C-Island Prime brand or private brands. From Corn Island, the product is exported directly via Miami, Fl or other Caribbean ports. CAF packs product under private label for some very important importers and distributors.


CAF also has also a number of joint ventures in shrimp farms located by the Pacific Ocean "Estero Real", close to the Gulf of Fonseca, producing p.vannamei. Exports made from the Pacific coast are shipped from the plant, directly to their final destination.

Name: The Billfish Foundation
Description:
The Billfish Foundation (TBF) is the only non-profit organization dedicated solely to conserving and enhancing billfish populations around the world. A membership-based organization headquartered in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, TBF’s international network of supporters includes conservation-minded anglers, captains, mates, scientists, policymakers, tournament directors, clubs and sportfishing businesses. A three-tiered strategy of research, education, and advocacy are employed by TBF in order to create marine conservation strategies that benefit oceanic resources while also empowering the recreational fishing community.

TBF recognizes that future fishing opportunities depend on today’s conservation successes. By coordinating efforts and speaking with one voice, TBF is able to work for solutions that are good for the fish but not punitive to recreational anglers. If you want to help ensure continued fishing opportunities, healthy oceans, and successful related economies, then TBF is the team for you! Please take a moment to explore our website and learn more about TBF’s work, become a member and make your commitment to ensuring a strong billfishing future for generations to come.

Name: Comeau's Sea Foods Ltd.
Description:
Comeau’s Sea Foods Group of Companies was founded by Bernardin J. Comeau and his brother, Clarence Comeau, in 1946 and has been a mainstay in the Canadian seafood industry ever since.

Comeau’s is a leader in the harvesting, processing and global distribution of fish and seafood products. We have pushed our boundaries using enhanced communications and secure transportation systems in order to reach our customers and suppliers all over the world.

Comeau’s Sea Foods Group offers fresh goods such as scallops, herring, herring meal and oil. We also process a full line of quality frozen products. Our value added product lines include, but is not limited to, smoked salmon, smoked mackerel, salt fish, breaded scallops, seafood spreads, seafood chowders and ready to eat items.

Comeau’s Sea Foods commitment to quality goods and services extends beyond our seafood products to the parts and supplies used throughout its production facilities. Comeau’s Sea Foods services also include industrial grade metal fabrication, machining, ship repair and maintenance services and the Nova West Laboratory.

By placing an emphasis on sourcing the right tools for the job and a fully equipped stockroom, we are able to ensure that production machinery consistently generates superior grade goods.

Our products are primarily from Atlantic Canadian sources and, with very few exceptions, all of our processing takes place in Nova Scotia. We are pleased to offer a selection of commodity and value-added products.

Comeau’s employees are loyal, dedicated and take pride in being part of the Comeau’s Sea Food’s family. 

We employ over 400 people and are one of the largest employers in Southwest Nova Scotia.

Comeau’s has employed multiple generations of families and many have worked for more than 30 years with the company.

Name: Supreme do Brasil Pescados
Description:
A Supreme do Brasil atua no mercado de piscicultura desde 2008, participando ativamente no crescimento da produção de Tilápia no Brasil.  

Situada na cidade de Santa Clara D’oeste, interior de São Paulo, a empresa conta atualmente com 100 colaboradores especializados em todos os processos a ponto de garantir a qualidade do produto desde a reprodução até sua distribuição.  

Na Supreme do Brasil são concentrados todos os processos, desde a reprodução dos alevinos, engorda em piscicultura própria, filetagem e embalagem em frigorífico próprio, além da distribuição. Dessa forma, temos a rastreabilidade da Tilápia durante todo o processo de crescimento, garantindo assim a qualidade do produto final.  

Atualmente contamos com distribuição no estado de São Paulo e nossa marca vem se tornando forte e em constante crescimento.  

A piscicultura no Brasil cresceu cerca de 4,9% no ano de 2019, chegando a produção média de 758.006 toneladas. Temos orgulho de fazer parte desses números com incríveis 14 toneladas de abate diário, mantendo o país como 4º maior produtor de Tilápia do mundo.  

Orgulho também de sermos sustentáveis. Além dos filés do peixe, conseguimos a produção própria de óleo e farinha de peixe, tendo o aproveitamento máximo das Tilápias. A farinha de peixe é utilizada como aditivo no alimento para aves, em especial galinhas poedeiras, suínos, ovinos e bovinos, em especial vacas leiteiras. É também utilizado como ração para animais aquáticos criados em aquicultura, especialmente para a criação de camarão e em operações de piscicultura. O uso de farinhas de peixe reduz significativamente os custos de produção industrial desses animais para o seu rápido crescimento, melhorando a sua situação nutricional, com importantes reflexos na melhoria da fertilidade e na saúde dos animais, causando uma acentuada diminuição da incidência de doenças.

Currently, the main commercial resources fished in the SPRFMO Area are Jack mackerel and jumbo flying squid in the Southeast Pacific and, to a much lesser degree, deep-sea species often associated with seamounts in the Southwest Pacific.

Name: Sgara
Description:
Sgara integrates the shrimp supply chain from farmer to buyer.

With Indonesia being the 4th largest shrimp producer and more than 95% of its shrimps exported, Indonesia still face lots of problems in its shrimp supply chain.

Farmers are still selling their shrimps to a chain of middlemen instead of selling them directly to end buyers which affects its prices and the possibility of fraud. If they were to sell their shrimps directly to end buyers, farmers don't have their own logistics and end buyers pay in longer payment terms which would delay the farmers' farming cycle resulting in less yield in a year.

Sgara connects farmers directly to end buyers, cuts out middlemen, provides dedicated professional harvesting and logistics team, and provide faster payments.

Chaque jour, nous développons notre esprit d'équipe et de partage au sein de notre entreprise, avec nos équipes, mais aussi avec nos partenaires, au premier rang desquels, les pêcheurs, garants de la qualité de nos approvisionnements.
Notre ambition : "valoriser les produits de la mer, dans le respect des matières premières et de notre savoir-faire, en créant des recettes variées et équilibrées à la fois issues de l'histoire de la conserverie et de notre créativité. 
Nous voulons le faire en respectant nos valeurs et en nous tournant vers l'avenir pour faire vivre et évoluer ce métier sur notre territoire.

Name: AquaClue.
Description:
AquaClue: Empowering Aquaculture for Smarter, Sustainable Success

Welcome to AquaClue, a pioneering digital platform designed to transform the way fish and shrimp farmers manage and grow their aquaculture ventures. With a user-friendly interface and a powerful suite of tools, AquaClue enables aquaculture farmers to make informed decisions, enhance productivity, and achieve sustainable growth.

Our mission is to bridge the gap between traditional aquaculture practices and modern technology, making critical insights accessible and actionable. AquaClue offers essential features like real-time weather updates, personalized to-do lists, a comprehensive calendar, in-depth educational resources, and a species-specific database—all tailored to the unique needs of fish and shrimp farmers.

Looking to the future, we’re committed to delivering cutting-edge IoT integrations and data-driven insights. With premium options for seamless online data syncing and smart calculators for feed, water quality, growth, and more, AquaClue is more than an app; it's a partner in every farmer's journey toward efficient, sustainable aquaculture.

Join us as we build a community that thrives on shared knowledge, innovative solutions, and a passion for better aquaculture. AquaClue—Empowering Farmers, Enriching Aquaculture.

Description:
Notre activité est intimement liée à notre territoire : nous sommes implantés à Roscoff en Bretagne depuis 1991 et nous valorisons les algues, une ressource marine d’exception. 

BRETALG se positionne sur le segment de la vente d’algues fraîches salées. Nous commercialisons nos produits via un réseau d’industriels et de grossistes alimentaires et nous sommes également reconnus en tant que centrale d’achat professionnelle dédiée aux algues alimentaires. Afin de répondre aux exigences des principaux clients et conformément aux standards internationaux (IFS / BRC) nous avons investi dans une nouvelle chaîne de production et dans la mise en place d’un système de traçabilité.

BRETALG propose principalement six variétés d'algues alimentaires fraîches salées et nous poursuivons l'enrichissement de notre gamme en proposant des algues déshydratées, en paillettes ou entières. Nous enrichissons continuellement nos gammes, n'hésitez pas à nous contacter !


BRETALG, established in 1991, is located in Roscoff, Brittany. We are a human-size company and our activity is strongly tied to the land. We value the Breton coast: a marine resource exceptionally rich in algae.

We mainly provide six fresh salty algae varieties by hand harvesting. We sell our products exclusively through a worldwide network of clients in the food business. Moreover, we are also a central buying office dedicated to the food algae. The takeover of BRETALG in 2015 has allowed the implement of a new production line and a traceability system. This, in order to match the needs of our main clients, through international standards (IFS/BRC).

BRETALG offers six fresh salty algae varieties and propose a range of dry (flakes or whole sheet), also frozen fresh seaweeds Our products are enriched and updated regularly. Please do not hesitate to contact us for further information.

Name: GREENHEAD LOBSTER LLC
Description:
Our lobsters are harvested by a dedicated fleet of Stonington, Maine lobstermen and women, many of whom have been delivering their daily catch of lobsters from the waters of Penobscot Bay to the Greenhead Lobster docks for generations, carrying on the strong fishing heritage of their families and this small island community located in the Gulf of Downeast Maine.

We are the only company that handles every step in-house, from our dock to your door. Wholesale or retail, we take pride in our wild-caught, Stonington, Maine lobster. 

On the docks daily for 25 years, Greenhead Lobster is Maine’s largest family-owned lobster supplier. Nestled in the Gulf of Maine, the Penobscot Bay provides the perfect environment for producing sweet lobster—cold, clean water, rocky, rural shores. The result is the most delicious, tender, sweet lobster, delivered to our docks every day. 

Our dockside tank rooms filter ocean water, providing the most natural environment to hold lobsters while they are graded for size and quality. Lobsters for the live market are then trucked to our Seabrook, NH fulfillment facility. This tank room location makes shipping nation and worldwide easy. 

After 25 years in the live lobster business, owner, Hugh Reynolds, recognized the importance of quality seafood processing and invested in creating the highest quality processing facility, located just 40 minutes from our Stonington, Maine docks. This investment allows Greenhead Lobster to have quality control over every step of processing. 

Using a High Pressure Processor (HPP) is what sets lobster processors apart. Sending live lobsters through our HPP allows for the quickest, most humane death. This stress-free, non-thermal process preserves flavor + texture. 

Whether live or processed, wholesale or retail, Greenhead Lobster takes pride in providing the highest quality lobster and representing the hardworking lobstermen and women of our community.

Name: Afritex Ventures
Description:
A taste for adventure. A passion for quality.

Our entire vessel fleet is self-owned and managed and all locations have state of the art packing and processing facilities, one with an on-site ice plant. All our operations are EU and HACCP accredited and our longline facility in Mozambique is BRC and Fair Trade certified.		
			
Our team collectively has decades of expertise in the fishing industry, with knowledge and skills development passed to all personnel across the board. Our group places high value on fair and ethical treatment of our personnel and corporate social responsibility. We are actively involved with various non-profit organisations – contributing towards HIV awareness, drug rehabilitation and have established a feeding program where we donate fish, which is a high source of protein to orphanages.	
		
Our freshly caught seafood is exported as fresh or value-added into a range of chef-inspired retail lines, merchandised under our Wild Side and Pristine Seafoods brands, the renowned Margaritaville brand and various private labels. These retail lines include a variety of tuna poke options, fish steaks with delectable toppings and sauces, seafood burgers, langoustine mac ‘n cheese and fish tacos. Our Afritex group is constantly striving to keep up with global market trends and to develop new and exciting products.

Name: FEDERPESCA - Federazione Nazionale delle Imprese di Pesca
Description:
La Federazione Nazionale delle Imprese di Pesca (FEDERPESCA), costituita nel 1961 e da allora aderente a CONFINDUSTRIA, associa, rappresenta e tutela gli armatori della pesca italiana e le imprese della filiera ittica presso la Pubblica Amministrazione, il Parlamento e l’Unione Europea. E’ parte contraente del C.C.N.L. della Pesca marittima di riferimento nel settore, in quanto maggiormente applicato, e del C.C.N.L. dei retifici meccanici.

In uno con le OO.SS. dei lavoratori, FEDERPESCA è parte dell’Osservatorio Nazionale della Pesca, dell’Ente Bilaterale della Pesca (E.BI.PESCA) e del Fondo di Assistenza integrativo del Servizio Sanitario Nazionale  (FIS Pesca) per la gestione degli istituti normativi previsti dal C.C.N.L. della Pesca.

Le imprese associate, circa duemila, esercitano l’attività di pesca, la trasformazione e la commercializzazione dei prodotti ittici, i servizi alle imprese, la produzione di reti e attrezzature per la pesca, le costruzioni navali e la produzione di impianti ed apparecchiature nell’ambito della filiera ittica allargata.

Obiettivo principale di Federpesca è quello di supportare le imprese della pesca italiana sia attraverso azioni di ordinaria assistenza e consulenza, che tramite iniziative specifiche volte a proiettarle su uno scenario di maggiore efficienza e competitività.

La Federazione interviene, anche attraverso Federpesca Ricerca&Sviluppo S.R.L., per sostenere le imprese in ambito internazionale, nella ricerca di mercati e nella intermediazione della filiera ittica; fornisce servizi reali e sostegno nell’attività di ricerca e studio delle esigenze di consumo, delle tecniche di conservazione, preparazione e confezionamento dei prodotti ittici.

Federpesca è membro della Federazione del Mare, è parte del Consiglio Direttivo del Cluster Tecnologico Blue Italian Growth (BIG) ed è promotrice della FEDEROP.IT, Associazione delle Organizzazioni dei Produttori della Pesca italiane.

Name: LA BALINESA
Description:
La Balinesa es una empresa especializada en la elaboración de pescado ahumado (salmón, bacalao y trucha). Contamos con más de 35 años de historia en los que hemos aprendido formas tradicionales y únicas de elaborar nuestros ahumados y convertirlos en un verdadero placer para el paladar. 
Gracias a nuestra constante apuesta en la innovación, calidad y sobre todo a nuestro equipo humano somos un referente en el mercado nacional con presencia de nuestras marcas en los principales retail del país e internacional en los mercados portugués, francés e italiano. Nuestra resposabilidad con la calidad y la seguridad alimentaria de nuestros ahumados son nuestro principal compromiso, contamos con la certifición IFS.  
La Balinesa pertenece al Grupo Brasmar, uno de los mayores players en el mercado, que tiene como reto principal adaptarse a las necesidades del mercado ofreciendo productos de calidad que estén a la altura de las expectativas de cada segmento del mercado al que van dirigidas. Asume el desafío de crear valor añadido al consumidor mediante un compromiso real con el cambio de la forma de pensar, en el desarrollo de nuevos procesos y adopción de tecnologías innovadoras para contribuir a un futuro prometedor, sostenible y valorado por los clientes más exigentes.

Name: Prince William Sound Aquaculture Corporation
Description:
Prince William Sound Aquaculture Corporation (PWSAC) is a non-profit corporation founded in 1974 by a local commercial fishermen’s organization to optimize Alaska’s wild salmon resources.

PWSAC produces hatchery-born, ocean-raised wild salmon for the commercial, sport, personal use and subsistence fisheries in the Prince William Sound and Copper River regions.

With headquarters in Cordova, Alaska, PWSAC operates four remote salmon hatcheries in Prince William Sound and one on the Copper River in Paxson. About 600 million salmon fry and smolt are produced for release into Pacific waters. The returning adults benefit all user groups.

PWSAC operations are financed through the sale of fish to processors and a salmon enhancement tax paid by commercial fishermen.

Name: Danforel
Description:
Europe's leading, largest, and oldest producer of fresh warm-smoked trout.
Since its beginnings in 1939, Danforel has been a private corporation that now employs around 140 people. Today we are Europe's leading, largest, and oldest producer of smoked trout fillets.

Efficient production with dedicated employees
Smoked trout fillets with Danforel's high quality and freshness are the result of our carefully organised, modern production in Grindsted. Daily we produce up to 120,000 consumer packs daily at our 15,000 m2 factory in Grindsted, Denmark. The majority comes from our own six aquaculture facilities. All our fish are ASC certified for responsible farming. Danforel is IFS certified and the majority of our fish is MAP packed to give the consumer the best quality.
With more than 75 years in the business, we know what is important to the consumer and to our customers.
We value a good working environment and a positive company culture. Danforel employees have an impressively high seniority - especially compared to the rest of the industry. At Danforel, we work to provide our employees with varied work, and we continuously train and develop our employees.

Name: Ocean Disclosure Project
Description: Established in 2015 by Sustainable Fisheries Partnership, the Ocean Disclosure Project (ODP) is a global platform for voluntary disclosure of seafood sourcing. The ODP is dedicated to increasing transparency in seafood supply chains by encouraging companies to publicly report on the seafood they source. The ODP provides a common template through which companies can disclose their wild-caught and farmed seafood sources, along with key indicators of sustainability, including certification and improvement, ratings, and environmental impacts of these sources. This information is used to create annual profiles reflecting the company’s sourcing from the previous year, which are then published on the ODP website.

Name: Aquatrade
Description:

Over the years, the company has evolved along with the industry and its needs, becoming what it is now: an integrated structure through which we support our clients at every stage of the work. Discover more on our website.

On these pages you will find information about the company, the vessel and our products of premium quality.

Name: FishBox Africa
Description: We empower fish farmers with comprehensive solutions that integrate advanced technology and financial support. Our AI-powered farm management software and IoT-based smart feeders optimise operations, reduce costs, and increase yields. We also offer robust security services to protect farm assets and provide tailored financing options to support sustainable growth. By addressing challenges such as high cost of production, environmental degradation, and social inequalities, we aim to de-risk aquaculture investment in Africa.

Name: Copper River Prince William Sound Marketing Association
Description:
