These fishermen bring you two brands of Wild Alaska Salmon; Alaska's premium wild salmon, Copper River salmon, and Prince William Sound salmon.

We offer point of sale materials, branding support, and high quality digital assets for your in house custom creative.

Visit our websites to learn why the Copper River and Prince William Sound salmon fisheries yield the highest quality wild Alaska king, sockeye and coho salmon.

Name: Landcatch Natural Selection
Description:
Landcatch Natural Selection is the aquaculture breeding division of Hendrix Genetics. Its core focus is on providing high quality salmon eggs, smolts (juvenile fish) and genetic services to salmon producers globally. Its unique expertise and technologies can be applied to any farmed aquatic species. 

Based in Scotland, but operating worldwide, the company has an excellent reputation in breeding technology. It has pioneered vital aspects of improving resistance to common diseases, infection from parasitic sea lice and pancreatic disease in salmon, as well as improving growth and feed conversion efficiency. As part of Hendrix Genetics, Landcatch is developing to serve a much bigger and wider range of aquaculture customers.

Name: Thetis Acquacoltura SRL
Description:
A company located near the Venetian Lagoon, specialising in all stages of production for different species of shellfish.

The Venice Lagoon has a long history of shellfish aquaculture and high demand for our products. Approximately 70,000 tonnes of clams are harvested each year in the area.

Name: Song Fish Dealer Pte Ltd
Description:
Song Fish Dealer Pte Ltd was established in 1975. Building on our reputation of exceptional quality, we expanded our business to become a leading supplier of frozen seafood and poultry. Today, our client base comprises of hotel chains, fine-dining restaurants, retailers, wholesalers and ship chandlers. We export a wide variety of products throughout Asia and the rest of the world, ensuring on-time delivery of cargo - as promised.

Our factory, sitting on an extensive land area of 5824sqm, has 3000 tonnes of cold storage capacity. We are fully equipped with modern blast freezing equipment and a fully automated packaging line. Operated by staff with first class processing skills, we consistently deliver top grade value added products.

As a HACCP and ISO 22000 certified company, our processing and packing operations comply to world health standards. We provide our customers with the highest quality seafood at the most competitive prices, and have been awarded the “A” rating by The Agri-food and Veterinary Authority of Singapore (AVA).

Our company has established a wide network of international packers and traders. Our goal is to help our clients grow their business, by leveraging on our strong reputation and extensive network of suppliers and partners.

Quality is our Number 1 priority, and our word is our promise.

Name: Polar Seafood Greenland
Description:
Polar Seafood Greenland er en førende aktør i den nordatlantiske fiskeriindustri. På basis af såvel egne havgående fartøjer som højteknologiske, landbaserede fabrikker har Polar Seafood Greenland gennem 40 år haft fokus på bæredygtig fangst af seafood til kvalitetsbevidste kunder over hele verden. Vores kernefokus er at udvikle og forædle et bredt udvalg af fisk og skaldyr, hvor vi kombinerer århundreders grønlandske fiskeritraditioner med nye, innovative metoder. Dette sikrer, at vores produkter ikke alene opfylder alle internationale standarder, men befinder sig i det absolutte førerfelt inden for premium seafood.

Vores engagement i miljøansvarlighed og bæredygtighed er fundamentalt, idet vi lever af - og respekterer - balancen i Arktis' unikke økosystem. Vi praktiserer således ansvarlige fiskerimetoder samt bevarelse af traditionelle, lokale samfunds erhvervsmuligheder. Med andre ord: Polar Seafood Greenland er dedikeret til at opretholde de højeste standarder for kvalitet og friskhed, samtidig med at vi bidrager til økonomisk vækst i vores lokalsamfund og miljøer.

Vi er som virksomhed dybt engageret i at arbejde med FNs Verdensmål. Dels gennem vores generelle ansvarlighed for mennesker og miljø, men også via vores uddannelsesprogrammer, MSC-certificering og et konstant fokus på bæredygtigt ressourceforbrug. 

I Polar Seafood Greenland er vi stolte af at repræsentere Grønlands ekstraordinære maritime arv, og med udgangspunkt i vores historie forpligter vi os til kontinuerligt at udvikle innovative, bæredygtige metoder til at levere verdensklasse seafood til det globale marked.

Name: Maine Lobster Dealers' Association
Description: The Maine Lobster Dealers Association is the single, united voice that supports lobster wholesalers and processors. MLDA was formed in 1985 in order to create a forum for Maine lobster businesses to come together and discuss important issues facing the industry. Our concerns include sustainability, economic viability and long-term health of the Maine lobster industry. The MLDA convenes the innovators and marketers of the Maine lobster industry and allows them to productively share their ideas and concerns. We collaborate with industry stakeholders to work proactively to support our business members.

Name: Columbia River Inter-Tribal Fish Commission
Description:
The Columbia River Inter-Tribal Fish Commission coordinates management policy and provides fisheries technical services for the Yakama, Warm Springs, Umatilla, and Nez Perce tribes.

CRITFC’s mission is accomplished with four primary goals:
1. Put Fish Back in the Rivers and Protect Watersheds Where Fish Live
2. Protect Tribal Treaty Fishing Rights
3. Share Salmon Culture
4. Provide Fisher Services

CRITFC employs around 130 staff in five locations: Headquarters (Portland, Oregon), Hagerman Genetics Lab (Hagerman, Idaho), Enforcement Office (Hood River, Oregon), Fishing Site Maintenance Office (The Dalles, Oregon), and the Coastal Margin and Ocean Prediction Center (Astoria, Oregon).

CRITFC employs biologists, data analysts, ecologists, meteorologists, public information specialists, policy analysts, accountants, lawyers, and administrators who work in fisheries research and analyses, advocacy, planning and coordination, harvest control and law enforcement.

Name: Observatoire des pêches côtières de Nouvelle-Calédonie
Description:
La 𝐩𝐞̂𝐜𝐡𝐞 𝐞𝐧 𝐎𝐜𝐞́𝐚𝐧𝐢𝐞 est un des piliers de la culture, de l’organisation sociale, de la sécurité alimentaire, et du développement économique des territoires. En Nouvelle-Calédonie, la pêche côtière occupe naturellement une place centrale dans le tissu social, culturel et économique de l’archipel. 𝐏𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐠𝐞́𝐫𝐞𝐫 𝐥𝐚 𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐨𝐮𝐫𝐜𝐞 𝐝𝐞 𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐢𝐞̀𝐫𝐞 𝐞́𝐪𝐮𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐛𝐫𝐞́𝐞 𝐞𝐭 𝐝𝐮𝐫𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞, il est important de connaître dans le détail toutes les pêches pratiquées, de manière professionnelle ou non. Si certaines informations existent depuis longtemps, elles nécessitent d’être rassemblées, complétées et interprétées pour avoir une vision globale : c’est le rôle de 𝐥’𝐎𝐛𝐬𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐨𝐢𝐫𝐞 𝐝𝐞𝐬 𝐩𝐞̂𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐬 𝐜𝐨̂𝐭𝐢𝐞̀𝐫𝐞𝐬 𝐝𝐞 𝐍𝐨𝐮𝐯𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐞-𝐂𝐚𝐥𝐞́𝐝𝐨𝐧𝐢𝐞. Il pourra ainsi produire des 𝐨𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐥𝐬 𝐝’𝐚𝐢𝐝𝐞 𝐚̀ 𝐥𝐚 𝐝𝐞́𝐜𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧 clairs et efficaces, et informer régulièrement tous les publics intéressés.

Une mission : centraliser, produire, valoriser, communiquer les données sur la pêche côtière afin d’éclairer les décisions. 3 objectifs :
-	Centraliser, compléter et valoriser les informations sur la pêche côtière
-	Améliorer les connaissances utiles à la pêche côtière
-	Communiquer sur la pêche côtière et animer le réseau d’acteurs

Fruit d’une volonté partagée des collectivités, de plusieurs années de réflexion et d’études préalables, c’est au sein de 𝐥’𝐀𝐃𝐄𝐂𝐀𝐋 𝐓𝐞𝐜𝐡𝐧𝐨𝐩𝐨𝐥𝐞 et dans le cadre du projet 𝐏𝐑𝐎𝐓𝐄𝐆𝐄, financé par 𝐥’𝐔𝐧𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐞𝐮𝐫𝐨𝐩𝐞́𝐞𝐧𝐧𝐞 (11ème Fond européen de développement -FED- régional), qu’il a pu voir le jour.

Name: Faroe Islands Sustainable Fisheries - FISF
Description:
Faroe Islands Sustainable Fisheries (FISF) is a collaboration between the fishing industry and the research environment in the Faroe Islands with the purpose to foster sustainable fisheries in our oceans. We want to contribute to the availability of fish products from sustainable fisheries. In the Faroe Islands the fishing industry has chosen to let the Marine Stewardship Council (MSC) vouch for an international certification and regular audit of our fisheries.
#sustainability #fishery #msc #marinestewardshipcouncil

Name: SEAPA
Description:
SEAPA has grown to be a global supplier of equipment which assists in the production of high-quality oysters.

With more than 20 years of experience, SEAPA has worked closely within the aquaculture industry developing making intelligent farming equipment for oyster farming by identifying an opportunity to make growing oysters easier and more productive.

With a focus on developing relationships with forward-thinking farmers, SEAPA works in partnership with these farmers to continue to improve oyster growing systems across the globe, by introducing innovative systems to the market, developing and improving industry needs of today and tomorrow.

Name: The National Fisheries & Aquaculture Authority, Republic of Liberia
Description:
The National Fisheries & Aquaculture Authority (NaFAA) was created by an Act of National Legislation on October 9, 2017. It functions as a fully-fledged autonomous body pursuant to the Public Authorities Law of the Republic of Liberia. NaFAA has its roots in the erstwhile Bureau of National Fisheries, which operated as a unit within the department of Technical services of the Ministry of Agriculture from 1956 to 2017.
Our Goal
The overall fisheries sector goal is: Sustainably managed and economically viable fisheries that generate prosperity for the present and generations yet unborn.
Our Mission
In order to facilitate the attainment of the overarching vision of the sector, the Government, as essential operational actions, will promote an improved institutional and legislative framework for fisheries management; adaptive research; community and stakeholder participation in capture and culture-based fisheries management and development; effective international, regional, sub-regional and bilateral cooperation for fisheries management; enhanced monitoring, control, and surveillance capabilities; and improve fish quality and value addition technologies for enhanced economic returns in fisheries.
Our Vision
To harmonize the varied and numerous long-term expectations of the primary, secondary, and tertiary stakeholders, and recognize the strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats of the fisheries sector, a clear and unambiguous vision statement is crystallized.

Name: The Tilapia Journal
Description:
The Tilapia Journal is a website that covers Tilapia and Aquaculture news, prices and other industry related topics.
Established in late 2010, our journal was created in response to the industry's need for a reliable and responsible reference to current Tilapia prices, real time industry news and current research trends in aquaculture. 
Our mission is to promote the consumption of Tilapia as a safe, nutritious and healthy alternative to other commercial species and to become the point of reference for current industry prices.

Name: Aquaculture Morocco International Conference
Description: We are delighted to announce the II Edition of the AQUACULTURE MOROCCO INTERNATIONAL CONFERENCE (AMIC), a premier global platform uniting researchers, policymakers, industry professionals, and non-governmental organizations to tackle key challenges and highlight breakthroughs in the aquaculture sector, both within Morocco and globally. Following the success of the inaugural edition in 2024, which welcomed 10 distinguished international experts from Europe, Asia, and the Americas, AMIC remains committed to bridging global knowledge with local innovation. The first edition provided Moroccan stakeholders with a broad overview of the diversity in aquaculture species and technologies practiced around the world. AMIC 2025 Theme: Genetics & Nutrition key drivers for Sustainable Development of Aquaculture in Morocco This year’s conference will highlight the strategic role of genetics and nutrition as essential pillars in advancing aquaculture development in Morocco. More than 20 international experts in aquaculture genetics and nutrition will participate to share their expertise and engage in discussions around key focus areas, including: Enhancing selective breeding programs adapted to Moroccan environments Developing climate-resilient aquatic strains Preserving local aquatic biodiversity Utilizing local feed ingredients to support a circular economy Applying genetic and nutritional tools to predict and mitigate aquatic animal diseases Reinforcing food security and ecosystem sustainability We invite you to join us for this exciting exchange of cutting-edge science, best practices, and collaborative initiatives to shape the future of aquaculture in Morocco and beyond.

Name: جمعية وطني للاستزراع المائي Wattani Aquaculture Association
Description:
The association was established with permission from the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development No. 10090, which is concerned with the Fresh water aquaculture to achieve the Kingdom’s Vision 2030 and with the support of the responsible authorities. Given the importance of the aquaculture industry and its economic role in the areas of development and food security, we set the objectives of the association to achieve a new system in line with the aspirations of the relevant authorities. To achieve self-sufficiency in fish products and work to unify cooperative efforts in the field of fresh water aquaculture by organizing and developing projects. Our role comes in providing technical, advisory, administrative and marketing services and reducing production costs for producers, which helps them grow and sustain.

L'entreprise est basée à Brest, mais ses produits sont distribués à travers le monde, ce qui témoigne de leur popularité et de leur réputation grandissante. Fiiish se distingue notamment par son approche axée sur la recherche et le développement, ce qui lui permet de proposer des leurres performants et adaptés aux besoins des pêcheurs.

Le produit phare de Fiiish est le "Black Minnow", un leurre souple révolutionnaire qui a connu un immense succès. Elle propose également une large gamme d'autres leurres souples, tels que le Crazy Sand Eel, le Power Tail, le Mud Digger, et bien d'autres encore. Chaque leurre est conçu avec précision pour imiter les mouvements et l'apparence des proies naturelles, ce qui augmente les chances de succès des pêcheurs.

Name: Mallaig Harbour Authority
Description:
Mallaig Harbour is a busy West Coast Port, servicing the fishing and Aquaculture industries, and providing infrastructure for lifeline ferry services operated by CalMac and private operators. Ferries operate to Skye; North Uist; The Small Isles of Eigg, Rum, Muck and Canna; and to the Knoydart peninsula, and suring the summer months there are up to 30 ferry movements each day.  Mallaig is one of the most picturesque villages on the rugged North West coastline of Scotland. The area is a haven for sailors of all types and Mallaig Yachting Marina provides the ideal location for experiencing and exploring the magnificent sailing opportunities available on the West Coast of Scotland.
The Harbour provides a range of services including warehousing for aquaculture feed; a flake ice plant and shore power for vessels, and also hosts a salmon harvesting station serviced by daily well boats; two fish processors and a shore base for Scottish Seafarms. An RNLI Severn class lifeboat also operates from the Harbour.

Name: Fisch-Informationszentrum e.V.
Description:
Das Fisch-Informationszentrum, kurz FIZ genannt, ist ein eingetragener Verein, der 1997 von Unternehmen und Verbänden der deutschen Fischwirtschaft gegründet wurde. Zu den Gründern gehören die Hochseefischerei und Fischindustrie, der Fischgroß- und einzelhandel sowie die Fischgastronomie.

Das FIZ übernimmt die firmen- und produktübergreifende Öffentlichkeitsarbeit für diesen Wirtschaftsbereich. Im Mittelpunkt steht dabei die informierende, aufklärende und beratende Kommunikation mit den Medien und Multiplikatoren. Das FIZ ist die Anlaufstelle für Fragen jeglicher Art zum Thema Fisch.

Bei der Umsetzung von Themen und Beantwortung von Fragestellungen wird das FIZ von zahlreichen Experten aus der "fischwirtschaftlichen Praxis" unterstützt und kooperiert mit Spezialisten aus Forschung und Wissenschaft.

Ziel des FIZ ist es, den Stellenwert von Fisch, Krebs- und Weichtieren sowie der Produkte hieraus weiter zu erhöhen und ihre Bedeutung für eine zeitgemäße und gesunde Ernährung noch mehr herauszustellen.

Hier eine Auswahl unserer FIZ-Aktivitäten:
- Verbraucherinformation
- Regelmäßige Presseaussendungen an die Tagespresse, Wirtschafts- und Publikumspresse
- Jahrespressekonferenz
- Erstellung und Verbreitung aktueller Marktforschungsdaten
- Pressegespräche

FIZ-Mitgliedschaft
Das Fisch-Informationszentrum ist ein eingetragener Verein, der von seinen Mitgliedern finanziert wird. Falls Sie Interesse an einer Mitgliedschaft im FIZ haben, können Sie sich auf unserer Website über eine Mitgliedschaft informieren.

Name: Independent Fisheries Limited

1967 saw the establishment of the present-day site on the Bamford Industrial Estate at Woolston and in 1974, the company made the decision to concentrate on the manufacture of value-added fast foods. This move led to the set-up of a network of national distributors, aimed at further developing retail and food-service markets throughout New Zealand.

Name: Fishta Seafood
Description:
Fishta Seafood Inc. started with a simple idea, to provide "Fish for every Filipino". Since 2008 we have been bringing the freshest seafood from farm to market. Our vision is to establish our identity as the leading provider of clean, safe and traceable fresh seafood to the Philippine market. Our business focuses on the sourcing, distribution and retailing of live and fresh seafood.
 
As part of the ISO Certified Santeh Group, we follow strict protocols on fish handling and safety. This has enabled us ensure the reliability and quality of our products. Through the years we have built a reliable network of farms and gained in-depth expertise on seafood. And have strong linkages and knowledge in the aquaculture and fishing industries.
 
Our retail business covers an extensive network of supermarkets, weekend markets, and households in urban centers. Every year we continue to expand our distribution channel to bring healthy produce to Filipinos nationwide. Name: King Fish Group of Companies
Description:
Most welcome to all the honorable visitors, before introducing all our companies to you we would like to take the privilege of thanking you for sharing your precious time with us. Please feel worthy, pleasure will always be ours. 

King Fish Group of Companies was Established in 1986 in the heart of fisheries industry Veraval (GUJRAT). Name: Piscine Energetics
Description:
Our story began with the introduction of invasive Mysis shrimp into interior lake systems in British Columbia in the 1960’s. 

Mysis perform a daily 500ft vertical migration in near freezing water in order to feed on micro algae, phytoplankton and zooplankton near the surface of the lake. As a result of the energy required to make this vertical migration, they store remarkable levels of beneficial Omega 3 and Omega 6 unsaturated fatty acids, long chain amino acid proteins, powerful antioxidants and color enhancing caratenoids such as astaxanthin. Mysis are therefore an incredibly nutritious feed source for fish, corals, flamingos and even butterflies and were introduced to BC in the hopes that they would be a good feed source for native fish, boosting aquaculture. 

However instead, they disrupted the natural food web by outcompeting the juvenile Kokanee salmon, having a negative effect on native fish populations.  Piscine Energetics stepped in by pioneering proprietary harvesting technology for the removal of invasive aquatic organisms and now transforms them into sustainable, premium fish food.  PE Mysis are harvested at night as they feed and flash frozen in order to provide your fish and corals with a fresh, naturally gut loaded, nutritionally complete and life like food source. 

For over a decade, under the auspices of the British Columbia Ministry of Environment, Piscine Energetics has been simultaneously restoring the natural food web in BC lakes whilst transforming invasive shrimp into sustainable fish food, supplying global aquaculture markets, Zoos, Public Aquariums, and the pet industry for aquarium hobbyists.

The peak body is governed by a Board of eight Directors, elected by Managed Fishery Licence (MFL) holders, as well as two Independent Directors appointed by the Board.

WRL represents this leading primary industry by engaging with MFL holders and advocating on their behalf, co-managing the western rock lobster fishery with government, strategically planning the industry's future, mitigating risks facing industry, managing industry's research and development program, managing education and safety, engaging with stakeholders, promoting the industry and highlighting western rock lobster as premium seafood globally.

Description:

Smartrawl can be easily retrofitted into the nets of vessels with no cables needed.  

Smartrawl is a Fisheries Innovation and Sustainability (FIS) project and is supported by DEFRA and the UK Seafood Innovation Fund. Smartrawl is based out of Heriot-Watt University Edinburgh in the Lyell Centre and the National Robotarium.

Name: Western and Central Pacific Fisheries Commission (WCPFC)
Description:
The Western and Central Pacific Fisheries Commission (WCPFC) is an international fisheries management organization responsible for the conservation and sustainable use of highly migratory fish stocks, particularly tuna, in the Western and Central Pacific Ocean. The WCPFC comprises 41 member countries, territories, and cooperating non-member countries, dedicated to promoting the sustainable management of the region's fisheries resources.

The WCPFC Secretariat is headquartered in Pohnpei, Federated States of Micronesia, which is a central location in the fisheries of the Western and Central Pacific Ocean. Established in 2004, the Secretariat functions in support of the Commission and its subsidiary bodies: the Northern Committee (NC), the Scientific Committee (SC), the Technical & Compliance Committee (TCC), and the Finance and Administration Committee (FAC). The 31 staff members of the Secretariat hail from all parts of the WCPFC membership, including Korea, Vanuatu, Australia, the Cook Islands, and the Marshall Islands, as well as a large contingent of staff from Pohnpei.

FIA obtained the MSC certification of the tuna fish swimming in the PNG Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) and Archipelagic Waters (AW) in May 2020.

FIA has developed a strategic plan (5 years) to ensure the alignment with the SDGs applicable for the tuna fish operation and also to improve the responsible management of the fisheries in order to ensure food security for the next generation and local labor for Papua New Guinea.

Name: TunaSolutions
Description:
TunaSolutions was born out of a successful commercial tuna company that caught and traded sashimi grade tuna across the globe. Innovation has always been at the heart of TunaSolutions’ whereby the founding members successfully pioneered the export of fresh sashimi grade tuna from the small-scale sector in South Africa.

Today, TunaSolutions focuses on eliminating the inefficiencies within the fragmented tuna supply chain by de-risking trade opportunities with new international partners through our proprietary online marketplace. The marketplace connects buyers and suppliers through a series of real-time auctions, bringing visibility to global supply and demand. Our technology provides a fully integrated grading, traceability and training solution to ensure that product quality is maximised and transparency is maintained throughout the supply chain. By creating solutions to cultural, financial and logistical barriers, TunaSolutions allows suppliers to get their tuna onto high paying markets, gives traders access to a wider buying audience and buyers the opportunity to choose suppliers that better meet their requirements.

To support fisheries development, TunaSolutions identifies, develops and delivers a suite of expertise through our technical services program to help cultivate the development and build capacity within new and existing fisheries. This support is aimed to bridge the gap between producers and market expectations focusing on: improved capture techniques, product handling, processing procedures, product development, sales & marketing in an effort to maximise value of every fish and create unprecedented market access. TunaSolutions also works closely with buyers to assist in developing their preferred fisheries through the delivery of technical expertise that secures sustainable, traceable supply chains.

Our mission is to: “maximise the value of every tuna that is harvested from the ocean"

Name: The Florida Oyster Trading Company
Description:
Oysters are not simply a commodity, for many Americans they are a symbol of a way of life.  Sadly over the past decade, natural oyster reefs and subsequent wild harvests have dramatically declined to a fraction of historic levels.  This trend has necessitated the need for an alternative source – aquaculture, or farming of the seas.  Aquaculture is not only a sustainable method of producing protein for a growing global population but also relieves harvest pressure on the wild populations.  In some cases, such as oysters, the extensive process provides ecological benefits that improve the quality of entire ecosystems.

The Gulf coast oyster aquaculture industry is burgeoning but experiencing bottlenecks.  Lack of consistent, quality seed availability not only limits industry-wide development but simultaneously puts farms at financial risk.  In response FOC has developed an innovative, patent-pending model to self-sufficiently produce oysters available to help farms aspiring to produce mass volume but cannot due to an inconsistent supply chain.  This production model is breaking the chain of globalizing an antiquated concept and bringing fire to the people.  

Most importantly FOC is championing seafood integrity.  Goods and services available through the Florida Oyster Trading Company will be authentic products of their environments, utilizing genetics from the estuaries in which the harvests are grown – not outsourced from other bays or even other states.  Florida seed spawned with Florida broodstock to be raised in the same Florida estuaries, certified by Florida's Division of Aquaculture Best Management Practices protocols and standards.  Adhering to the highest of standards.  

The Florida Oyster Trading Company - Authentic, Craft Oysters

Companies interested in learning more about obtaining self-sufficient seed production capabilities, please contact us.

This project has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon 2020 Programme under grant agreement No 862658.

Name: Fish-X
Description:
About/Bio Fish-X Project

The Fish-X Project is a technology and open-source project which aims at making the European Union fishery industry sustainable. It is a 3-year project and has been initiated and funded by the EU Horizon Europe Programme.

The Fish-X Project is being implemented by seven European organisations, called consortium partners, including TransMarTech Schleswig-Holstein (TMT, Germany), EU Tech Chamber (EUTECH, Germany), Collecte Localisation Satellites (CLS, France), North.io (Germany), Sciaena (Portugal), OURZ (Germany), and WWF EPO (Belgium).

The Fish-X Project is developing a Fisheries’ Dataspace (Fish-X) and an Insight Platform that is based on smart orchestrated architecture and open interoperable technology via Gaia-X. The dataspace and platform allow users to utilise the data for improved fishing methods, increased yields, and protected endangered fish species. Moreover, the project also targets streamlining the Monitoring, Control, and Surveillance (MCS) Processes to support the implementation of Common Fisheries Policies (CFP). The project also brings together different stakeholders such as large to small-scale fisheries, fishermen associations, regulators, relevant ministries, and decision-makers, to participate in the consultative process of developing and refining new technologies in view of the changes and revisions in CFP.

Name: Pacific States Marine Fisheries Commission
Description:
Established in 1947 by consent of Congress, the Pacific States Marine Fisheries Commission (PSMFC) is an interstate compact agency that helps resource agencies and the fishing industry sustainably manage our valuable Pacific Ocean resources in a five-state region.  Member states include California, Oregon, Washington, Idaho, and Alaska.  Each represented by three Commissioners.  
Mission

As stated by the governing compact, PSMFC's purpose shall be “to promote the better utilization of fisheries – marine, shell, and anadromous, which are of mutual concern, and to develop a joint program of protection and prevention of physical waste of such fisheries in all of those areas of the Pacific Ocean over which the compacting states jointly or separately now have or may hereafter acquire jurisdiction.”

PSMFC's primary goal is to promote and support policies and actions to conserve, develop, and manage our fishery resources in California, Oregon, Washington, Idaho and Alaska. We accomplish this through coordinating research activities, monitoring fishing activities, and facilitating a wide variety of projects. We work to collect data and maintain databases on salmon, steelhead, and other marine fish for fishery managers and the fishing industry.

Name: Australia's Oyster Coast
Description:
Australia’s Oyster Coast is the world's largest food grade Rock Oyster producer and Australia's largest Rock and Pacific oyster processor. 

From working directly with farmers, as well as farming ourselves, to building relationships with wholesalers and retailers including chefs who serve our oysters at our country’s finest restaurants and everyone in between, we are passionate about supplying the best possible oysters to the Australian market and beyond.

As a vertically integrated business, we ensure the highest standards from farm to fork, responsible for growing, procuring, grading, transporting, marketing and selling oysters. Name: Norte Pesca S/A
Description:
A Norte Pesca é uma das empresas de pesca mais tradicionais do Brasil, tendo sido fundada em 1960 pela Família Hazin, atual controladora. Inicialmente se dedicou ao desenvolvimento e exploração da pesca da lagosta e sua exportação. É empresa pioneira nesta atividade.

Atualmente é uma das maiores exportadoras de pescado industrializado do Brasil, principalmente do peixe Atum tendo também significada presença no mercado nacional.

A Norte Pesca está envolvida na pesca de atuns e afins desde 1985 quando construiu seus primeiros barcos atuneiros congeladores. Já em 1995, iniciou um programa de arrendamento de embarcações estrangeiras que permitiu à companhia, assimilar tecnologia e converter toda sua frota para o modelo de espinhel monofilamento. O sucesso na captura de atuns e afins na região Nordeste do Brasil atraiu dezenas de embarcações de outras regiões do país, para, através do suporte da Norte Pesca, virem a operar baseados em Natal. O Know-how adquirido, a logística eficiente e um bom serviço de apoio às embarcações fazem hoje da Norte Pesca uma excepcional opção para basear sua frota no Brasil.

A missão da Norte Pesca é explorar a indústria e o comércio de produtos de Pesca e Aqüicultura de forma responsável, inovadora e diversificada, comprometendo-se com o respeito ao meio-ambiente e consciente da responsabilidade social perante as comunidades pesqueiras.

Description:

Name: GRUPO EURORED
Description:
Grupo Eurored is a group of companies with more than 45 years of experience dedicated to the design and manufacture of fishing gear as well as comprehensive aquaculture facilities and services.

We manufacture pelagic, semi-pelagic, bottom trawls, outrigger nets, etc., always at the cutting edge and developed hand in hand with our customers, as well as supplying everything necessary for fishing operations: MORGERE trawl doors, COTESI nets, ropes, stays, BRIDON cable, flotation, CROSBY GROUP marine hardware (CROSBY, TRAWLEX, GUNNEBO), longline material, etc.

In addition, Eurored Aquaculture is a division with the ability to offer comprehensive technical solutions for Offshore facilities, working with certified products from the most prestigious brands, and with the most rigorous suppliers in terms of quality in the sector worldwide, such as Gunnebo Aquaculture and The Crosby Group, complying with all the aquaculture industry regulations, such as NS9145 and ISO 9001 certification from Det Norske Veritas (DNV). We are specialists in the development of turnkey projects of aquaculture, from the conceptualization and design phase, to its implementation and start-up, and also in everything related to its maintenance. We offer tailor-made solutions for the design and development of OffShore facilities, focused on the implementation of Long Line and Fish Farming production systems. We have a technical office made up of experts in the sector who advise and provide support to the client in all project’s phases, analyzing all the elements of the installation, such as moorings, framework, cages, nets, beacons, etc... as well as their design, economic assessment, management of subsidies and grants for the execution of aquaculture operations and development of R&D projects. In addition, all assembly and manufacturing procedures go through our own exhaustive quality controls in order to offer maximum safety.

Name: CAP BOURBON
Description:
Cap Bourbon est une société réunionnaise créée en 1998, spécialisée dans la pêche à la légine.

> Avec ses filiales, notamment Océane Production acquise en 2018, l’entreprise met à disposition de tous les professionnels du monde de la mer locaux, nationaux et internationaux (pêcheurs, importateurs, négociants...) une offre multiservices complète : logistique, stockage, valorisation, découpe, conditionnement, opérations douanières…
La synergie Cap Bourbon/Océane Production permet aujourd’hui de mettre sa filière intégrée au service des produits de la mer surgelés en poursuivant le travail sur l'excellence et les produits d'exception, ainsi que la recherche constante en matière d'innovation. L'illustration de l’esprit entrepreneur des marins.

> Avec son palangrier congélateur, le Cap Kersaint, Cap Bourbon est un acteur régional majeur de la valorisation de la légine. Sa pêche sélective, respectant des normes strictes pour une pêche durable édictées par l'administration des TAAF, lui a permis d'obtenir le label MSC pour ses bonnes pratiques de pêche dans une région isolée, au climat subantarctique qui regorge d’un écosystème exceptionnel.

> A travers sa participation à la gestion de l'OSIRIS II pour le compte du GIE PLRH (GIE Protection Légine et Ressources Halieutiques), l’entreprise réunionnaise s'implique avec la DMSOI dans la préservation et la sécurisation des TAAF. Ce patrouilleur austral prend également part à des missions de contrôle de l’environnement marin ou de soutien scientifique.

Name: Observatorio Español de Acuicultura
Description:
Antecedentes

En el año 2001, la antigua Secretaria General de Pesca Marítima del Ministerio de Agricultura, Pesca y Alimentación, acomete la  realización del Libro Blanco de la Acuicultura en España, una de cuyas líneas estratégicas era la creación del Observatorio Español de Acuicultura (OESA), que debía crearse con el objetivo de fomentar la interrelación entre las distintas áreas del sector de la acuicultura.

Objetivos y actuaciones

La Fundación Observatorio Español de Acuicultura nace con objetivos renovados, así, tal y como se recoge en el capítulo segundo de sus Estatutos, el fin principal de la Fundación, es de carácter de investigación en el campo de la acuicultura (cultivo de peces, moluscos, crustáceos y otras especies de interés).

Patronato

El patronato de la Fundación, está integrado por las siguientes instituciones:

    * Secretaria General del Mar
    * Junta Nacional Asesora de Cultivos Marinos (JACUMAR)
    * Instituto Español de Oceanografía
    * Agencia Estatal Consejo Superior de Investigaciones Científicas
    * Fundación Española de Ciencia y Tecnología
    * Fundación Alfonso Martín Escudero

Name: Great Lakes Fishery Commission

The commission coordinates fisheries research, controls the invasive sea lamprey, and facilitates cooperative fishery management among the state, provincial, tribal, and federal management agencies.

Name: Centre for Fisheries Ecosystems Research (CFER)
Description:
CFER fulfills these roles within four primary areas:

1) Conducting Fisheries Ecosystem Science
CFER will add to the fisheries research capacity in Newfoundland and Labrador and will work with government agencies and others who share the goals of CFER, including various researchers at Memorial University.

2) Increasing Research Capacity
CFER's specialized researchers - finfish, pelagic, crustacean and fisheries acoustics science and management - will help gather more information and scientific support for ecosystem-based and precautionary approaches to fisheries management.
CFER provides opportunities for fisheries scientists to work in our province and allows research opportunities to more than 20 fisheries science graduate students annually.

3) Increasing Access to Inshore and Offshore Research Vessels
CFER has the RV Gecho II, its own inshore research vessel, and has charter access to the RV Celtic Explorer, a world-class offshore research vessel. These crafts enable major fisheries surveys and provide opportunities for research and training both within CFER and with academia and government.

4) Collaborating with National and International Fisheries Scientists
CFER collaborates with Canadian fisheries scientists and creates linkages and partnerships with researchers from around the world.

Name: Room 4 RAS
Description:
In January 2022, I started to work as a freelancer under the name Room 4 RAS, with the goal to help people make better decisions about RAS installations by using over 30 years of actual experience designing, installing and more importantly handling, operating and troubleshooting large scale RAS systems.

As specialists in design and installation of self-cleaning-low maintenance Moving Bed Biofilm Reactors (MBBR), our solutions aim to remove common problems in aquaculture such as:

• Settlement of particles due to unfiltered faeces or uneaten feed that must be avoided in any RAS system as they can become potential disease reservoirs

• Formation of hydrogen sulphide as consequence of anoxic sediment and its adverse effects as known causes of severe failures in important large scale RAS projects when working with seawater

• Blockages of biomedia in faulty hydraulics designs 

Our tailor-made solutions are based on:

• Combining different aeration systems with correct MBBR water inlet and water outlet hydraulics design in order to:

-	 Keep the biofilter in perfect conditions to sustain the development of beneficial bacteria as well as to remove the heterotrophic bacteria competition from the equation 

-	Promote chaotic biomedia movement from the very bottom to the surface to support keeping the MBBR tank floor and walls clean to help in avoiding potential areas of sedimentation

-	Avoid  accumulation and blockages of biomedia in certain areas of the MBBR ensuring a more even distribution of the material all over the tank and therefore better biofilter performance

• Implementing a MBBR bypass system that gives flexibility to carry out maintenance tasks or therapeutical treatments 


In addition to the above, we also offer consultancy due to our comprehensive RAS design know-how, with special insight on the system general hydraulics layout and calculations aiming to optimise and improve the system cleanliness and energy efficiency.

Contact us through LinkedIn if you would like to talk

Name: Nauterra Italia
Description:
Da sempre peschiamo, selezioniamo e prepariamo solo il miglior tonno: compatto, tenero, saporito.

Oggi siamo un'azienda moderna, focalizzata sull'innovazione e con un'ampia offerta di prodotti sul mercato.

Fondata nel 1951 a Grado (GO), la nostra è una marca storica del mercato alimentare italiano.

Nel 1993 siamo entrati a far parte del Gruppo Calvo, multinazionale spagnola con stabilimenti in tutto il mondo.

Siamo l’unica azienda conserviera in Italia ad avere una flotta di proprietà, costituita da 10 imbarcazioni di cui 6 pescherecci per la pesca del tonno in tutti gli oceani del mondo.

L’intero processo produttivo, quindi, è interamente sotto il controllo di Nostromo fin dal momento della pesca: la migliore garanzia per una qualità sempre costante e rigorosamente controllata.

Il sistema di tracciabilità di Nostromo permette di conoscere tutti i dati relativi all’origine del tonno contenuto nella lattina: specie, oceano e zona FAO di pesca, la barca che l’ha pescato, quando è stato pescato e che metodo di pesca è stato utilizzato, affinché si possa esercitare una decisione informata di consumo.

Name: Aquaculture ID
Description:
Aquaculture ID is a branch of Fleuren & Nooijen BV. The latter started in 1986 with a small hatchery for African catfish and has developed into a company designing and constructing aquaculture systems for a wide range of species. Using its extensive fish farming experience and knowledge, Fleuren & Nooijen is now a market leader helping people achieve their aquaculture goals.

Fleuren & Nooijen previously worked with both commercial fish farmers as well as academic researchers from universities and institutes. Both customer groups still do maintain fish in our systems, but each with different goals and specific needs. In order to improve our service to the separate customer groups, it was decided that specific business units for each group would be established. The incorporation of these units was in effect as of the 1st of May 2018.

COMMERCIAL AQUACULTURE
Aquaculture ID specifically focuses on commercial aquaculture. Aquaculture is our identity; we are African catfish fingerling farmers ourselves and we design and construct complete and innovative fish farms for a large number of fish species for our customers. Each project starts with an idea from the customer, and using our innovative designs, we endeavour to turn each aquaculture project into a successful one.

AQUATIC RESEARCH FACILITIES
Fleuren & Nooijen BV has a specific branch proactive in the design and construction of complete aquatic research facilities. The branch can be reached through www.zebcare.nl.

Name: NORAY Seafood
Description:
Inspirados por la excelente calidad de nuestros langostinos, desde Noray trabajamos día a día para criar, de forma totalmente natural y sostenible, el mejor langostino sin sulfitos en nuestra granja bajo techo situada en plena meseta vallisoletana.

Desde nuestra puesta en marcha en 2008, nos esforzamos por producir un langostino Premium, ultra-fresco, sostenible y con origen 100% en España. El resultado es un producto de alto valor gastronómico. Por su excelente sabor y calidad, el langostino de Noray recibió la máxima puntuación (3 estrellas) en los prestigiosos Superior Taste Awards 2022 que el International Taste Institute celebra en Bélgica de la mano de los mejores chefs.

Tras años de inversión en I+D, nos hemos convertido en un referente de acuicultura sostenible en Europa. Así, nuestro esfuerzo por la sostenibilidad se ha visto premiado con la certificación ASC, convirtiéndonos en la primera granja de langostinos bajo techo en obtenerla.

Inspired by the excellent quality of our shrimp, at Noray we work every day to raise, in a totally natural and sustainable way, the best shrimp without sulphites in our indoor farm located in the heart of the Valladolid plateau.

Since our launch in 2008, we strive to offer a Premium ultra-fresh, sustainable shrimp whose breeding takes place 100% in Spain. The result is a product of high gastronomic value. For its excellent taste and quality, Noray shrimp was awarded with the highest possible score (3 stars) in the prestigious Superior Taste Award 2022 celebrated in Belgium by the International Taste Institute, with the help of the best chefs.

Following years of investment in R&D, we can proudly say that we are a benchmark for sustainable aquaculture in Europe. Therefore, our effort towards sustainability was recognized with ASC certification, our effort towards sustainability was recognised with the ASC certification, making us the first indoor shrimps farm to obtain this certification.

The sustainable local production model guarantees the freshest and healthiest salmon available on the market grown close to the consumer in an environmentally friendly manner that protects the ecosystem and reduces the carbon footprint.
Swiss Blue Salmon represents the socially responsible choice for consumer health, the environment and the economy.

From the #sea to your #plate


For 20 years in the Indian Ocean and Madagascar, we have gradually set up an integrated sector with 5 production sites on the red island. This is based in particular on an artisanal fishing model, guaranteed day fishing and adjusted to seasonal changes. This organization allows us to offer you a qualitative range of seafood from Madagascar.


Our products are developed in accordance with the challenges of resource sustainability and industry viability. We are therefore working as closely as possible to villages, NGOs such as Blue Venture.org and the authorities to ensure respect for the environment and the people who live in it.


We serve the whole world by container and our logistical organization allows us to meet your needs throughout the year, with deliveries to France and Europe, by pallet (Warehouses in Le Havre and Marseille).

Name: Ashored Innovations
Description:
Ashored Innovations uses hardware and software to increase efficiencies for fishermen, while also protecting at-risk marine habitats.

Ashored retrofits existing lobster and crab traps with underwater buoys that can be propelled to the surface using acoustic release technology. Trap owners will be able to anchor their boats, select their proprietary frequency, and activate the buoy’s release to the surface. Ashored’s buoys use artificial intelligence to collect data and make recommendations, and use geo-tracking to locate lost traps.

Name: Comité Régional des Pêches Maritimes et des Élevages Marins de Nouvelle-Aquitaine (CRPMEM)
Description:
Le CRPMEM Nouvelle-Aquitaine est la structure professionnelle centrale du domaine de la pêche maritime en région Nouvelle-Aquitaine. 

Il s'agit de l'un des 12 comités régionaux de France qui assurent la défense des intérêts généraux de la filière et des professionnels du secteur, pêcheurs, et éleveurs marins.

Name: Grupo ConstAmar
Description:
Empresas vinculantes comprometidas a entender el sector camaronero para servirle. 
En ConstAmar nuestra prioridad es que el sector Camaronero sea sostenible y sustentable en el tiempo.

Name: Gel-Pêche
Description:
Gel-Pêche is the main importer of tropical seafood products (shrimps, fish, crabs, octopus...) 

Specialized in Madagascar shrimps since its beginning, Gel-Pêche has set up, with the Réfrigépêche group, a totally integrated chain which relies, on board trawlers as well as on land, on strict quality control procedures. This allows us to offer a complete range of seafood products from Madagascar. With this experience, Gel-Pêche travels the world in search of the best products and develops partnerships with recognized suppliers in Asia, Africa and South America.

Location: Saint Aignan de Grand Lieu , Pays de la Loire

Name: North Pacific Seafoods, Inc.
Description: North Pacific Seafoods, Inc. owns and operates five shore-based seafood processing plants strategically located in key seafood harvesting regions of Alaska - Kodiak, Sitka, Twin Hills and Naknek (two plants).  Our diverse locations enable us to process a wide range of Alaska seafood species. North Pacific Seafoods’ plants produce seafood products for the domestic and international markets.  We pride ourselves on offering high quality, sustainable seafood that is always traceable to the source.

Name: SEAFOOD Co., Ltd.
Description:
SEAFOOD CO., LTD. has maintained a constant focus on becoming the most trusted sources of seafood knowledge and innovation in the seafood industry for the past 20 years. Our mission is to provide good quality and selection of seafood.

We are now preparing to ascend to the next level as a global company responsible for high energy seafood, direct processing, and world wide distribution service.  

Our core values are based on
- Producing seafood products with Korea's hallmark of advanced science
- Producing sustainable seafood with our next generation in mind
- Profitability for the sake of social responsibility 
Such are the corporate philosophies that our firm subscribes to as it takes its place as a leader among the world’s top food service companies.

Name: Waterdance Ltd
Description:
Waterdance is a leading UK South West fishing company. Established in 1956 the company is one of the Greendale group of family businesses. Waterdance core business is in the fish catching sector; the company owns and directly operates 23 fishing vessels from two core ports; Brixham and Newlyn. Waterdance also owns significant UK fish quota which its own boats utilise and which is also traded domestically and internationally.  
The Waterdance fleet comprises Beam Trawlers, Netters, Potters and Scallopers. The beam trawler fleet is homeported to Brixham in Devon, the netters to Newlyn in Cornwall. The potters operate from Exmouth and Salcombe respectively. The waters of the western channel provide clean deep waters and strong currents which provide the perfect environment for world class seafood. Sustainable fishing methods ensure that these stocks remain intact. 
Major specifies of fish caught include; Sole, Plaice, Hake, Cuttlefish and Pollack. The potters harvest Whelks, Crab and Lobsters. The majority of this fish is sold through local fish markets but the Waterdance fleet also supplies the group farm shop, the Greendale Farm shop (near Exeter) and utilises the Greendale haulage fleet to supply fish to Europe on a regular basis.      
Waterdance fishing vessels are all flagged to the UK ; where the maritime safety standards are some of the highest in the world.  In addition to the existing fleet, Waterdance has two exciting new builds under construction; a 35 metre Beam trawler – GEORGINA OF LADRAM, and a 15 metre Potter – NICHOLA OF LADRAM.   
Waterdance has over 120 ocean going personnel and a shore network of staff and wider employment footprint  that lands, processes, distributes and sells seafood, supporting a strong South West local economy.     
The company has a strategy of investing locally, continually modernising the fleet, improving fish quality, and striving to get the best fish to customers.

Name: Nergård Group
Description:
Nergård har tilgang til førsteklasses råstoff fra noen av verdens best forvaltede fiskebestander, både fra kystflåten og havgående flåte, og fra egne trålere. Vi produserer varer basert på råstoff fra hvitfisk som torsk, hyse og sei, samt blåkveite og uer, og en rekke andre hvitfiskarter som kvantumsmessig er beskjedne. Vi leverer ferskfisk, saltfisk og tørrfisk. Torsk, sei, hyse og blåkveite leveres også sløyd og fryst for videreforedling. 

Nergård er Nord-Norges største leverandør av produkter av pelagiske arter, som sild, lodde og makrell. Vi har to moderne MSC-sertifiserte fabrikker som produserer rundfryste produkter og filetprodukter. Lodderogn er også i produktporteføljen.

Name: Atlantic Shrimpers Limited
Description:
Building Relationships with Great Taste !!!

Atlantic Shrimpers Ltd is a producer & exporter of the premium PRIM7*STARS brand of sea frozen products from Nigeria. ASL is largest fishing company in Nigeria and perhaps in West Africa, having a fleet of over 70 fishing trawlers.

Prim7Stars is a seafood brand known for a high quality range of wild caught products. Approx 3.500 metric tons of tropical shrimps are caught annually & distributed worldwide by Primstar B.V.– The Netherlands.

ASL has been active in shrimp trawling since the 1990s; expanding its fleet from 25 to 70 trawlers now. In 2001 ASL obtained its own Dry Dock that facilitates in-house maintenance of the whole vessel. ASL also has in-house lab, fuel bunkers, workshops & cold storage facilities. The intrinsic value of the Prim7Stars brand is based on consistency - both Quantity & Quality.

FRIENDS OF THE SEA
Black Tiger Prawns(Peneaus monodon) from FAO area 34 caught by ASL are in conformity with Friend of Sea Criteria for Sustainable Fisheries.

THE FISH SHOP
We are committed to continuously supply frozen Shrimp, Fish and Value added products to the Nigerian market thorough our retail outlets THE FISH SHOP, which makes the country less dependent on imported fish for proteins.

RETAIL OUTLETS: 
"The Fish Shop" are located strategically across Lagos, Ibadan & Abuja. Current outlets are at ABUJA, IBADAN, APAPA, FESTAC, IKEJA, KIRIKIRI, LEKKI, SURULERE & VICTORIA ISLAND. More to come

Shrimps, the healthy choice !!

Name: Sitka Seafood Market
Description:
Sitka Seafood Market is your premier destination for seasonally-rotating, responsibly harvested seafood. We offer a diverse selection that includes premium varieties such as salmon, white fish, and shellfish, ensuring an incredible culinary experience.

Our Commitment:
Sustainability and traceability are not just principles; they are the foundation of our mission. We take pride in delivering seafood that is sourced with meticulous care, prioritizing the health of marine ecosystems and supporting practices that align with a responsible approach to fishing. We take great joy in sharing where your seafood came from and supporting the local fishermen who are catching your fish.

What We Offer:

Seasonal Delights: 
Explore the rich flavors of the seasons with our carefully curated seafood varieties, each offering a unique taste.

Direct to Consumer: 
Elevate your home cooking with the finest seafood delivered straight to your doorstep. Experience a quality and taste that cannot be found at your local supermarket. 

Wholesale Opportunities: 
We cater to the needs of caterers and private chefs by providing top-tier seafood in bulk. Elevate your culinary creations with the premium quality of Sitka Seafood Market.

Join Us in the Culinary Adventure:
Whether you're a seafood enthusiast or a culinary professional, our commitment to quality ensures an unparalleled dining experience.

Experience the Difference:
Sitka Seafood Market is not just a marketplace; it's a commitment to a healthier, more sustainable future. Dive into the world of Sitka Seafood Market and discover the true essence of ocean-to-table excellence.

Description:
provides top-of-the-line quality products and service.

Name: COFREPECHE
Description:
COFREPECHE, société anonyme à conseil d'administration, a été créée en 1980 par des professionnels du monde maritime. En 1994, COFREPECHE a repris France Aquaculture, ancienne filiale de l'Ifremer, spécialisée dans les activités aquacoles.
Fort de plus de 30 ans d’expérience, notre bureau d’études a développé un ensemble unique de savoir-faire techniques, économiques et financiers dans les secteurs de l’halieutique, de l'aquaculture et de la gestion intégrée des zones côtières. Nos experts interviennent à l’échelle internationale, aussi bien sur des études et audits que sur des missions d’ingénierie et d’assistance technique. 
Depuis sa création, COFREPECHE a ainsi réalisé plus de 750 projets dans 70 pays à travers l'Europe, l'Afrique, l'Amérique du Sud, l'Asie, le Moyen-Orient, l’Océan Pacifique et l’Océan Indien, auprès de clients privés et publics  dont des instances de développement international (tel que l'Agence Française de Développement, l'Union Européenne, la Banque Mondiale, les Nations Unies, le Millenium Challenge Corporation).

COFREPECHE, limited company with a management board, was created in 1983 by professional of the maritime sector. In 1994, COFREPECHE acquired France Aquaculture, former subsidiary of Ifremer specialised in aquaculture activities.
With 35 years of experience, COFREPECHE has developed a technical, economic and financial know-how in the fisheries and aquaculture sector and the integrated coastal zone management. Our experts work all around the world providing advices, technical assistance and carrying out studies and audits.
COFREPECHE has implemented over 750 projects worldwide in more than 70 countries across Europe, the American continent, Asia, Africa, the Middle East, the Pacific Ocean and the Indian Ocean, for private and public clients including international development organizations (such as the European Union, the World Bank, the United Nation, the French Development Agency, the Millenium Challenge Corporation).

Name: PT. Sanjaya Internasional Fishery
Description: PT Sanjaya Internasional Fishery was established in 2009 in Muara Angke, North Jakarta. With his experience, Mr. Hongpeng, the founder of our company, pioneered business opportunities with only a facility on the road Pelelangan Baru No.6 in its initial establishment, PT Sanjaya Internasional Fishery was only engaged in the export of fresh fish packed in styrofoam boxes which were then sent via air cargo to neighboring countries I such as Malaysia and Singapore.

Name: Fisheries Innovation & Sustainability
Description:
Fisheries Innovation & Sustainability is a coalition of experts driving strategic innovation for a prosperous and sustainable UK seafood industry. 

Our remit is to facilitate, coordinate and leverage investment for innovation in UK seafood.


Who we are
FIS is a coalition of experts driving strategic innovation for a prosperous and sustainable UK seafood industry.

What we do
We champion innovation projects which will make the greatest positive change for the UK seafood industry and the people driving it. Since 2014, we have commissioned multidisciplinary research projects worth more than £2m to support prosperous and sustainable fisheries.

Why we do it
FIS believes that innovation is the key to releasing untapped potential for greater prosperity and sustainability in the UK seafood industry.

How we do it
FIS creates a pre-competitive environment for collaboration, bringing seafood experts, government and scientists together to champion innovation in the UK seafood industry. FIS connects the seafood supply chain around shared opportunities for innovation to add value to UK businesses and communities. Our projects have a clear pathway to impact and are designed by experts operating on a pre-competitive and non-profit basis.

Name: Fishing Headquarters
Description:
Fishing Headquarters is a charter fishing company based out of Fort Lauderdale.  We run daily deep sea fishing trips open to the public on both our large party fishing boats and our sportfishing "6 pack" charter boats.  We take our clients out to the fishing grounds offshore Ft Lauderdale on guided trips with Ft Lauderdale's most experienced and charismatic crews.  Our boats can accommodate individuals and small groups, as well as very large parties of up to 150 people.  We are conveniently located on the main beach of Fort Lauderdale, at the intersection of Las Olas Blvd and southbound A1A.  

All of our charters include all necessary bait, tackle, licenses, rods/reels, coolers with ice and expert help from courteous crewmembers.  We also offer catering upon request.  No experience is necessary for any of our passengers, crew are always available to assist with everything.  

Our vessels include the Catch My Drift, an 85ft Gulfcraft licensed to carry up to 130 passengers, the only air conditioned party fishing boat in Ft Lauderdale.  The Mary B III, a 55ft party fishing boat, licensed to carry a maximum of 53 passengers and specially optimized for sportfishing with groups of 7-15 passengers.  We also operate a fleet of "6-pack" sportfishing boats, ranging in size from 42-50ft in length, which run daily deep sea fishing charters for groups of 6 of less passengers.  With fishing grounds only 20 minutes from our docks, Fishing Headquarters is a very convenient and safe way to enjoy a half day of fishing.  Most of our charters are for half day trips, but full day and even overnight charters are available.  Multiple boat sportfishing charters can be arranged for large company events and we can even arrange and coordinate a tournament for these multiple boat charters.

In addition to fishing trips, we also offer Intracoastal Cruises, Cocktail Cruising, Document Signing at Sea and even Funerals at Sea.

Name: ITECHMER_2025
Description: The first trade fair for fishing industry in France, Itechmer is a meeting of professionals working in sectors from ship design to final processing of seafood products, including capture, security, logistics, marketing, etc. A MEETING FOR DECISION-MAKERS Itechmer is not just an Exhibition where you need to be. It is above all a place for business, promotion and communication.Like the 2017 edition, Itechmer is the only global Exhibition devoted to fishing industry in France for 3 major reasons: representativeness of its exhibitors, qualified visitors and recognised professional organisation.

Name: EFFOP
Description:
On a global level, Europe is producing 10% of the world's fishmeal and 20% of the world's fish oil.
Fishmeal and fish oil are important marine ingredients with an increasing global demand. Used for aquaculture, human consumption and animal feeds, the products bring the important omega-3 fatty acids EPA and DHA in to the human food chain via farmed fish and fish oil supplements.

The fishmeal and fish oil industry in Europe produces 530,000  tonnes of fishmeal and 160,000 tonnes of fish oil annually and the total value of production is approximately 1 billion €/year. Exports go to a large variety of countries.
Production is based on landings of small, oily, short-lived species such as blue whiting, capelin, sand eel, Norway pout and sprat as well as by-products (trimmings) from the consumption fish processing sector. 

The production sites of the European fishmeal and fish oil industry are placed in more remote coastal areas and the sector contributes to growth and development in coastal communities across Europe.

Production is based on a sustainable exploitation of resources and the sector meets the international standards and certifications applying to raw materials, production and traceability. 

European Fishmeal represents producers from EU as well as non-EU countries. The member countries are Denmark, the Faroe Islands, Germany, Iceland, Ireland, Norway and the United Kingdom.

European Fishmeal and Fish Oil Producers was called EUfishmeal until March 2020.

Owned by the Oceana Group, Daybrook is involved in the harvesting and processing of the Gulf menhaden species, and in the marketing and selling of derived fishmeal and fish oil products, including Prime, Natural (PetFood) and SuperPrime grades of fishmeal.

Name: Austevoll Seafood ASA
Description: Austevoll Seafood ASA (AUSS), a publically traded company on the Oslo Stock Exchange, is a globally integrated pelagic fishery and seafood specialist. Headquartered in Austevoll, Norway, our investment strategy is based on conviction, industrial insight and long-term investments in seafood companies globally, which add sustainable value to our business activities.

Name: Réunimer
Description: En l’espace d’une vingtaine d’années, REUNIMER est parvenu à se structurer autour d’activités s’étendant de la pêche jusqu’à la vente de produits de la mer, se positionnant comme le seul groupe local présent sur l’ensemble de la chaîne de valeur.

Name: IMCS Network
Description:
The IMCS Network is a global community bringing together national fisheries agencies and regional fisheries organisations to strengthen fisheries monitoring, control and surveillance (MCS), compliance and enforcement.

Founded in 2001, the Network was born from a simple truth: the professionals on the frontlines of fisheries MCS need to be connected, supported and empowered.

Today, we connect 81 national agencies and regional organisations, alongside partners from IGOs, NGOs and academia. Together, we are building a globally connected and capable fisheries MCS community — one that works collectively to achieve and sustain effective compliance in support of marine conservation and fisheries management.

Our work focuses on promoting communication, collaboration, coordination and capacity development — the foundations of lasting impact.

Name: CNFTPA
Description: Résultat d’une mutation de l’ex Ecole des Agents Techniques de l’Océanographie et des Pêches Maritimes de Dakar/Thiaroye(EATOPM), le CNFTPA a su constamment adapter son offre de formation aux besoins définis par l’Etat, les secteurs parapublic et privé au niveau national et international. Depuis quelques années, au vu de l’évolution du secteur, le centre a notamment élargi ses activités de formation à la pêche continentale et à l’aquaculture. Une réforme qui participe parfaitement des nouvelles orientations assignées au secteur par Monsieur le président de la république, à travers le Plan Sénégal Emergent(PSE). Fort de ses cinquante années d’expérience, l’ambition du CNFTPA est de former des acteurs capables de contribuer efficacement au développement durable du secteur de la Pêche et de l’aquaculture. Expérience assurément exaltante que nous comptons perpétuer avec la contribution de chacun.

We're reshaping the future of the wild salmon industry. We recognize the challenges it faces, from aging technology to volatile prices. It’s time for transformation.

We redefine processing, making wild seafood more profitable while enhancing fisheries’ quality and value. This means economically viable harvesting, thriving communities, and strong fisheries.

We promise to increase the value of wild fish with a process that is more efficient, profitable, and fair. Our solution ensures a positive, long-term impact on Alaska’s wild salmon industry.

Name: LetSea AS
Description:
LetSea er Norges største forsøk- og forskningssenter for havbruk, lokalisert i Dønna kommune på Helgeland. Navnet avspeiler hovedoppgaven; å se etter nye muligheter innenfor havbruk. Bedriften har over 220 forsøksenheter på seks ulike lokasjoner. 

LetSea dekker hele spekteret av næringens behov med alle nødvendige fasiliteter som forsøks- og fullskala merder i sjø, RAS-anlegg, kar på land og våtlab. 

Kundene er både norske og internasjonale oppdrettere, forskningsinstitusjoner, fôrprodusenter og utstyrsleverandører. LetSea har i dag 40 ansatte og planlegger for videre vekst og et bredere engasjement, bl.a. i bedrifter som utvikler ny teknologi og metodikk for havbruksnæringen. Oppdragene favner hele spekteret av forsøk og forskning med et industrielt, praktisk utgangspunkt og helhetlige konsepter for lønnsom og bærekraftig produksjon, bedre fiskehelse og redusert miljøavtrykk.

LetSea er kåret til årets norske Gaselle-bedrift 2017. Prisen ble utdelt av Kronprins Haakon under den nasjonale konferansen ved Christiania Theater i Oslo. LetSea har tidligere vunnet Gaselle-prisen for Nordland to år på rad. 

Økonomi: 
- Omsetningen har økt fra 20 til 323 millioner i løpet av siste fire år

For tiden arbeider LetSea med disse utfordringene for havbruksnæringen:

Name: Akuvatur Su Ürünleri A.Ş.
Description:
Akuvatur 1990 yılında Adana’da 300 hektar alan üzerinde Levrek ve Çipura balığı yavrusu üretim tesisleri ile faaliyetlerine başlamıştır.

Bugün 5 ayrı bölgedeki çağdaş entegre üretim tesisleri, yeni türlerin yetiştiriciliğiyle ilgili Ar-Ge çalışmaları ve sadece kendisinin başarabildiği üretim teknikleri neticesinde nadide Akdeniz Balıklarını, zengin ürün çeşitliliği ve “doğal lezzeti” ile Akdeniz mutfağına tekrar kazandırmaktadır.

Ana faaliyet alanları “Çipura ve Levrek Yavru Balık üretimi” ve “Porsiyonluk Akdeniz Balıkları Üretimi” olan Akuvatur; Yurt içinde ve yurt dışındaki güçlü satış ve dağıtım ağı ile yaygın satış noktalarında yerini almaktadır.

ISO 9001, ISO14001, HACCP uygulamaları ile kalite anlayışı açısından sektördeki öncü görevini sürdüren Akuvatur; “Takip Edilebilirlik” ve “Sürdürülebilirlik” uygulamalarıyla da üretimin çevre ve ürün güvenliği ile olan tüm süreçlerini kontrol altında tutmaktadır.

Akuvatur, mesleğinde uzman ve profesyonel çalışanlarıyla Akdeniz'in farklı türlerinin aynı anda üretimini gerçekleştirebilen Avrupa’daki tek firma olma özelliğiyle dünyada hızla gelişen kültür balıkçılığı sektöründe çalışmalarını sürdürmektedir.

Name: Bluewild
Description: Bluewild is a Norwegian fishing company counting 60 employees. We operates the freezing-trawler "Langenes" and the combined danish seine and purse seien fishing vessel "Genesis".

Name: Sea Star Seafood
Description:
Sea Star Seafood Corporation was established in 1983 with the mission of becoming the “premier provider of case ready seafood solutions to retail customers nationally”. When the company was founded, the retail seafood industry consisted primarily of fresh fish counters and frozen block fish offerings. Recognizing our customers' need for self-service frozen seafood, Sea Star initiated the development of a product line and production capability that today has made us an industry leader and the major USA producer of case ready frozen seafood products. 

Sea Star Seafood operates a state-of-the-art, HACCP-approved, USDC-certified facility with the capability to process, package and distribute frozen fish, shellfish, and value-added seafood in case ready packages that include Tray Pack Vacuum (TPV), Tray Pack Refresh, Individual Vacuum Packed (IVP), Individually Quick Frozen (IQF), and bulk. Our vacuum packaging and tray pack capabilities keep frozen fish fresher throughout the distribution process. Some of the many species we offer includes: salmon, swordfish, halibut, tilapia, haddock, flounder, cod, snapper, sea scallops, jonah crab and lobster tails.

We endeavor to establish long-term business partnerships that thoughtfully combine a high quality product mix with our merchandising and promotional expertise to maximize sales and category performance. For more information, including product details, recipe ideas and more, please visit our web site at www.SeaStarCorp.com.

Name: FENIP | Moroccan Federation of Industries for the Processing and Valorization of Fishery Products
Description: The National Federation of Industries for the Processing and Valorization of Fishery Products (FENIP) is a professional organization created on November 21, 1996. It succeeds the Federation of Canning Industries of Morocco "FICOMA" created in 1948, bringing together 7 professional associations that encompass +300 companies processing and valorizing seafood products operating in the six branches of activity of the sector: -The semi-preserved fish industry: represented by AMASCOP -The fishmeal and oil industry: represented by ANAFAP -The freezing of seafood products on land: represented by ANICOM -The packaging and export of fresh fish: represented by APROMER -All the seafood processing industries in Agadir represented by FIPROMER -The fish canning industry: represented by UNICOP -The seaweed processing industry ( AGAR AGAR ) associate member. A sectoral federation member of the CGEM, the FENIP's main purpose is: -To represent and defend the interests of its members, -To study measures to develop the fishery product processing industries and improve the conditions of production, distribution and sale of products, -To provide its members with any assistance likely to promote and develop their activities. #Together, let's contribute to the local and international influence of the fisheries sector specific to the Kingdom of Morocco.

Name: Aquaculture Centre of Excellence Pte Ltd
Description:
ACE’s patented and innovative “Eco-Ark” is a floating closed containment farm. ACE reinvents a game-changing approach to meeting the increasing demand for food fish sustainably and ethically.  ACE’s “Eco-Ark” filters and contains microplastic contamination, red-algae and other particulates from the seawater, thereby ensuring that the fishes are reared in a healthy environment, making them nutritious and safe for consumption.  It applies technology to precision aquaculture, state-of–art post-harvest processing, fish health monitoring and management and fish harvest traceability to provide a high level of assurance to consumers for fresh, tasty, and nutritious food fish.

Besides food fish, ACE is also concerned with marine and environmental stewardship. It aims to address the growing concern of  food security, overfishing, and carbon emissions from fishing boats and transportation logistics.  Its  Eco-Ark filtration system removes fish waste before releasing it back to the ocean, preventing pollution. It generates energy from the Solar rooftop panel of the fish farm and transports directly from the farm to customers.  In all, ACE’s “Eco-Ark” Eco-Farming Establishment conserves the ocean for generations ahead.

Name: Cook Inlet Aquaculture Association
Description:
The Cook Inlet Aquaculture Association was incorporated in 1976 to provide the Cook Inlet drainage with an organized, scientifically-respected community, responsible for the protection of self-perpetuating salmon stocks and the rehabilitation of salmon stocks and habitat.

These goals set forth by the board of directors are accomplished by maximizing the value of Cook Inlet’s common property salmon resource through the use of science, education, and technology.

Name: O'Hara Corporation
Description:
For over 140 years, O’Hara Corporation has withstood the test of time operating fishing vessels across both the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans. Francis J. O’Hara began building his sailing fleet in the 1880s starting in Boston, Massachusetts; after five generations, the process continues. From setting a seine net off the Coast of Maine for herring, participating in the scallop fishery out of New Bedford, Mass, fish processing operations in China, operating factory processing boats in the Pacific Northwest, and even a marina in Maine, O’Hara has diversified into a multinational family business.

The heart of O’Hara Corporation surrounds our catcher processor vessels fishing in the Bering Sea, Aleutian Islands, and Gulf of Alaska. Our vessels catch flounder, Atka mackerel, Pacific Ocean perch, cod, pollock, among others. With a current home port of Seattle, Washington, the Araho (194’/59m), Alaska Spirit (221’/69m), Constellation (165’/50m), Defender (124’/38m) and her sister ship Enterprise (124.9’/38m) catch, process, and freeze their catch at sea. Our flagship vessel, Araho, is the newest addition to the Alaska groundfish fleet. Our five fishing vessels operate in one of the most sustainable fishing industries in the world. O’Hara Corporation will continue to strive for the best quality groundfish product we can offer our worldwide customers.

Maine is still the roots for the O’Hara Corporation. In Rockland, we have diversified into a full-service marina, operating a 75-Ton Marine lift, the largest inside storage complex in the state, a boat hauling operation, and year round marine service. Our Rockland Maine facility, where the main office and marina is located, has state of the art office space for rent along the waterfront to tie in with the growth of Main Street’s galleries, restaurants, and shops.

Name: Scoot Science
Description:
We make oceans manageable, insurable and investable.
Scoot Science is the ocean company.

What's happening beneath the surface doesn't have to be a mystery. Scoot Science fills in the data gaps, and then uses a combination of traditional numerical ocean models and predictive artificial intelligence to deliver accurate underwater weather.

What can Scoot Science do for your marine operations?

* Visualize - Wake up each day to the headlines of your underwater weather and a complete picture of the local sub-surface conditions
*Analyze - Know the environmental threats to your marine operations before the impacts materialize
* Empower - Make informed logistics and operations decisions, protect your marine assets and strengthen your bottom line

Name: France Terre de Pêches
Description:
En France, l’industrie de la pêche est singulière par la variété des pratiques de pêche, des espèces capturées et des acteurs qui la composent. Reflet de cette diversité, France Terre de Pêches est un regroupement de plus de 1000 entreprises de pêche, qui emploient plus de 5500 marins-pêcheurs en mer, et nourrissent plus de 10 millions de Français. Formé en 2024 à l’initiative d’organisations de producteurs, France Terre de Pêches entend partager avec les Français les engagements et réalités des pêcheurs, et porter la voix du terrain dans le débat public. 

Notre action repose sur 3 convictions : 
1. Les entreprises de pêche en France proposent des produits de qualité, pêchés par des professionnels passionnés par la mer et respectueux de l’environnement,
2. Les entreprises de pêche en France agissent pour la souveraineté alimentaire et continueront de le faire demain,
3. La filière pêche en France repose sur des métiers et des savoir-faire au cœur de la protection des écosystèmes marins et côtiers, dont les prochaines générations doivent s’emparer.

Notre volonté est de présenter nos métiers et d’exposer la réalité de notre filière, de son fonctionnement, de ses pratiques et de ses acteurs. Nous voulons expliquer et faire connaître les enjeux économiques, environnementaux et humains qui entourent la pêche aujourd’hui, et que nous tentons d’adresser pour garantir l’existence d’une pêche durable, en mesure d’assurer sa mission essentielle : nourrir les populations.

 Ce projet est co-financé par l'Union Européenne.
