Name: Fogo Island Co-operative Society Ltd.
Description:
Anchored in the cold waters of the North Atlantic Ocean, off Newfoundland's rugged North East Coast, the Fogo Island Co-operative Society Limited remains committed to providing a quality living to its fisher and processor membership. In 1967, the residents of Fogo Island had to make a life-altering decision. Leave our beloved island home and resettle on the mainland of Newfoundland and Labrador, or stay and find a way to develop a long-term, sustainable community. We stayed and turned to what we knew best for hundreds of years - the· sea and its renewable natural resources. 

Following a process of community self-discovery, known worldwide as the" Fogo Process", our fishers formed the Fogo Island Co-operative Society Ltd., a community-based enterprise on which we built the economy of our island. We built more efficient, larger fishing vessels; we reactivated processing facilities abandoned by private enterprise and we invested in new process technology and quality systems; we invested in infrastructure to support our primary industry, including our own product development laboratory, fabrication facility, and a marine services center; and provided opportunities for members to receive training, special skills development and continuous quality assurance initiatives. We promoted our Co-operative model, our quality seafood and focused on the development of international markets. 

The Fogo Island Co-op has not only survived through some very turbulent times in our fishery, it has thrived for over 45 years. We have remained resilient and are focused on future opportunities for fishers, employees, and communities. We are strong and we are re-inventing ourselves again.

Name: It'll Never Work
Description:
"It'll Never Work" is a documentary following the build of the UK’s first fully electric fishing boat, currently being screened in cinemas across Scotland as well as available to stream online at https://itllneverwork․boats

The film captures some fascinating local characters and documents the highs and lows of converting a diesel fishing boat.

The star of the show is Hans Unkles. He launched Lorna Jane, the first boat in the UK fishing fleet to be powered entirely by solar panels, this summer.

But the star behind the scenes, or rather, behind the camera, is Joe Osborn. The 23-year-old film maker lives next door to Hans at Carsaig, ideally placed to follow the year-long design and build of the boat from the garden workshop. Joe studied contemporary filmmaking at UHI in Inverness. 

He said: “Hans invited me over to the shed and suggested I document the building of his electric fishing boat. I’m glad he did. This is my first documentary. I’ve made over 20 short films, mostly sci-fi and horror.” 

“I’m a fisherman too. I’ve been out on the boat filming. Most fishing boats have this level of vibration and noise. On this boat, there is no noise, but you are still going. It is amazing.”

Speaking about the first screening, he said: “Seeing everyone there was brilliant. It is quite a small community here and the level of support was so good. People have said it is very watchable. Regardless of whether you are interested in the techy side, the environmental side, the fishing side, it seems to appeal to all walks of life."

Name: Lake Victoria Fisheries Organization (LVFO)
Description:
The Lake Victoria Fisheries Organization (LVFO) is a specialized institution of the East African Community (EAC) whose mandate is to coordinate the management and development of fisheries and aquaculture resources in the EAC region.

A Conference of Plenipotentiaries on the Establishment of the Lake Victoria Fisheries Organization, which was held in Kisumu, Kenya, on 30 June 1994, adopted the Convention for the Establishment of the Lake Victoria Fisheries Organization (LVFO). The Convention was registered with the Secretariat of the United Nations on 30 July 1996 under registration number 32987.

At its 9th Session held in Nairobi, Kenya, on 29 January 2016, the Council of Ministers of the LVFO adopted amendments to the Convention with a view to, inter alia, opening membership to all Partner States of the East African Community, and extending the competence of the LVFO to the fisheries and aquaculture resources of the East African Community water bodies.  The amendments entered into force thirty days after their adoption, that is, on 28 February 2016 and the Republic of Burundi officially  acceded to the convention on 4th October 2017.

Name: Marine Garden
Description:
Marine. The Future.

Marine Garden is a prototype, the first of its kind designed to demonstrate how to create a more sustainable, environmentally-friendly prawn farming enterprise using recirculating aquaculture systems that can be implemented in non-coastal settings.

Located more than 100 km from the coast in regional Esk, founder Martin Zhang has utilised his extensive experience and knowledge of traditional aquatic filtration systems to develop a unique venture that has the potential to produce higher yield prawn harvests in a more sustainable setting. 

As part of this innovative enterprise, Marine Garden have proposed a model that can effectively deal with the waste product from such a traditional farming set up. Through the specialised filtration system, they have the ability to use the minimal waste as a nutrient-dense fertiliser (ideal for crop farming) while the remaining filtered ocean water can then be fed back into the aquaculture process to create an efficient recirculating system.

Name: Association Nationale des Organisations de Producteurs
Description:
L'ANOP est une fédération de 7 organisations de producteurs de la pêche maritime françaises. Par la diversité de ses adhérents, nous sommes le reflet vibrant de la richesse et de la diversité de la pêche française, englobant des pêcheurs côtiers aux pêcheurs hauturiers, des petites embarcations aux grands navires. Nous sommes présents sur toutes les façades, représentons tous les métiers, tous les engins et toutes les tailles de navires.

 Nos valeurs fondamentales 

Nous sommes profondément engagés envers la durabilité et la préservation des ressources marines. Notre mission est de promouvoir une pêche responsable qui assure la pérennité des métiers tout en respectant l'écosystème marin.

La pêche est une véritable passion qui unit des individus venant de tous horizons. Elle anime et fait vivre des territoires entiers, et elle permet de nourrir des millions de français. Les pêcheurs sont les agriculteurs en mer. 

Ensemble, nous formons un tissu riche et coloré, tissé de traditions, de cultures et de savoirs uniques. C'est cette diversité qui fait la force et la beauté de notre secteur. C'est cette diversité qui nous permet d'innover, de nous adapter aux défis du présent et de construire un avenir durable pour nos océans et nos communautés de pêcheurs.

 Déconstruire les mythes 

Nous sommes conscients des défis auxquels notre secteur est confronté. Notre secteur essuie régulièrement de nombreuses critiques, qui naissent d’une lecture simplifiée et erronée de nos activités et de notre réalité. Nous prenons ces préoccupations au sérieux et nous nous engageons à promouvoir une pêche responsable tout en contribuant au bien-être de nos communautés. Il en va là de la survie de nos métiers et nos pêcheurs ont à cœur de préserver la ressource qui les fait vivre.

Name: new madina shrimp hatchery
Description:
During the larval stages penaeid shrimp show filter-feeding behaviour and microalgae are their most important dietary source.

Larval life of penaeid shrimp consists of a succession of moults and metamorphoses. During the first naupliar stages there is only endogenous feeding. After a first metamorphosis, larvae go through 3 zoea stages. In this phase the filter feeding behaviour is predominant and production is based on supply of microalgae. After 5 days at 28°C, they metamorphose into mysis stage for 3 days, finally turning into postlarval stages after a final metamorphosis. Even though the latter stages have predatory behaviour, the mysis and PL stages of shrimp still ingest microalgae. The presence of microalgae improves survival and growth also during the post larval stages 

Algal species used to feed prawn
Algal species used to feed prawn in hatcheries are generally chosen according to size and ability to grow in culture conditions. However, nutritional composition is at least as important to support good growth.

Different species are cultured on site, the most popular being the Chaetoceros, Skeletonema, Thalassiosira, Tetraselmis. They differ in size, in easiness of culture and in nutritional value.

Proviron produces a number of algae that are top choice for shrimp larviculture. ChaetoPrime, ThalaPrime and TetraPrime are our own freeze dried versions of Chaetoceros, Thalassiosira and Tetraselmis. Nannochloropsis (NannoPrime) and Isochrysis (IsoPrime) are more difficult to culture but have an excellent nutritional profile and are therefore promising species for use in prawn larval culture. No need to culture them on site as the freeze dried form is readily available and very easy to use

OV SOFTECH se centra en la fabricación de productos que permiten a la industria optimizar y controlar el ciclo productivo y medioambiental, tales como sistemas de alimentación, sensorica submarina, cámaras, sistemas de estimación de biomasa, sistemas de iluminación y sistemas de software Ad hoc.

OV posee un compromiso con la calidad total en todas las áreas de la empresa, dotando a sus procesos, productos y servicios de una garantía de satisfacción para nuestros clientes.

Valores:
* Máxima calidad en nuestros productos y servicios
* Respeto al medio ambiente
* Oportunidad de desarrollo profesional

Name: Den Levende Fiskerihavn
Description:
Den Levende Fiskerihavn har til formål at revitalisere Rødvig Havn som et centrum for havrelaterede aktiviteter. Ved at ska- be synergier mellem fiskeri, forskning og lokalsamfundet sø- ger projektet at genoplive fiskeriet, forbedre havmiljøet og styrke biodiversiteten.

Hovedaktiviteterne i projektet omfatter etablering af et hav- og forskningscenter på Rødvig Havn, udklækning af fisk samt dyrkning og forskning i søgræs, tang, forskning i skånsomme fiskemetoder og havmiljø samt formidling af havet og dets be- tydning for lokalsamfundet og turister.
Gennem disse aktiviteter sigter projektet mod at skabe et "living lab" for fiskeri og forskning samt tiltrække besøgende til havnen, hvor hele det lokalsamfund og erhvervsliv er involveret.

Name: Fédération de la Marne pour la Pêche et la Protection du Milieu Aquatique
Description:
Fondée en 1933, la Fédération de la Marne pour la Pêche et la Protection du Milieu Aquatique accompagnée de ses 27 associations présentes sur l’ensemble du département encadrent la pratique de la pêche de loisir. Aussi, la Fédération a pour vocation de partager toute la passion qu’incarne cette pratique.
Les adhérents, les bénévoles, et la Fédération constituent une équipe redoutable pour garantir la préservation, la connaissance et la protection des milieux aquatiques. 
Tout au cours de l’année, la Fédération épaulée par les AAPPMA mènent de nombreuses actions sur les 3 855 km de cours d’eau du département afin de lutter contre les menaces pouvant bouleverser ces écosystèmes fragiles.  Ensemble, tous ces acteurs s’expriment au nom des poissons, se battent contre la pollution, œuvrent en faveur d’une gestion respectueuse des ressources en eau, et contribuent à la restauration des milieux aquatiques.
S’engager à défendre les poissons c’est maintenir la passion !

Name: Homarus Inc.
Description: The constant reduction in lobster landings in the Southern Gulf of Saint-Lawrence and Northumberland Strait incited the Maritime Fishermen’s Union (MFU) to put in place Homarus Inc., a non-profit company, which has worked since 2002 at the development of strategies for enhancing the resource as well as the socio-economic viability of the fishery for this general area. Based in Shediac, NB, Homarus has since been working on several research and development axes, such as (1) resource enhancement (lobster stocking program and artificial reefs), (2) lobster resource monitoring programs, (3) ecosystem impacts research (contaminants, climate change, etc.), (4) economic viability (artificial bait, lobster quality) and (5) fishermen and general public education with the Homarus Eco-Centre.

Description:
Le Groupement d’Employeurs – Pêche Durable en Méditerranée est une association basée à Balaruc-les-Bains, dédiée à l’accompagnement des entreprises de pêche et de commerce maritime. 
Notre mission : mutualiser l’emploi, sécuriser les parcours professionnels et accompagner les acteurs du secteur dans un contexte en constante évolution.

Nous apportons à nos membres :
- la mise à disposition de salariés qualifiés,
- un accompagnement juridique, social, RH et comptable,
- un soutien pour répondre aux exigences techniques, administratives et économiques du métier,
- un espace d’échanges et d’informations pour anticiper les évolutions du secteur.

Engagés pour une pêche durable et responsable, nous soutenons les pratiques qui respectent la saisonnalité, la ressource et l’équilibre des écosystèmes méditerranéens. Nous accompagnons l’ensemble de la filière, depuis la pêche et la maintenance des bateaux jusqu’à la valorisation des produits, pour garantir qualité, durabilité et innovation.

Notre action couvre l’ensemble de la façade méditerranéenne, en lien étroit avec les entreprises locales, les institutions et les partenaires du territoire.

 Ensemble, nous construisons une filière plus solide, plus durable et plus innovante.

Name: Scotvax Aquaculture Contractors
Description:
Originating from the Scottish Hebridean Island of Skye, and the once bustling fishing village and ferry port of Kyleakin, George Nisbett, founder and Managing Director of Scotvax, started his aquaculture company in 2007.

With his heritage and background in the fishing and salmon farming industry, having worked at sea on local fishing boats, and for local salmon fish farms, George has been immersed in this industry his whole life.  Aquaculture is George’s passion.  Scotvax was founded through the natural progression of George’s passion and Island life.

With George at the helm, the company developed globally over the years, but still maintains its roots and values on the Island and fish farming ways of life.  Many of the Scotvax employees originate from the Hebridean Islands, all with strong family connections for generations in the fishing and salmon industries.

This environment and background, with our exceptional knowledge, combined with our many years of hands-on global experience, and life-long passion of the industry, has created one of the world’s leading fish vaccinating and aquaculture business specialists.  

Our aim is to help you, with the health and welfare of your fish our main priority, to enhance your business and help exceed the expectations of the best of quality of your fish on the market.

Name: ACCSP: Atlantic Coastal Cooperative Statistics Program
Description:
The Atlantic Coastal Cooperative Statistics Program (ACCSP) was established in 1995 as one of five regional Fisheries Information Networks (FINs) to address the issues of inconsistent temporal and spatial coverage and dataset compatibility by standardizing and centralizing fisheries-dependent data 
collection and data management along the Atlantic coast. FINs are collaborative state-federal programs that supply dependable and timely marine fisheries data in their respective regions. ACCSP is part of the Atlantic States Marine Fisheries Commission and is composed of representatives from marine fisheries management agencies coastwide, including the Commission, the 3 Atlantic coast fishery management councils, the 15 Atlantic states, Potomac River Fisheries Commission (PRFC), D.C. Fisheries and Wildlife Division, NOAA Fisheries, and US Fish and Wildlife 
Service. ACCSP plays a critical role in supporting effective resource management through its efforts to standardize data collection and dissemination across jurisdictions and to efficiently provide Atlantic coast data to the state, regional, and national data systems.

Name: Kystfisker Kompagniet ApS
Description:
Kystfisker Kompagniet is a small, fishermen-owned and operated fish-processing facility supplying high-quality, sustainable products from small-scale, low-impact fisheries in Denmark. Our mission is to ensure fair prices for our fishermen, while supplying top-quality products at competitive prices. 

We believe each fish deserves to be treated with a singular devotion to quality and the respect due to a wild creature, all the way to the consumer. In the same way that our fishermen consistently give individual attention to the fish they catch, our service is tailored to our customers’ needs. At Kystfisker Kompagniet, our people and our focus on quality and service are the difference. 

We work closely with the Danish small-scale fishermen’s association FSK (Foreningen for Skånsomt Kystfiskeri) and the European small-scale fishermen’s network LIFE (Low Impact Fishers of Europe). We believe in quality over quantity, full traceability, locally-sourced fish, a short value-chain and fair prices. 

Minimal by-catch, high quality fish, low fuel consumption, passive gears and supporting local fishing communities are central to the way we think and work at Kystfisker Kompagniet. 

Contact us at +45 26830179 or +45 58351218 or email to: david@kystfiskerkompagniet.dk for information on buying or selling  - or for general inquiries.

Description:
Kompanija Montefish d.o.o. osnovana je 2001. godine u Tivtu. U početku smo se bavili samo ulovom svježe ribe, da bismo tokom vremena proširili svoju djelatnost na proizvodnju, uvoz, izvoz, pakovanje i distribuciju svježe i zamrznute ribe i plodova mora.

Vrhunski kvalitet proizvoda je naš imperativ. Zato se naša uzgajališta nalaze na Verigama, najčistijem dijelu Boke Kotorske, jer je čista morska voda osnovni uslov za kvalitetan uzgoj ribe i školjki.

Stalna želja za usavršavanjem i usvajanjem novih znanja, unaprijeđivanje poslovanja, kao i neprekidno ulaganje u ljudske resurse i tehnologiju garant su da će naši kupci da dobiju svježe, kvalitetne i sigurne proizvode.

Brzina i kvalitet naše usluge potvrđene su HACCP (2010) sertifikatom, kao i brojnim nagradama na prehrambenim sajmovima. Takođe, radimo i na uvođenju ISO 9001 standarda koji će nam omogućiti još kvalitetnije upravljanje poslovnim procesima. To su razlozi zbog kojih možemo sa sigurnošću tvrditi kako uspješno ostvarujemo svoje poslovne ciljeve i predstavljamo vodeću kompaniju u Crnoj Gori u oblasti proizvodnje i trgovine svježom i zamrznutom ribom i plodovima mora.

Name: Plastic Fisher
Description: Plastic Fishing or Plastic Fishery could solve the worldwide plastic waste problem in lakes, rivers, seas and oceans fast and efficient -  it is really necessary to clean up the waters fast as possible because of the microplastic problem. Small and large ships such as boats, container ships and cruise ships could take a lot of plastic along their way, especially as many of them drive along coast with lots of plastic waste. The Greenhouse Ship project introduced this new form of plastic fishing by upgrading new and old boats or ships with plastic fishing nets on the sides. The plastic fishery can become a complete new market and give the plastic waste a better value worldwide, in the end all would win. Many would profit, so like the nature with all their life forms, the poorest who collect plastic waste to survive and a more sustainable fishery and sustainable shipping industry - who really will do something for the climate protection, nature conservation and environmental protection. Plastic fishers and other new jobs could be created with a worldwide plastic fishing market. It is not just a chance, it has really big potential and will create a lot of opportunities for existing companies, branches and industries. #PlasticFisher, #PlasticFishing, #PlasticFishery #SustainableFishing #SustainableShipping

Description:

As we are Having Experience of more than 14 years in the field of seafood Inspection, with this vast experience we evolved to source quality seafood at the best price all over India. We supply seafood to several trading companies in India and around the world.

We commit to the highest quality norms, which meet all international regulatory standards to deliver a quality product. A rigorous quality system made up of well-placed process controls monitored by qualified personnel and supported by in-person quality checks ensures that the final product is free from contamination including any antibiotics and micro-organisms. We also provide beforehand quality check documentation to our customers to ensure them optimum quality products.

Currently, we are exporting seafood such as Vannamei Shrimp | Flower Shrimp | Tiger Shrimp | White Shrimp | Octopus | Squid | CuttleFish | Indian Mackerel | Anchovy Fish | Baigai Top Shell | Blue Crab | Ribbon Fish

OUR INSPECTION STAGES:
During Production (Du-Pro) Inspection: This inspection is conducted, when 25% - 40% of the production is completed.

Initial Production Check (IPC): 5% - 10% of the goods are inspected under IPC

Final Random Inspection (FRI): this detailed inspection is conducted, when the total consignment is packed and ready for dispatch.

Hygiene Inspection (HI): inspection of worker personnel hygiene, building and equipment sanitation, food handling, process inspections, and audits are conducted.

Container Loading Inspection (CLI): Inspection while Loading seafood in the container.

We render efficient and effective services on both Purchasing and Inspection services for your seafood products all over India.

Name: Muirs Tours
Description:
A passionate New Zealander, Deon Muir has spent most of his professional career representing both his country and the mighty Waikato. Captain of the New Zealand Maori All Blacks and Super Captain of the Chiefs, Deon’s heart has always been in the provinces playing over 156 games and scoring over 250 points. Deon has spent most of his time since retiring giving back to the game he loves as director of rugby and coaching local teams.

Before and during all the accolades and international travel, Deon spent his years fishing and diving off the coast of Maketu. Soaking up local knowledge and history helped develop his passion for the outdoors and his desire to respect local traditions while giving back to the community.

When you book with Muirs Tours, this is not just a fishing charter, it is about understanding what it takes to be a kaitiaki of our Moana, improving your fishing skills, and just having a good time.

Name: South African Tuna Association
Description:
The South African Tuna Association (SATA) was formed as a non-profit organisation in 1985 by a group of pole-and-line tuna vessel owners. It is one of the oldest and longest standing South African registered industrial bodies, in terms of the Marine Living Resources Act. Today, the Association represents some 75 local pole-and-line vessel owners and operators.

SATA is committed to, and advocates for sustainable practices, particularly regarding South Atlantic albacore tuna, the fisheries traditional target species. The Association continues to play an active role in the management of tuna fisheries, both locally and abroad, to enhance the benefits of responsible fishing for South African fisheries.

Name: Hawk Lake Lodge
Description:
Hawk Lake Lodge is located in Kenora, Ontario Canada.  Settled on Hawk Lake and 2,600 acres of prime fishing habitat.  10 private cabins overlooking the water with private decks, living rooms and all the amenities of a world class resort.  With 19 private lakes you will fish a different lake each day and be the only boat on the water.  We currently hold 7 catch and release world records including the largest smallmouth bass (8 pounds 10 ounces) and walleye (37 inches).  Trophy fish abound in our waters that have been catch and release for decades.

At night relax on the deck overlooking the water during happy hour, then enjoy a gourmet meal from our cordon blue chef.  Enjoy our hot tubs, sauna, fitness center and throw cards on the poker table or shoot some pool in the billiards room.  With a maximum capacity of just 24 and a staff of 10 our guests are well cared for from the minute you walk through our doors.

The best smallmouth waters in all of Canada, with all the luxury you would expect in a resort closer to home.  Weather you are spinning or fly fishing our waters are a blast to explore.  With 5 different species for which you can fish for trophies (smallmouth, pike, walleye, lake trout and black crappie) you won't want to leave

Name: Deep Blue Aquatic Systems
Description:
Deep Blue Aqua is a water orientated company covering a range of water-related industries. The common thread being the cleaning of water to sustain life within that water. We cover it whether its live lobster caught at sea, abalone in an aquaculture facility, trout eggs in a fish hatchery, penguins in rehab, sharks in a public aquarium or just kids in a school swimming pool or natural swimming pool.

We supply a range of services and equipment into the aquaculture industry in Southern Africa and beyond.

Design and building of customised live holding facilities for the purging of a variety of marine shellfish species such as rock lobster, oysters and abalone. Our systems built-in on-land facilities or sea-going fishing vessels are ideal for preparing stock for live shipping or processing.

Supply and installation of filtration equipment for large scale home or commercial aquariums.

Swimming pools need to be crystal clear whether it’s a school pool, rim flow pool with a view or an eco-pool with a beautiful natural brown tint to the water. Designing and building the filtration that keeps them that way is our forte.

We supply a range of water quality monitoring equipment for use in aquaculture, live-holding systems, aquariums, water treatment works and the mining industry.

Charters at Chad's Coastwide Sports Fishing include your stay in Port Hardy, your meals, an experienced guide and boat and the packaging of your salmon or halibut. With all the details taken care of, fishing with Chad's Coastwide Sports Fishing Charters could easily become the fishing experience of a lifetime.

Name: HydroWave LLC.
Description:
Designed by Anglers, For Anglers. 
The HydroWave is a patented electronic sound device that emits the natural sounds of bait fish and predatory fish feeding on them. Together the sounds produce an instinctive response in nearby predatory fish such as large mouth bass and other predatory fish that can hear the sounds and feel the vibrations of the sound waves in the water. Predatory Fish include Large Mouth Bass, Small Mouth Bass, Sand Bass, Crappie, Stripers, Walleyes, Muskie, Channel Catfish and more. By hearing the sounds and feeling the vibrations the fish are drawn in the direction of the HydroWave more eager to strike bait. This device is intended to give fishermen a considerable advantage in catching a greater number of "keeper" fish.

Name: Kamer Eco Granja
Description:
Kamer es una empresa acuícola que busca innovar con tecnologías emergentes en la acuacultura de Latinoamérica. Contamos con cursos y capacitaciones a empresas acuícolas para innovar sus técnicas de producción, mejorando sus ganancias a través de un aprovechamiento integral de los recursos.

Kamer es un proyecto acuícola que nace en 2014 y ha tenido la oportunidad de desarrollar una producción acuícola de camarón y tilapia empleando la tecnología Biofloc. Desde su creación hemos buscado la forma de adaptar esta nueva tecnología a la realidad del sureste mexicano, participando así en el Programa Estímulos a la Innovación (PEI) de CONACYT en 2016 y 2017, siendo caso de éxito con los proyectos: 
*Desarrollo de una planta piloto de cría y engorde de tilapia y camarón blanco empleando tecnología Biofloc (2016)
*Planta Piloto para recuperación y procesamiento de materia orgánica de estanque de piscicultura para la producción de alimento balanceado (2017)

Name: Oceanbait AS
Description:
Hvem er vi?
Vi er et selskap etablert i 2021 og vi spesialiserer oss i utvikling, produksjon og salg av agn til teiner. Vi er selv ivrige hobbyfiskere og bruker aktivt produktene vi utvikler.

Vår visjon
Vi ønsker å bidra til et enklere, billigere og mer bærekraftig fiske etter krabbe og hummer gjennom våre produkter. Vi er spesielt opptatt av å redusere unødvendig fiske etter teinemat. Enkelte steder i Norge, som i Oslofjorden, er fiskebestanden i knestående. At vi fanger liten fisk for å bruke den som agn i krabbeteiner er med på å gjøre situasjonen verre. Vi håper at våre produkter kan bidra til å hjelpe fiskebestanden opp igjen.

Våre produkter
Våre produkter  er utviklet med tanke på miljø, bærekraft og økonomi.

Når vi utvikler et agn-produkt, er det for å løse et problem. Vår problemstilling har vært: “Hvordan kan vi gjøre fiske mer tilgjengelig for alle, samtidig som vi bidrar til et bedre miljø og beskytter fiskebestanden?”

Vi setter oss hårete mål, men vi liker en real utfordring og er riktig så fornøyd med hva vi har fått til så langt.

Agn til teinefiske er grunnlaget for vår bedrift, men vi jobber stadig med nye produkter.

Name: The Atlantic Groundfish Council
Description:
The Atlantic Groundfish Council represents year-round groundfish harvesters in Atlantic Canada. We are committed to a balanced, sustainable groundfish industry that puts the responsible management of the resource first and foremost. Whether they be based in Lunenburg, NS or Arnold’s Cove, NL, our members believe the future of tomorrow is based on the decisions we make today. We recognize that continued innovation, value positioning and investment requires a stable policy and business environment. This combination of sustainability, value and stability generates economic opportunities and supports rural communities and people living throughout Atlantic Canada.



Since its inception in 1997, the Atlantic Groundfish Council (formerly known as the Groundfish Enterprise Allocation Council), has evolved to be a leader in the fishing industry. We work to improve fisheries of today and tomorrow by contributing to fisheries research and policy development that will improve the sustainability and management of groundfish fisheries while demonstrating our shared successes by supporting sustainability certifications for many of the stocks we fish.

Despite challenges of climate change and ecosystem shifts, we continue to move forward with optimism and confidence. We recognize our industry as an integral part of Canada’s coastal rural economy and we provide sustainably harvested wild fish and seafood to feed the world. We are committed to protecting the future of our marine resources. We believe in a sustainable, stable and prosperous groundfish industry for Canada, and we are committed to doing our part to make that vision a reality.

Nugren also builds, trains and offer consultancy in the eco-friendly Ahotor oven. This eco-friendly oven has the following advantages over the conventional chorkor oven, and the gas fired ovens:

-- It uses less firewood - making it economical and a cost effective.
-- It produces even less smoke - making it very eco-friendly and benefit the operator's safety and health wise.
-- Fish processed with it are safe from Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAH) which are quite dangerous for human consumption.
-- And it's a new technology but based on the old system of smoking fish - making it easy to adopt and use without a steep learning curve.

Description:

Founded in 2003, the company is a comprehensive fishery enterprise integrating ocean fishing, cold chain logistics, aquatic products processing, port services and import and export trade. The company has fixed assets of 2 billion yuan, more than 2000 employees and an annual income of 2.5 billion yuan. Shandong Bodelong Group strives to build the whole industrial chain of the fishery industry, build a strong blue economy enterprise, and provide natural, healthy, safe and high-quality marine products for mankind.

Shandong Bodelong Group has vigorously developed the ocean fishing industry, and owns 67 fishing vessels of all kinds, of which 23 have been registered in the EU. The company has a total of five large pelagic fishing fleets, which carry out production operations in the southwest Atlantic, Indian Ocean, North Pacific, Central and Western Pacific and Southeast Pacific, as well as in the waters of Argentina and West Africa, gradually realizing the international fishery industry layout. The catch of aquatic products includes: Argentine squid, tuna, Peru squid, cod, Antarctic ice fish, ray, eel, and dozens of economic fish such as African hairtail, cuttlefish, Ghana squid, octopus, with an annual fishing capacity of 100,000.00 tons.

Name: High Country Salmon
Description:
High Country Salmon is a boutique, 100% New Zealand family-owned salmon farm, situated 3km south of Twizel.  We grow glacial mountain salmon (chinook or king salmon) in the hydro canal system – this constant flow of fresh, clean water means that our salmon are fitter than sea-run salmon, resulting in firmer flesh and a cleaner, more delicate flavour.  We do everything on-site, resulting in the shortest supply chain possible and the freshest salmon available.

Visitors to High Country Salmon have a range of things to do; have a coffee and some delicious salmon-inspired food from Logans licensed café, feed the salmon (for free) on the floating deck, purchase some salmon to take home from the Fish Shop, or even catch your own salmon!  There is plenty of parking, and the scenery is spectacular! 

Fish Shop: a wonderful range of whole fish, fillets, steaks, portions, smoked and cured salmon. We offer complimentary packaging with ice to keep your salmon in tip-top condition until you get home.  If you would like some salmon delivered to your door throughout New Zealand, just place an order via our website.

Logans Cafe: a mouth-watering selection of cabinet foods including sashimi & sushi or order off our kitchen menu.  Pair your meal with a craft beer or local wine. There is delicious Allpress coffee and loads of outdoor dining areas on the floating deck or the landscaped garden.

Fish Feeding: feed our hungry salmon out on the deck for free (or a donation to the charity of the month).  This is available all day, every day and is great fun for all ages.

Catch-A-Fish: suitable for the whole family, you can catch your own glacial mountain salmon and take it home.  No gear required and no experience necessary.  Filleting available for an extra charge.  Although walk-ins are available, we recommend booking to guarantee your spot.  For more information, to book, or to see our terms & conditions, please visit our website.

Name: Coldwater Lobster Association
Description:
Fishermen Working Together for a Better Future.

The business of fishing and the management of fisheries is constantly evolving. The complexities of balancing environmental policies, regulatory compliance with social, economic, and/or cultural responsibilities has never presented such a challenge.  Lobster fishermen in southwest, Nova Scotia, have witnessed an ever-increasing interest in their waters from offshore exploratory drilling, wind farm proposals, pipeline expansions, marine protected areas,  the protection of endangered species particularly, Right Whales and stock assessments.  It is imperative that fishermen are both privy to the changes/issues that are impacting the lobster industry but that they are also in a position to voice their concerns and provide insight to the decision and policy makers.  Now, more than ever,  fishermen must join forces as one organized collective voice. Coldwater Lobster Association was established in 2014 to address these concerns by employing a forward-thinking approach with an open and honest communication that fosters a climate of trust between fishermen and policy makers.  Establishing an association with a structured membership regime is the best approach to facilitate the emerging needs of fishermen in Lobster Fishing Area 34 (LFA 34) to ensure a safe, sustainable and prosperous lobster fishery for current and future generations.  

Coldwater Lobster Association works with industry stakeholders, government agencies and others for the betterment of the lobster industry as we move into the future. A strong fishing industry will allow potential young fishermen the opportunity to stay in Nova Scotia and will help our rural fishing communities thrive. 

Coldwater Lobster Association is governed by elected Board of Directors comprised of lobster licence holders holding lobster licences in LFA 34 who have a vested interest in the lobster industry and its future.

Name: FAROELANDIA.COM
Description:
The Faroelandia brand is the result of hundreds of years of hard work at sea. This experience acquired over generations enables us to bring the world’s best seafood to discerning customers around the globe. We have developed a wide selection of high quality products ranging from live langoustines delivered to the world’s most famous restaurants to fresh, frozen, dried, salted or smoked seafood products delivered to gourmet dining establishments, the catering industry and selected supermarkets around the world.

Situated in the heart of the Gulf Stream in the North Atlantic, the Faroe Islands have a rich fishing tradition stretching back for hundreds of years. Strong currents and cool waters ensure optimal conditions for cod, haddock, and a variety of shellfish. Using small coastal fishing vessels, FaroeLandia Ltd. carries on this rich tradition today. 

FaroeLandia selects only the highest quality seafood products for their customers. To do this, FaroeLandia has implemented a series of procedures and quality control systems that start the moment the fish is captured and are carried forward throughout the distribution chain. All of FaroeLandia’s fishing trips last only hours. After harvest, their seafood is handled by professionals whom only select the best quality, line-caught seafood for distribution. Selection is based on environmental factors as well as freshness. To further ensure freshness, all products are shipped within 24 hours. Once a product has shipped, FaroeLandia continues to track each box and pallet throughout the shipping process. 

Dedicated to providing environmentally sound seafood, FaroeLandia trains its employees in the many issues impacting food quality, food safety, and environmental integrity.

L’Association Centre Atlantique des Acheteurs des Produits de la Pêche dite l’ACAAPP regroupe actuellement 350 adhérents répartis en cinq collèges ; mareyeurs, poissonniers, grossistes, usiniers, grande distribution, tous premiers acheteurs sur les neuf criées de Royan à la Turballe.
Les fonctions constitutives de l’association sont d’assurer la gestion des transactions financières sous halles à marée :
- garantir le paiement des producteurs, 
- assurer le recouvrement des paiements des achats des adhérents, 
- recouvrer et reverser les frais, taxes et cotisations liés aux ventes, 
- gérer l’ensemble des flux de trésorerie, les finances de l’association, 
- maîtriser le risque acheteur et de gérer les couvertures financières des adhérents.
En sus de cette fonction financière, l’ACAAPP s’implique également dans d’autres problématiques logistiques, sociétales ou éthiques concernant la filière pêche, afin de renforcer un esprit de collaboration et d’interprofession grâce à son rôle prépondérant entre tous les acteurs de la filière.

Kalatalouden Keskusliitto perustettiin Suomen Kalastusyhdistyksen nimellä vuonna 1891. Nykyisellä nimellään järjestö on toiminut vuodesta 1978 lähtien. Järjestön jäseniä ovat 21 maakunnallista kalatalouskeskusta ja kalastajaliittoa sekä kolme muuta jäsentä.

Name: RDB Fishing
Description:
La boutique RDB Fishing est située à Grolley en Suisse et est le fruit d'un passionné de pêche à la mouche et aux leurres des truites, ombres et carnassiers d'eau douce.
La pêche aux leurres et à la mouche nécessite une grande quantité de matériel afin de palier a toute éventualité en action de pêche.
L'acquisition de divers modèles de leurres est très onéreuse. En effet, la plupart de ces articles ont des prix très élevés.
Dans l'optique de pouvoir pratiquer différentes pêches a des prix raisonnables, tout en utilisant du matériel de qualité, RDB Fishing a entrepris des recherches auprès de divers fabricants de matériel de pêche.
Suite à plusieurs années d'investigation, RDB Fishing a obtenu de magnifiques opportunités et a décidé d'en faire profiter également d'autres pêcheurs. C'est ainsi qu'est né RDB Fishing.
Nous cherchons constament a vous proposer des produits et services de qualité à des prix compétitifs. Dans cette optique, nous allons au fil du temps étoffer notre catalogue.
A la demande de nombreux clients, nous proposons également des produits de marques reconnues.
Les prix bas pratiqués par RDB Fishing sont le résultat d'une extrême optimisation des coûts et des frais de fonctionnement.
A titre d'exemple, nous n'avons pas de magasin, pas d'exposition de matériel etc.
Toutefois, le client qui le désire peut, sur rendez-vous, venir choisir ces articles à notre dépôt.
Nous ne faisons pas de publicité ou que le stricte nécessaire.
Nos tarifs sont calculés au plus juste, nous n'augmentons pas nos prix afin de proposer, par exemple, des frais de port gratuits.
Toute l'infrastructure de notre boutique online a été mise sur pied par RDB Fishing.
Nous n'avons pas de personnel, le conditionnement, la préparation des commandes, tout s'effectue en famille.
Tous nos produits sont également satisfaits ou remboursé.
Le propriétaire de RDB Fishing ne pêche plus qu'avec ces propres articles. Il peut ainsi assouvir sa passion à petits prix.

Description:
Oceans Economy was founded on the idea to develop a successful and sustainable aquaculture company in South Africa and to unlock the huge potential that the South African oceans have to offer.

By combining a unique blend of expertise, we at Oceans Economy have begun our adventure by embarking on a journey to cultivate Seaweed and Abalone along the coast of Southern Africa. Name: Barren Island Oysters
Description:
Maryland's best oyster.  Sustainably raised and responsibly harvested in the heart of the Chesapeake Bay.

Barren Island Oysters is using “off-bottom cages,” a new type of aquaculture in Maryland where oysters are placed in cages which sit on the bottom of the bay and are periodically hoisted out of water for hand cleaning, chipping, and sorting before being returned to the water.

Our aquaculture techniques preserve local habitat and protect the spawned eggs of many fish and shellfish species.Our aquaculture cages are submerged in shallow water and out of sight, which maintains the natural beauty of the Bay.

It is our mission to preserve the Bay and produce the most delicious Maryland oyster.

Name: PT. Segara Sumber Kahuripan
Description: PT. Segara Sumber Kahuripan is Indonesia’s leading green caviar seaweed company. Green caviar seaweed (Caulerpa lentillifera) is a genus of seaweed in the green algae group. We provide the highest quality and most nutritious green caviar available. Segara green caviar is rich in protein, fucoidan, calcium, and vitamins. Folklore medicine boasts their anti-aging properties and for that reason, they have been called the “longevity seaweeds”. We grow green caviar seaweed in a clean and controlled environment to maintain the quality.  We aim to provide sustainable cultivated green caviar and introduce this unique seaweed as an alternative food source that is healthy, tasty and environmentally friendly.

Name: Sea Fare Foods Corp
Description:
Miller's SEA FARE Crab Meat.  Unsurpassed flavor, visual appeal and texture - - - a healthy choice, harvested with respect for our natural ecosystems.

For over 30 years, Sea Fare has supplied quality retailers with the premium quality, wild catch crabmeat. In the mid 1970's Sea Fare began working with local fisherman in the Gulf of Siam to produce fresh tasting, shell-free crabmeat. The delicate blue swimming crab had long been a delicacy in Thailand and local fishing families were adept at catching and picking the crab to remove the delicious body and claw meat. However, distribution was limited to the distance the crabmeat could be shipped on ice. Finding a way to can this delicacy without losing its fresh caught flavor was the challenge. Our exclusive packing process meets that challenge to provide US consumers with fresh tasting, shelf stable, shell-free crabmeat.

Name: Mingbo Aquatic
Description: Mingbo Aquatic is a provincial state-owned high-tech enterprise specializing in the development of marine fish, sea cucumbers, and clams, innovation and demonstration of ecological aquaculture models, research and development of modern fishery equipment, and construction of marine ranches. The company develops modern fisheries, smart fisheries, and leisure fisheries, builds new healthy aquaculture models such as "land-sea relay", and creates a full industry chain from parent fish selection and breeding, seedling cultivation, processing to sales and technical services. It is the first in China to develop more than 20 marine fish varieties such as semi-smooth tongue, spotted bream, Yunlong grouper, Golden Tiger hybrid grouper, and yellow-banded grouper. It breeds and promotes 20 million seedlings of grouper, spotted bream, semi-smooth tongue, etc., and annually produces 1,000 tons of high-quality commercial fish such as spotted bream and grouper in ecological aquaculture.

This rare seafood is amazingly delicious and very simple to prepare. Percebes capture the taste of the sea with a flavor evoking fresh clams and lobster. Boiled in briny water (sea water is best!), they are ready to eat in minutes. Simply grasp the shell and peel away the outer skin to expose the flesh - tender and bright orange as coral, with a sweet, rich flavor. Be sure not to spill the juices which are the most flavorful part!

Since its establishment in 2015, the company has built a strong reputation for reliably supplying both global and regional markets, including Ethiopia, Yemen, UAE and Djibouti, as well as reaching customers across East Africa, East Asia, and the GCC countries.

Headquartered in Bosaso, Yustan Seafood has an extensive local presence along Somalia’s coastal regions, supported by a robust cold chain infrastructure in key locations such as Baargaal, Xaafuun, Eyl and Garacad. This network ensures that the seafood products remain fresh and of the highest quality from sea to market.

In addition to its regional operations, Yustan Seafood has recently launched a state-of-the-art HACCP-certified processing plant in Bosaso. This facility adheres to the strictest international standards, allowing the company to expand its reach into new markets, including Europe and North America, while maintaining top-tier product safety and quality.

Name: AquaFinn
Description:
AquaFinn creates safe, biodegradable, environmentally friendly products for aquatic enthusiasts. AquaFinn products are the result of 30 years of experience in the aquaculture industry, both nationally and internationally. AquaFinn was founded by Paul Curtis.

- MinnFinn is a chemically stabilized oxidizer and is similar to those used in the biomedical industry for sanitizing equipment and work areas in a non-toxic and environmentally safe manner.  MinnFinn has an amazing efficacy for the control of mortality and morbidity caused by most all external organisms such as parasites, fungus and bacteria all while being non-toxic to the fish and user and non-damaging to the environment.

- MedFinn is a medicated feed based on Romet-TC. Romet-TC is a proven effective broad spectrum antibiotic used in the commercial fish farming trade, and is one of only three antibiotics approved for use in fish by the FDA. AquaFinn has used their knowledge of commercial fish farming to design a version of Romet-TC for the Koi and goldfish enthusiast that adjusts dose and duration to safely maximize effectiveness. MedFinn combines the safety and effectiveness of Romet-TC, with dosing formulas to help you calculate the proper amount of feed needed based on the weight of fish in your tank or pond, producing a very effective tool to help eliminate internal bacterial infections. 

AquaFinn's parent company, AquaSolver, provides consulting services for projects ranging from full aquaculture facilities anywhere in the world, to local koi ponds and aquariums.

Name: MUGENUNI Pty Ltd
Description:

Our urchins are grown in a 100% land based aquaculture operation with no wild-catch component.  We spawn our own urchin larvae and grow these to mature product using proprietary technology.  

By farming on land, we can artificially control the spawning events and growing environment to supply urchin with plump and  tasty "AAA" roe 52 weeks of the year, avoiding all the sustainability, seasonal quality and supply issues associated with wild-catch urchin.

Name: Hutton Junior Fisheries Biology Program
Description:
The Hutton Junior Fisheries Biology Program is a paid summer internship and mentoring program for high schools juniors and seniors, and is sponsored by the American Fisheries Society (AFS). Selected students known as “Hutton Scholars,” are matched and mentored by a fisheries professional to enjoy a hands-on fisheries science experience in a marine and/or freshwater setting.
Hutton Scholars who successfully complete the program receive a $4,000 scholarship as well as valuable work experience in an exciting and rewarding field.

Visit Our Website: www.hutton.fisheries.org

Our Mission:
- To increase diversity within the Fisheries professions.

Our Vision:
- The principle goal of the Hutton Program is to stimulate interest in careers in Fisheries science and management among groups underrepresented in the fisheries professions, including minorities and women.

The Hutton Junior Fisheries Biology Program Scholars:
- Gain awareness of conservation issues and the importance of healthy aquatic systems
- Participate in habitat protection, restoration and management projects
- Develop an understanding of what being a fisheries biologist involves and of career opportunities in the field

How can you support the Hutton Program? 
- Become a mentor
- Become a host organization
- Donate

Name: Marine Fishers Association Inc.
Description:
The MFA, which was founded in 2007, represents the interests of the majority of South Australia’s 250+ commercial Marine Scalefish Fishery (MSF) licence holders. These fishers are local, based mainly in regional SA, and supply local wild-caught fish to local and interstate markets.

The fishery is SA’s oldest continually running industry and began in 1836 – we are celebrating our 185th anniversary in 2021!

Our members are often 4th or even 6th generation fishers and they and their families form the backbone of many of our coastal, regional communities.

The MFA works with the Government through a co-management arrangement to ensure that our local fish stocks continue to be sustainable and our oceans remain pristine – we take our marine stewardship responsibilities seriously because we rely on healthy fish stocks and a healthy environment for our and our children’s future.

Bedriften drev på den tiden med produksjon av tørrfisk og klippfisk. 

Tidlig på 1960-tallet startet daværende Brødrene Hveding opp produksjon av lutefisk.
Kunnskapene om tørrfisk dannet grunnlaget for tilvirkning av høykvalitets lutefisk. 

Taste Of North er en av de største lutefiskprodusenter i landet, med høy kapasitet både på fersk og frossen lutefisk. Gjennom butikk kjedene er vi landsdekkende.

Taste Of North er en godkjent eksportørbedrift lokalisert i topp moderne produksjonslokaler på Korsnes i Tysfjord, i nær tilknytning til Lofoten. Vi har eget fiskemottak og har en allsidig utstyrspark for å utføre forskjellige produksjoner. Anlegget er utrustet med en effektiv vakumeringslinje, filetlinje, klippfisktørke, spiralfryser og flowpack-anlegg med veiesystemer. 

Vi er kjent for høy kvalitet, hvilket i første rekke skyldes lang erfaring og lange tradisjoner.
I vårt produktspekter inngår, foruten lutefisk i mange forskjellige varianter også røkelaks, klippfisk, snacksprodukter, hundeprodukter og boknafisk.

Vi kan fra 2013 levere lakseprodukter av ypperste kvalitet. Vi kan tilby helfilet, posjonstykker og loins av pre-rigor laks fryst i spiralfrys. Dette medfører fryst laks med lengre holdbarhetstid som kan regnes som fersk ved tining. 

I tillegg produserer vi også andre fiskeprodukter på bestilling fra kunde.

Description:
Benur Top Group is a group of aquaculture companies consisting of PT. Swadaya Mitra Perkasa, PT. Benur Andalan Abadi, and PT. Tri Karta Pratama. The group specialises in the production of Litopenaeus vannamei (Vannamei) and Penaeus monodon (Tiger/Windu) shrimps and their post larvaes.

In the past decades, the post larvae product of the companies was widely known in the industry as 'Swadaya' or 'Benur Swadaya'. Benur Top has only been popular as a brand among shrimp farmers since 2014.

The Group was founded by Mady Tandiah and Leonardo RJ Tiro (Mr. BONG) in 1994. The gentlemen bring with them a combined total of 70 years industry experience in management, design, research, and operation.  

The Indonesian based companies are nationally known for consistently producing high-quality, disease free (SPF), and rapid growth shrimps and post larvaes. 

Benur Top Group has been in the industry for more than 25 years and currently has 10 production facilities across western, central, and eastern part of Indonesia.  

Benur Top has become a trusted brand among shrimp farmers in Aceh, North Sumatera, Riau, South Sumatera, Lampung, Batam, Bangka Belitung, Banten, West Java, Central Java, East Java, Bali, Sumbawa, Lombok, Sulawesi, and Maluku.

Visión: En Atlántico Sostenible, aspiramos a ser líderes en la pesca responsable y la exportación de productos marinos frescos y de calidad premium, promoviendo prácticas sostenibles que protejan y conserven los ecosistemas marinos. Nos vemos como una referencia global en la industria pesquera y lideres en la exportación de pescado y mariscos frescos desde la Argentina hacia UE. Misión: Nuestra misión en Atlántico Sostenible es proporcionar pescado y mariscos frescos, auténticos y de alta calidad, capturados mediante técnicas artesanales y sostenibles. Nos dedicamos a mantener un equilibrio entre la demanda de productos marinos excepcionales y la necesidad de proteger el medio ambiente marino. Trabajamos incansablemente para garantizar que cada cliente disfrute de productos del mar que sean nutritivos, sabrosos y producidos de manera ética. A través de nuestro compromiso con la sostenibilidad y la excelencia, buscamos inspirar un cambio positivo en la industria pesquera y contribuir al bienestar de las comunidades costeras y marinas.

Name: Taku Store
Description:
Each year at Taku Fisheries we purchase over seven million pounds of fish from local Alaskan Fishermen.  Although the bulk of our production flows into domestic and foreign wholesale markets, we are able to select the best quality fresh fish from the catch for our own smokehouse and mail order needs.

We are a bona fide licensed, insured and bonded Alaskan seafood processing company.  At Taku Fisheries we operate under the Federal Food and Drug Administration's HACCP guidelines, the State of Alaska's Environmental Health Department regulations and inspections, as well as our own company quality and sanitation standards - the most stringent of all.

At Taku, we enjoy a two-decade relationship of mutual respect with our local fishermen.  We all love this community built around salmon and share a passion for angling.  Up here, fishing is not just a profession - it's a way of life.  Our close relationship with the men and women on the boats guides the way a fish we catch in the wilds of Alaska arrives at your kitchen in pristine condition.

We control the whole process - from the fish that come to our docks to the fish that are delivered to your door - so you can be assured of our commitment.  Our practices promote environmentally sustainable fishing and handling procedures that provide a maximum health and taste benefit.  Alaska is unquestionably home to the best wild seafood in the world, and we provide you with direct access to the very best.  We are Nature's Fish Store.

Name: Fischerei Köllnitz
Description: An der Groß Schauener Seenkette, mitten im Naturschutzgebiet liegt unsere Fischerei Köllnitz. Seit 800 Jahren werden hier hauptsächlich Zander, Aal, Hecht und Karpfen mit traditionell handwerklichem Geschick gefangen und verarbeitet. Heute ist Köllnitz ein einzigartiges Erlebnis und ein überregional bekanntes Ausflugsziel, an dem Besucher eine reichhaltige Natur mit spannenden Bewohnern beobachten können und die nachhaltige Interaktion von Mensch und Natur im Mittelpunkt steht. Ob beim Netze Flicken oder in unserer hauseigenen Räucherei: Auf dem weitläufigen Gelände bekommen unsere Gäste einen authentischen Eindruck von der Arbeit unserer Fischer. Noch tiefere Einblicke in den Ort und seine Umgebung bietet die Ausstellung der Heinz Sielmann Stiftung. Die lokalen Erzeugnisse sind aber nicht nur zum Anschauen gedacht. Unser Restaurant „Köllnitzer Fischerstuben“ lädt nach einem Tag voller Aktivitäten zu einer großen Vielfalt an Köstlichkeiten und hauseigenen Spezialitäten ein. Wer länger bleiben mag, der findet in unserem urigen Hotel “Köllnitzer Hof” Entspannung und Erholung. Wir freuen uns auf euren Besuch!

Name: BCPA
Description:
BCPA (SARL) est un Bureau d’Études spécialisé dans le domaine de la gestion des Pêches, de l’Aquaculture et de l’Environnement Marin et Côtier. 
Créé en 1994, BCPA a été restructuré en 2020 pour mettre davantage l’accent sur le développement de la petite pêche et se veut être un acteur stratégique dans la promotion de la mise en œuvre des Directives Volontaires visant à assurer la durabilité de la pêche artisanale (FAO) à Madagascar.
La vision du BCPA est de faire en sorte que « la science halieutique soit véritablement au service de la gestion pour un développement du secteur de la pêche de Madagascar ».  
BCPA apporte son soutien « scientifique et stratégique » pour répondre aux questionnements évoqués par le Code de conduite pour une pêche responsable et plus particulièrement aux Directives volontaires visant la durabilité de la pêche artisanale (FAO) : 
(i)- Comment assurer une exploitation durable des ressources halieutiques
et (ii)- Comment contribuer au développement équitable des communautés des petits pêcheurs.

• disease diagnosis and health management for marine and fresh water species, including koi and aquarium fish,
• system design,
• production-quality feed and filtration products,
• finfish husbandry and larval rearing,
• assisted reproduction.

AquaSolver is available when you need help solving an existing problem, designing and building a special project, or sourcing and supplying equipment and feed. BlueTide Aquaculture Limited is combining traditional backyard business based on partnerships and modern technological innovation to restore the culturally iconic Toheroa through aquaculture. Our mission is to see the Toheroa thrive in our waters once more, by driving restoration we aim to provide our own and the world with a true taste of Aotearoa, all whilst tilting the balance of our pristine whenua back in the blue.

Our journey started at the University of Waikato in 2019 and have recently completed Callaghan Innovation's 2019/2020 C-Prize incubator programme as finalists. Through significant support from Callaghan, the Sustainable Seas Fund & Soda Inc, we are working hard to create a brighter future for New Zealand's blue economy.

To ensure our Kaupapa succeeds we have partnered with Te Roroa and the University of Waikato to transform our shared vision into a reality. Our partnerships are the cornerstone of BlueTides organisation, collectively we are excited to share our story and journey with the world.

Name: Nortas Pty Ltd
Description:
Nortas was a leading producer of premium quality seafood before it was sold to Australia's largest salmon producer Tassal in 2003.  Nortas employed over 200 people in several locations.  The company was vertically integrated, controlling the complete production process from hatchery through to marine farms, processing and smoking of salmon products in its own modern facilities and ultimately sales and marketing of the finished product throughout Australia.  Annual turnover was around $30 million at the time of the business sale.

The business was developed by Tasmanian aquaculture pioneer Richard Doedens a former abalone diver who built a rainbow trout farm at National Park in Tasmania.  With the advent of marine farming in Tasmania Richard joined forces with a Norwegian salmon farming company Noraqua and Nortas was born. The first marine farm was based on Bruny Island. 

Nortas operated a hatchery at National Park and marine farms at Bruny Is, Storm Bay (Tasmania's first off-shore marine farm) and Macquarie Harbour growing Atlantic salmon and ocean trout.

Masalah utama yang kami tangani adalah tingginya tingkat kerugian nelayan, di mana lebih dari 41% trip melaut berakhir merugi akibat ketidakpastian hasil tangkapan. Melalui teknologi prediksi berbasis data, Iwak membantu nelayan mengurangi risiko melaut tanpa hasil sekaligus meningkatkan efisiensi bahan bakar, waktu, dan biaya operasional.

Selain memprediksi lokasi ikan, Iwak juga menyajikan data koordinat berisiko tinggi, yaitu area yang dapat menyebabkan kerusakan alat tangkap atau bahaya navigasi, sehingga nelayan dapat menghindari kerugian akibat faktor lingkungan laut.

Inovasi ini tumbuh melalui semangat gotong royong dan kolaborasi komunitas nelayan. Kami percaya bahwa keberlanjutan laut hanya dapat dicapai melalui teknologi yang inklusif dan bermanfaat bagi semua dari nelayan tradisional hingga pembuat kebijakan perikanan.

Name: ECOPECHE Océan Indien
Description:
La pêche artisanale joue un rôle important dans l'économie et la sécurité alimentaire de nombreux pays de l'océan Indien. Cette forme de pêche est pratiquée par des communautés côtières qui utilisent des techniques traditionnelles et des petits bateaux pour capturer des poissons et des fruits de mer. Cependant, la pêche artisanale dans cette région est confrontée à plusieurs défis qui entravent son développement durable.
L'un des principaux défis est la surpêche et la surexploitation des ressources marines, qui peuvent entraîner la diminution des stocks de poissons et la perte de biodiversité marine. Pour relever ce défi, il est important de promouvoir des pratiques de pêche durable, telles que la limitation des captures, la réglementation de la taille des poissons capturés et l'utilisation de méthodes de pêche sélectives.
Un autre défi majeur est le manque de moyens et d'infrastructures pour les pêcheurs artisanaux. Les petits bateaux utilisés pour la pêche artisanale peuvent être fragiles et peu sûrs, ce qui expose les pêcheurs à des risques. Il est donc important d'investir dans des équipements de sécurité, tels que des gilets de sauvetage et des radios, ainsi que dans des infrastructures portuaires appropriées pour faciliter le débarquement et la commercialisation des produits de la pêche.
En outre, les pêcheurs artisans sont souvent confrontés à des défis financiers et de commercialisation. Ils ont souvent du mal à accéder aux marchés de vente en gros et de détail, ce qui limite leurs possibilités de commercialisation et leur revenu. Pour y remédier, il est important de mettre en place des systèmes de commercialisation efficaces, tels que des coopératives de pêcheurs qui peuvent faciliter l'approvisionnement en matériel, la vente des produits et la négociation des prix.
Enfin, il est important de promouvoir l'implication des communautés locales dans la gestion des ressources marines et la prise de décisions en matière de pêche artisanale.

Description:
BIO3000 biomass estimator -	Biomass measurement of farmed salmon.

BIO3000 is a biomass estimator for farmed Atlantic salmon.

The biomass estimator is a tool to give our customers full control of the biomass in their cages at all times.

The system has the capacity to measure a large number of fish in a short period of time, and estimates the average weight very accurately. Name: Agastia Ltd.
Description:
Agastia Farm is a unique ecologically clean and high-tech fish farm based on recirculation aquaculture technology (RAS) in Siberia, Russia (Novosibirsk). The system operates as an indoor closed system using pure artesian spring water source. The fish farm is designed to produce 400 tons of African "marble" catfish (Clarias gariepinus) per year. 

Our significant advantages are the creation of unique conditions for purification and renewal of water in the required volumes, as well as in the organisation of our own production line of quality fish feeds from natural ingredients. This allows us to constantly maintain the optimal and healthy habitat of our fish, thus ensuring the conditions for cultivation of the catfish from the spawning process to fish of commercial size and to receive an ecologically clean delicacy product.

RAS technology makes it possible to produce fish in an environmentally friendly way. The limited consumption of new water, and thereby limited amount of discharge water, makes it possible to clean off the major part of the waste from fish production. With the help of a multi-stage filtration system, which includes ultraviolet water disinfection plants, mechanical filters, bio-filters, oxygenators and pump units, 200 cubic meters of water are cleaned and refreshed every hour (i.e. 4.8 million litres of water per day). The software, in turn, allows for thorough monitoring and optimisation of all production parameters.

Description:
This Institute is an association of individuals and business firms engaged in, or allied to, the consumer seafood supply industry. It is formed for the following purposes:

1. To preserve, foster and promote the consumer seafood supply industry, and related activities and to advocate sound legislation for the improvement, preservation, and promotion of the industry; and

2. To market and promote the consumption of seafood and to provide, enhance and communicate a positive image of seafood products and the seafood industry to consumers; and

3. To protect the right of the public to demand free and continuous access to seafood of all types as a valuable source of food and useful commercial products; and

4. To advocate true conservation through utilization of the maximum sustainable yield of all marine resources; and

5. To encourage efforts to plant and maintain those species of marine life susceptible to scientific propagation.

Description:
Le Laboratoire d'Hydrobiologie et d'Aquaculture (LHA) est une entité de recherche implantée au sein de l'Université d'Abomey-Calavi et plus précisément à la Faculté des Sciences Agronomiques. Il comporte deux unités fonctionnelles de recherches et de formation:
01. L’ Unité d’Analyses Hydrobiologiques et de Démographie (UAHD)
Elle s’occupe des:
Recherches Hydrobiologiques sur le terrain et en laboratoire
Etudes sur la biologie et l’écologie des poissons d’eau douce et saumâtres
Etudes de biologie et d’aménagement des pêches continentales
Etudes d’impact environnemental sur les écosystèmes aquatiques continentaux
Formation (stages et diplômes divers)
L’UAHD dispose d’un laboratoire d’analyses Hydrobiologiques et organise divers échantillonnages et formation sur le terrain
02. Unité de Formation et de Recherches en Aquaculture (UFRA)
Elle s’occupe de :
Recherches sur l’adaptation des espèces locales à l’élevage
Etudes sur la reproduction artificielle des poissons
Etudes sur l’élevage larvaire
Etudes de croissance (larves et juvéniles) en conditions contrôlées
Etudes sur les sous-produits agricoles comme sources d’alimentation pour les espèces aquacoles
Formation (stages et diplômes divers)

L’UFRA est située à la ferme d’application de la FSA de l’Université d’Abomey-Calavi et dispose d’infrastructures et d’équipement modernes permettant aux chercheurs, étudiants de travailler dans des conditions optimales :
Bassins d’expérimentation et d’élevage
Etangs
Ecloserie

Name: Asosiasi Purse Seine Indonesia
Description:
APSI (Association of Purse Seine Industry) established to bridge the gap between stakeholders in the purse seine fishery industry and the government, fostering essential dialogue to harmonize interests, support fishermen, and advocate for practical implementation strategies. As an organization, we aim to empower stakeholders in adopting best practices while ensuring compliance with regulations, contributing to a sustainable and responsible purse seine fishery in Indonesia.

We serve as a platform for discussions among our members, enabling them to provide collective feedback to the government. In doing so, we advocate for policy refinements that benefit all stakeholders and help mitigate operational disruptions. Our approach ensures that the voices of industry players are heard and that the policies introduced are effective and reflective of the industry's needs.

At APSI, we are deeply committed to supporting our members by promoting adherence to reporting obligations and improving data accuracy. To strengthen our fishery's operations, we have integrated the deployment of observers as part of our Fishery Improvement Project (FIP) efforts. This initiative is designed to ensure transparency, enhance data collection, and monitor fishing activities, ultimately improving the overall sustainability of the industry.

Additionally, APSI supports its members by organizing training initiatives to improve the skills and qualifications of fishermen. We offer programs such as BST-F and SKN training to equip them with the necessary credentials to enhance their career prospects and operational efficiency.

Özellikle yurtdışı pazarına sesleniyoruz. Ülkemizde, karides dendiğinde, akla ilk gelen firmalardan biri olmanın gururu yanında ülkemize sağladığımız döviz girdisi ile de gurur duyuyoruz.

Em 1999 expandiu sua área de atuação, incluindo no seu roteiro as cidades de Ayolas e Ita-cora, no Paraguai, Paso de la Patria, na Argentina, e cidades dos estados de Mato Grosso e Amazonas.

Em todo esse período, acumulou experiência e conhecimento para oferecer ao cliente sempre os melhores pontos de pesca, piloteiros treinados, conforto, segurança e, claro, tudo regado a uma culinária maravilhosa, preparada pelo próprio Pedrinho.

Seguindo essa proposta de trabalho, foi fundada em 2005 a Pedrinho Pesca, uma empresa com proposta inovadora, visando, com muito profissionalismo, a satisfação, o conforto, a tranquilidade e a segurança dos amantes da Pesca Esportiva.

Em um só lugar, a empresa reúne a parte comercial, administrativa e uma deliciosa área de confraternização para os pescadores, dotada de churrasqueira, fogão a lenha, fogão e forno industrial, área verde, criando um clima de pescaria antes, durante e depois da viagem.

Renowned for their taste, cleanliness, and nutritional value, our agro-products have earned a strong demand in global markets. To ensure freshness and safety, our products are packed in durable packaging materials, maintaining their quality during transit.

Our Contribution to Sustainability and Health:-
Collagen extraction from fish waste not only reduces environmental contamination but also provides a vital component for the pharmaceutical and healthcare industries. Collagen is a key structural protein in the human body, accounting for 25% to 30% of total body protein and 75% of skin composition.

Fish collagen is rich in essential amino acids like glycine, proline, and hydroxyproline. As the most abundant protein in animal bodies, it finds widespread application in biomedical and pharmaceutical fields. It supports joint health, tendons, skin, hair, nails, cartilage, muscles, wound healing, bedsore prevention, tissue repair, and overall holistic well-being. 

Our Leadership:-
Under the visionary guidance of our founder, Mr. Rajat Mandal, we have established a loyal customer base across the country. Our dedicated customer service team is always ready to assist, ensuring prompt and reliable support for all your needs.

Choose House of Fishes for quality, innovation, and sustainability.

This knowledge means participants gain a more comprehensive understanding of the role they play in the end product. For this reason most of those who do complete our programs receive multiple nationally recognised units of competency.

In order to raise the bar in their own operations, corporations working in the sectors we service require solutions that will assist not only in work being conducted safely and efficiently, but safer projects from design through to occupation and operation by the end user.

As we grow, our aim is to provide a full suite of training and assessment programs that will prepare new and experienced employees with the skills, knowledge and attitudes to work safely.

Name: Kulmer Fisch
Description:
Alles begann mit ein paar Fischteichen und einem selbstgebauten Räucherofen mit einer Kapazität von drei Forellen. Damals, wie heute, wurden die Räucherforellen im Restaurant verkostet und für gut befunden. Für Visionär Franz Kulmer war dieser Erfolg jedoch noch nicht genug und so schaffte er sich einen professionellen Räucherofen an, um Forellen für Airlines und führende Hotelbetriebe produzieren zu können. 

Als Franz Kulmer eine Anfrage für Räucherlachs erhielt, dachte er sich: “Die Welt sei verrückt!”, denn Lachs gäbe es in Österreich doch keinen. Kurze Zeit später importierte er Lachs aus Schweden und exportierte feinsten Räucherlachs in den Norden zurück. Mit der ununterbrochenen Unterstützung von Gattin Anna, geht die Produktion mittlerweile weit über Forellen und Lachs hinaus und Kulmer Fisch ist für Flexibilität und Regionalität im österreichischen Handel bekannt. 

Höchste Reinheitsgebote und hochwertiges Bergwasser sind zwei Grundsteine für den Erfolg von Kulmer Fisch. Mit ASC, MSC und IFS Zertifizierungen ist das Unternehmen an der Spitze der hochqualitativen Fischproduktion. Auch die Weiterführung des 4-Generationen Familienbetriebes ist mit der Mitarbeit von Schwiegersohn Gernot sowie den drei Töchtern Silvia, Sophie und Elisabeth gesichert, damit Konsumenten auch in den nächsten Jahren von hochqualitativen Fischspezialitäten profitieren können.

Name: Red Española de Grupos de Pesca
Description:
La Red Española de Grupos de Pesca (REGP) es una plataforma gestionada y dinamizada por la Dirección General de Ordenación Pesquera y Acuicultura, dependiente del Ministerio de Agricultura, Pesca y Alimentación y designada como Autoridad de Gestión (AG) en el Fondo Europeo Marítimo, de la Pesca y la Acuicultura (FEMPA). 
Se constituyó en el año 2010, en el marco del Fondo Europeo de la Pesca (2007-2013), para facilitar la cooperación y el trabajo en red, dando el protagonismo a las aportaciones de los grupos, AG, organismos intermedios de gestión (OIG) e instituciones públicas y privadas implicadas en consolidar un presente sostenible y un futuro viable para las comunidades pesqueras. 
Durante estos años la REGP ha jugado un papel fundamental como eje central que ha canalizado toda la información relativa al Desarrollo Local Participativo (DLP). Los objetivos primordiales de la red para este periodo son el conocimiento y la difusión del DLP, el intercambio de experiencias y servir de puente de comunicación con otras Redes europeas, principalmente la denominada FAMENET.
En este sentido, las actuaciones de la red se enmarcan en 3 ámbitos:
•	Encuentros.
•	Gestión técnica.
•	Visibilidad.
En la actualidad la REGP está integrada por 11 OIG y 44 Grupos de Acción Local de Pesca, correspondientes a las regiones de: Asturias, Andalucía, Canarias, Cantabria, Cataluña, C. Valenciana, Ceuta, Galicia, Baleares, País Vasco y Murcia.

Name: Agni Nets
Description:
In 1996, Agni Nets was started with the promise to provide and promote superior quality products for our fisherfolk. It was a time when the demand for fishing nets was higher than supply, and we were pioneers in bridging this gap. For over two decades, Agni Nets has been known for the manufacture and supply of high quality monofilament fishing nets. We have many competitors in the region now, and yet the demand for fishing nets, and the preference for Agni Nets, remain high.

With more than 20 years of experience, Agni Nets has diversified into the production of fishing lines, industrial yarn, sports nets and agricultural nets. We’ve done this while retaining the superior quality our brand has become known for. Our products have been appreciated for their tenacity and strength, while also being acknowledged as soft and easy to handle.

To provide the best for our customers in terms of quality and price, we started manufacturing our own monofilament yarn in 2007. Since then, we have expanded operations to manufacture industrial yarns, sports nets and agriculture nets. We continue to innovate with our product lines even today. We purchase our raw materials from Gujarat State Fertilizers & Chemicals Limited (GSFC), which supplies premium quality Nylon-6 and Co-polymers in India. All chemicals used to enhance the quality of our products are imported, and are of premium quality. Our fishing ware, manufactured using Japanese machinery, boast finesse in their finish quality.

Despite the years, our superior quality fishing nets remain our signature products. They are manufactured with emphasis on knot strength, shine and softness. They are made available in single and double knots. To ensure that our fishing gear is suitable for diverse requirements, we manufacture monofilament fishing nets from 0.16 to 0.50 mm, in all sizes and depth measurement. Additionally, we manufacture multi filaments nets ranging from 2 ply to 8 ply.
